title: Finance / Accounting Trainer (Freelance/Part Time)
description: Position Overview: WLP Academy is looking for a skilled and experienced Accounting/Finance Trainer to join our team. The Trainer will play a key role in providing top-notch training, ensuring adult learners achieve effective learning outcomes and assessments. This role demands a strong commitment to upholding assessment integrity while contributing to a positive and engaging learning environment. The ideal candidate will be passionate about teaching and dedicated to promoting a culture of excellence. Training Delivery: •	Participate in curriculum improvements and deliver course content in alignment with WLP’s curriculum and learning standards. •	Facilitate manual and digital assessments to evaluate and track learner performance and progress. •	Delivery of Core Accounting Concepts: Double Entry, Accounting Standards & Accounting Cycle •	Financial Statement Analysis: Guide learners on how to read and interpret the Profit & Loss (Statement of Comprehensive Income) and Balance Sheet (Statement of Financial Position). •	Interpretation of financials: Teach calculation of key financial ratios (Current Ratio, ROI, Gross Margin) to assess business health. •	Data Analytics & Visualization: Guide learners on extracting raw financial data and transforming it into actionable insights. •	Ensure learners meet the specified course learning objectives through engaging and effective teaching methods. •	Conduct engaging, practical training sessions (in-person, virtual, or hybrid). •	Adapt delivery style to suit different audience types (e.g., professionals, entrepreneurs, junior staff). •	Encourage participant interaction, learning, and real-world application. •	Provide practical exercises and simulations in financial statements analysis Assessment Integrity: •	Uphold the highest standards of assessment integrity, ensuring that assessments follow WSQ guidelines and internal policies. •	Ensure that all assessment questions are marked, and learners' answers are accurately evaluated. •	Verify that all questions are answered and attempted by learners. Incomplete responses should not be marked as “pass.” •	Conduct and properly document oral recovery sessions, ensuring transparency and consistency. •	Prevent instances of “flashing” answers and address any behaviors that compromise assessment integrity. •	Ensure incorrect answers are not marked as "pass"; only correct answers aligned with the assessment criteria should be considered. Administrative Responsibilities: •	Complete and submit Assessment Summary Records (ASRs) and other required documentation in a timely manner. •	Maintain accurate records of attendance, assessments, and learner-related data. •	Ensure that all training materials remain up-to-date and relevant in accordance with industry standards. Learner Support and Engagement: •	Provide constructive feedback on assessments and offer guidance for learners to improve. •	Address learner concerns professionally, promoting a supportive and positive learning environment. •	Foster inclusivity and respect in the classroom, ensuring equal learning opportunities for all students. Requirements •	ACLP (Advanced Certificate in Learning and Performance) or ACTA (Advanced Certificate in Training and Assessment) certification is required. •	Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field. •	Professional certification (e.g., CA, ACCA, CPA) is a plus. •	At least 3–5 years of practical accounting experience. •	Strong presentation, facilitation, and interpersonal skills. •	Prior training or teaching experience is highly desirable. •	Ability to simplify complex financial concepts for diverse learners.

title: Valuation Advisory Manager
description: Grant Thornton is a global professional service network of more than 73,000 people in 150 markets. Our team in Singapore is growing rapidly – and we know this success is made possible by our people. That’s why we make sure our people have the right tools and environment to thrive. Each individual at Grant Thornton Singapore is empowered to be themselves within an inclusive team. Our supportive structure gives our people the autonomy to shape their careers and drive positive change the firm. It’s an exciting time to join us as we grow with organisations of all sizes and industries to create change in Singapore, across the region and in the world. The Valuation team Our Valuation team blends technical expertise with a pragmatic outlook to deliver support during the financial reporting process, transactions, restructuring and disputes. The Valuations team creates meaningful value tailored to the needs of each client and is agile and adaptable to market developments. Responsibilities Perform valuations for businesses, intangible assets, financial instruments, etc., for the purpose of financial reporting, transactions, and litigation support Conduct market and industry research, construct and analyse financial models, and ensure best practices and quality in valuation work Support in pitching new projects, including preparation of proposals, engagement letters, marketing materials, and technical documents Perform purchase price allocations for post-transaction financial reporting purposes. Prepare comprehensive valuation reports and review memos which meet the requirements of internal and external clients. Assist in client management, practice development, and business development activities Build and maintain client relationships before, during, and after engagements Mentor and conduct training sessions for team member Qualifications and skills At least 5 years of relevant experience in valuation advisory in a professional services firm Recognised degree in Accounting, Finance, Business or other related fields or equivalent professional qualifications Familiar with Singapore Financial Reporting Standards, IFRS and US GAAP Strong written and verbal communication and presentation skills Good understanding of business valuation concepts and the ability to analyse situations and recommend valuation approaches and next steps Familiarity with key valuation methodologies Strong work ethic and ability to adapt to new challenges in a collaborative team environment Excellent problem-solving skills and ability to prioritise client engagements

title: Manager, Treasury & Governance, Office of Finance
description: COMPANY DESCRIPTION Singapore Management University is a place where high-level professionalism blends together with a healthy informality. The 'family-like' atmosphere among the SMU community fosters a culture where employees work, plan, organise and play together - building a strong collegiality and morale within the university. RESPONSIBILITIES Responsible for financial books of the University's subsidiaries, ensuring compliance with Singapore FRS and any local statutory requirements. Lead financial audits and statutory filings of overseas operations, as required by local regulations. Oversee monthly and annual close process and full financial reporting (all functions) of overseas operations. Keep abreast of new tax rules and guidance, in Singapore and locations where the University operates and assess impact to the University. Perform assessments of complex accounting issues and address queries on accounting and other financial matters. Engage in business partnership with other University departments, provide financial support and business insights. Develop and implement accounting policies, procedures and controls, governance framework for all overseas entities. Provide recommendations for continuous improvement on policies and processes. Design financial analysis to extract key insights from financial data. Prepare key reports, papers and presentations to senior management, the Board, and other stakeholders. Coordinate interactions both within Finance and with other internal and external stakeholders, eg represent SMU in independently liaising with external stakeholders (e.g. auditors, lawyers, bankers) and internal stakeholders (e.g. senior management). Manage/support systems implementation and related IT projects e.g. HRFS project. Lead/support finance related and cross department projects. Prior experience in the government or education sector and/or management of overseas entities would be preferred. Strong technical knowledge in accounting, well versed in FRS and its application. Experience in process flow, governance and compliance matters. Highly proficient in Microsoft Office applications. Strong written and verbal communication and presentation skills. Knowledge and hands-on experience in SAP will be advantageous. Highly motivated individual, resourceful and able to multi-task and work independently. Good analytical and organization skills, able to work flexibly to lead projects and demonstrate critical thinking to problem solve. Effective team lead in a hands-on management capacity. Proactive and mature team player with good interpersonal skills who can communicate effectively with internal and external stakeholders. title: Analyst – Investor Relations & Corporate Development
description: About CapBridge CapBridge, a member of FOMO Group, is a leading digital investment platform headquartered in Singapore. As a Capital Markets Services licensee, CapBridge is regulated by the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) to deal in capital markets products, including securities and collective investment schemes, and to provide custodial services. It is also an exempt financial adviser licensed to issue or promulgate analyses and reports on investment products. CapBridge enables HNWIs and institutional clients to invest in traditional and digital assets via its one-stop digital investment platform, providing highly curated, top-quality, and institutional-grade opportunities to meet clients' diverse asset allocation needs. Through CapBridge's associated company, FOMO Pay Pte Ltd, a regulated MAS Payment Services Act (PSA) Digital Payment Token (DPT) entity, qualified clients can also invest into CapBridge curated products using DPTs, providing a seamless bridge between Traditional Finance (TradFi) and Web3 Finance. About the Role! This role is ideal for individuals who are intellectually curious, commercially minded, and motivated by building—not just advising on—the future of financial services. We are seeking a highly analytical and execution-focused Analyst to support the Investor Relations and Corporate Development function. This role will work closely with the senior Investor Relations lead to support fundraising, investor communications, financial analysis, and strategic initiatives across the group and its operating companies. The role requires strong financial rigor, including hands-on financial modelling, alongside the ability to develop, structure, and storyboard high-quality investor and executive-level presentations. Key Responsibilities Investor Relations & Fundraising Support · Support fundraising initiatives by preparing high-quality investor materials, including pitch decks, 	financial models, and investor updates. · Assist with investor outreach tracking, follow-ups, and pipeline management. · Coordinate and manage data rooms and due diligence materials for fundraising processes. · Conduct market, investor, and peer benchmarking research to support capital-raising efforts. Presentation Development & Storyboarding · Own the development, structuring, and storyboarding of investor presentations in collaboration with senior 	leadership. · Translate complex financial and strategic information into clear, compelling, and investor-ready narratives. · Create executive-level PowerPoint presentations for investors, board meetings, and internal stakeholders. · Ensure consistency of messaging, visuals, and data across all investor-facing materials. Financial Analysis & Modeling · Build and maintain detailed financial models, including forecasts, scenario analyses, and valuation models. · Analyse group and operating company performance to support investor reporting and strategic decisions. · Prepare sensitivity analyses and commercial assessments to support fundraising and strategic initiatives. Corporate Development & M&A Support · Support the evaluation of acquisition opportunities through financial analysis, market mapping, and 	benchmarking. · Assist in preparing investment memoranda and internal decision-making materials. · Support transaction execution processes in coordination with internal teams and external advisors. Reporting & Stakeholder Support · Support quarterly and ad hoc investor reporting, including performance summaries and KPI 	dashboards. · Coordinate inputs across finance, legal, and operating teams to ensure accuracy and alignment of 	investor materials. · Maintain a high standard of quality, accuracy, and professionalism across all communications. Qualifications & Experience · Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Business, Economics, or a related discipline. · Minimum of 3 years’ experience in investment banking or a Big Four audit firm, with exposure to M&A 	advisory, transaction services, or corporate finance. · Proven ability to develop investor-ready presentations, including structuring and storyboarding 	complex financial and strategic narratives. · Strong financial modeling, valuation, and analytical capabilities. · Advanced proficiency in Excel and PowerPoint (or equivalent tools). · Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with strong attention to detail and visual clarity. · Ability to perform effectively under tight deadlines and manage multiple workstreams concurrently. · Comfortable working with senior executives, investors, and external advisors. · Highly proactive, hands-on, and execution-driven. · Fluent in both English and mandarin to manage Chinese stakeholders. Our Perks and Benefits: · Competitive Compensation and Benefit Package · Group Hospitalisation and Medical Insurance · Fun and Dynamic Environment with plenty of learning and growth opportunities · Career Advancement · Professional Development Training Workshops · Team Building Sessions

title: Senior Accountant
description: Senior Accountant / Accountant (Retail & E-commerce | Singapore) Our client is a growing retail and e-commerce company with an established presence across physical stores, online platforms, and department store channels. As part of its continued expansion, the company is seeking a hands-on and experienced Senior Accountant / Accountant to oversee the full spectrum of finance operations in Singapore. This is a critical role that will work closely with business stakeholders and play an integral part in financial management, cashflow planning, and business performance. This is an excellent opportunity for a motivated finance professional to take ownership of the finance function and contribute to business growth in a dynamic retail and supply chain environment. Key Responsibilities: • Lead and manage the full spectrum of accounting and finance operations for the Singapore business. • Oversee cashflow planning and cash management to ensure smooth operational funding. • Handle GST reporting, IRAS compliance, and corporate tax filings in accordance with local regulations. • Assist in the budgeting and forecasting processes to support strategic and operational planning. • Prepare financial reports, analysis, and insights to support management decision-making. • Manage relationships with banks, including banking facilities, financing arrangements, and all company bank accounts. • Develop, standardize, and strengthen finance policies, processes, and internal controls. • Supervise 1–2 direct reports within the finance team. • Partner with internal stakeholders across retail operations, supply chain, and e-commerce functions. • Support financial matters related to physical retail stores, online sales channels, and department store partnerships. Qualifications & Experience: • Bachelor’s degree in accounting, Finance, or a related field; professional certifications (ACCA/CPA/CA) are an advantage. • Strong experience in full-spectrum accounting and finance operations in a commercial environment. • Solid hands-on knowledge of cashflow management, banking matters, and financial controls. • Confident stakeholder manager, able to work closely with operational and business teams. • Hands-on, proactive, and adaptable, with the ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment.

Commercial deployment • Ensure effective utilization of commercial resources to strengthen business performance oversight. • Define channel & customer segmentation and determine priorities • Develop and support channel / account strategy • Support commercial cycle objectives framing and deployment Who we’re looking for • Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent experience holder, preferably in Finance, Economics, or Business Administration. • Minimum of 3 years of related experiences • Strong business acumen and budgeting & forecasting skills. • Proficient in financial modeling, analytical, and problem-solving skills. • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to effectively collaborate with diverse teams. • Proactive, results-oriented, and able to work independently as well as part of a team.

title: Manager, CRM & Marketing Efficiency
description: COMPANY DESCRIPTION Kaplan Higher Education Academy (Kaplan) is part of Kaplan Inc., one of the world's most diverse education providers and the largest subsidiary of Graham Holdings Company (NYSE:GHC). Kaplan has welcomed students from over 55 countries and regions. As a Lifelong Integrated Learning Partner, we offer over 450 academic and professional certification programme options* for higher learning and skills development, providing opportunities for individuals to always stay relevant. To date, these opportunities have benefitted more than 100,000 graduates^. We have a diverse team of individuals dedicated to our mission to help individuals achieve their educational and career goals, and build futures, one success story at a time. Our core values - Opportunity, Knowledge, Support, Integrity and Results - help guide the way we work. At Kaplan, our employees are our most valuable assets, and everyone's contribution is integral to our success. We have been recognised by The Straits Times, for the second successive year, as being one of Singapore's Best Employers for 2023 . This large-scale and comprehensive study assessed the attractiveness of organisations in Singapore based on an independent employee survey. We strive to continue providing a professional, positive and stimulating work enviroment with plenty of room for initiatives, so that our employees can thrive with us.  If you are seeking opportunities for growth, we welcome you to write to us! *Based on full-time and part-time study modes across programmes offered at Kaplan. ^Total Diploma and Degree graduates from Kaplan from 2007 to 2023. RESPONSIBILITIES THE ROLE: Manager, CRM & Marketing Efficiency The Manager, CRM & Marketing Efficiency is responsible for building a high-performing CRM ecosystem that supports accurate data capture, standardised processes, effective qualification, and clear forecasting. This role also leads the optimisation of contact centre operations, including agent performance, automation, and AI-driven engagement. The manager works closely with marketing, recruitment, operations, and technology stakeholders to drive efficiency and revenue growth. Key Responsibilities: CRM Optimisation Develop and enforce CRM best practices across marketing and recruitment teams to ensure consistent, high-quality data capture. Design and maintain standardised workflows that support accurate segmentation, reporting, and lifecycle tracking. Lead ongoing CRM improvements, including field structures, validation rules, automation logic, and reporting frameworks. Monitor CRM data health and implement corrective actions to improve completeness, accuracy, and usability. Support forecasting and performance analysis through reliable CRM outputs and dashboards. Contact Centre and Qualification Matrix Optimisation Oversee daily operations of contact centre agents, including performance management, training, scheduling, and process improvements. Implement automation and digital tools to reduce manual workload and improve lead response times. Develop and scale AI-powered contact centre capabilities to support qualification, nurturing, and re-engagement. Redesign and maintain a clear qualification matrix and disqualification logic aligned with customer lifecycle stages. Drive measurable improvement in lead-to-application and lead-to-enrolment conversion. Stakeholder Management Collaborate with marketing, recruitment, operations, technology, and compliance teams to develop CRM and contact centre enhancements. Facilitate alignment between departments on lead management workflows, qualification standards, and data requirements. Communicate project plans, progress, and outcomes to senior leadership and cross-functional stakeholders. Translate business needs into actionable CRM and operational requirements for technical teams. Marketing and Sales Pipeline Efficiency Analyse pipeline performance from lead to enrolment and identify friction points across channels and stages. Partner with marketing to improve lead quality, scoring, and routing logic. Partner with recruitment to improve follow-up discipline, pipeline hygiene, and forecasting accuracy. Identify opportunities to reduce processing delays, eliminate redundant steps, and optimise contact strategies. Develop dashboards and operational frameworks that support predictable, scalable growth. Quality assurance and feedback Establish QA procedures for CRM usage, contact centre interactions, and lead-handling practices. Conduct regular audits on data quality, qualification consistency, and process adherence. Provide structured feedback and training to teams to improve CRM discipline and customer experience. Monitor feedback loops from agents, applicants, and stakeholders to refine processes and tools. Risk Management and Compliance Ensure CRM processes and contact centre operations comply with internal governance, data privacy policies, and regulatory requirements. Implement controls to reduce risk linked to inaccurate data, improper follow-up, or non-compliant communication practices. Maintain documentation for workflows, qualification criteria, disqualification reasons, and system changes. Support audits and work with legal/privacy teams on risk mitigation and data-handling best practices. Support any other tasks as assigned by Supervisor or Head of Department Assist in ad hoc projects that align with the institution's broader marketing and recruitment objectives. Respond promptly to any last-minute content needs or adjustments, ensuring flexibility and responsiveness to meet evolving marketing goals. QUALIFICATIONS Essential Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Business, Analytics, Information Systems, or related field. 5+ years of experience in CRM management, marketing operations, sales operations, or contact centre optimisation. Demonstrated experience owning or implementing CRM workflows, data governance, and pipeline processes. Proven track record of improving lead quality, pipeline conversion, or operational efficiency. Experience working with cross-functional teams across marketing, sales/recruitment, analytics, and technology. Experience managing or improving contact centre operations and agent performance. Familiarity with data privacy requirements related to CRM and customer communication. Strong CRM administration and optimisation ability (Salesforce, HubSpot, MS Dynamics, or equivalent). Confident with data analysis, dashboards, forecasting, and performance reporting. Ability to design workflows, diagnose bottlenecks, and implement scalable processes. Strong organisational skills, able to manage multiple projects and stakeholders. Clear communication skills, able to translate business needs into system requirements. Ability to lead operational improvements, set standards, and enforce process discipline. Strong problem-solving mindset and comfort with building new systems from scratch. English - spoken and written as required to fulfill daily tasks Microsoft Office Desirable Experience scaling AI-driven contact centre operations. Experience in education, training, or service-based industries. Background working with compliance, legal, or governance teams on system-related risk mitigation. Experience with automation tools, lead-scoring models, or customer lifecycle frameworks. Ability to create QA frameworks, SOPs, and training modules for CRM or contact centre teams. Strong change-management capability for driving adoption of new processes or systems. Ability to present operational insights and recommendations to senior leadership with clarity and confidence. title: AI Finance / Accounting Trainer (Freelance/Part Time)
description: Position Overview: WLP Academy is looking for a skilled and experienced Accounting/Finance Trainer to join our team. The Trainer will play a key role in providing top-notch training, ensuring adult learners achieve effective learning outcomes and assessments. This role demands a strong commitment to upholding assessment integrity while contributing to a positive and engaging learning environment. The ideal candidate will be passionate about teaching and dedicated to promoting a culture of excellence. Training Delivery: •	Participate in curriculum improvements and deliver course content in alignment with WLP’s curriculum and learning standards. •	Design and develop AI training content, including both core/beginner and advanced levels. •	Prompt Engineering for Finance: Teach learners how to construct precise prompts to generate financial summaries, draft overdue payment emails, and interpret P&L variances using LLMs (Large Language Models). •	Excel & AI Integration: Guide learners on using AI features within spreadsheets (e.g., Copilot for Excel) to clean data and generate formulas automatically. •	Facilitate manual and digital assessments to evaluate and track learner performance and progress. •	Ensure learners meet the specified course learning objectives through engaging and effective teaching methods. •	Conduct engaging, practical training sessions (in-person)making complex AI tools accessible and relevant to learners. •	Adapt delivery style to suit different audience types (e.g., professionals, entrepreneurs, junior staff). •	Encourage participant interaction, learning, and real-world application. •	Provide practical exercises and simulations in financial statements analysis Assessment Integrity: •	Uphold the highest standards of assessment integrity, ensuring that assessments follow WSQ guidelines and internal policies. •	Ensure that all assessment questions are marked, and learners' answers are accurately evaluated. •	Verify that all questions are answered and attempted by learners. Incomplete responses should not be marked as “pass.” •	Conduct and properly document oral recovery sessions, ensuring transparency and consistency. •	Prevent instances of “flashing” answers and address any behaviors that compromise assessment integrity. •	Ensure incorrect answers are not marked as "pass"; only correct answers aligned with the assessment criteria should be considered. Administrative Responsibilities: •	Complete and submit Assessment Summary Records (ASRs) and other required documentation in a timely manner. •	Maintain accurate records of attendance, assessments, and learner-related data. •	Ensure that all training materials remain up-to-date and relevant in accordance with industry standards. Learner Support and Engagement: •	Provide constructive feedback on assessments and offer guidance for learners to improve. •	Address learner concerns professionally, promoting a supportive and positive learning environment. •	Foster inclusivity and respect in the classroom, ensuring equal learning opportunities for all students. Requirements •	ACLP (Advanced Certificate in Learning and Performance) or ACTA (Advanced Certificate in Training and Assessment) certification is required. •	Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field. •	Prior experience teaching (preferably 3 years) or training adult learners in AI / digital technology. •	Professional certification (e.g., CA, ACCA, CPA) is a plus. •	At least 3–5 years of practical accounting experience. •	Strong presentation, facilitation, and interpersonal skills. •	Prior training or teaching experience is highly desirable. •	Ability to simplify complex financial concepts for diverse learners.

Event Review and Learning Support Support the implementation of a club event evaluation process to gather feedback and learning points from student-led activities Help student leaders reflect on event outcomes and identify opportunities for improvement Share insights and good practices across clubs to strengthen collective learning and capability building 6. Process Support and Continuous Improvement Provide input on improving student council and club-related processes to enhance coordination, clarity, and ease of execution Support the development and maintenance of simple tools, templates, and guides that help student leaders plan and run events more effectively Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives aimed at strengthening student-led engagement activities MBA Commencement Management and Coordination Role Purpose: Serve as the key liaison for the MBA Programs Office in the planning, coordination, and execution of the MBA Commencement Ceremony, working closely with BIZ Corporate Communications and the Office of University Registrar to ensure a smooth and meaningful ceremony experience for graduates. Key Responsibilities: Coordinate with BIZ Corporate Communications and the Office of University Registrar on planning requirements, timelines, and ceremony logistics Liaise with the Deanery, Academic Directors to form the Academic Procession and stage party, including on appointment of readers Recruit and coordinate a team of Staff Representatives to support ceremony operations Support communication to graduates on ceremony arrangements, where required Ensure operational readiness including briefing of staff, coordination of duties, and contingency planning Be the Point of Contact to nominated valedictorians to communication on ceremony preparation such as valedictorian speeches and ceremony rehearsal Procurement and Operational Logistics Support Role Purpose: Ensure timely, compliant and efficient procurement and logistics processes supporting student initiatives and experiential learning programmes. Key Responsibilities: Manage procurement processes in line with university guidelines Support purchasing, vendor sourcing, quotations, invoicing and documentation Provide logistical support for events and programmes Coordinate inventory management of materials and supplies Maintain accurate procurement records and ensure compliance Any other ad hoc responsibilities assigned by the Team Lead. Qualifications At least a Bachelor’s Degree from a recognized University At least 5 years of relevant experience in student life, customer service or event management Able to work independently, pro-active, task-oriented, yet service-minded A fast learner, willingness to learn, meticulous with good problem-solving skills and ability to multi-task A team-player, with willingness to support inter-team/office activities Ability to develop good rapport with students, as well as university and corporate partners Strong interpersonal, verbal and written communication skills Comfortable in communicating with senior management/faculty, cross department colleagues and diverse culture (experienced) students Service oriented individual Comfortable navigating with Social Media channels like Facebook, Instagram and LinkedIn Experience in using Microsoft Office such as Word, Excel, PowerPoint and Outlook and other educational IT System Experience in working in education industry will be a plus but not necessary (Appointment job grade will commensurate with the selected candidate's experience)

title: Senior Executive, Accounts
description: Our Finance Team ensures the overall financial health of the organization, and supports the development of the organization's budgeting, financial planning and long-term financial goals. The  Senior Executive, Accounts plays a critical role in maintaining financial integrity and supporting effective financial management for RC. The Senior Executive, Accounts is responsible for ensuring accurate financial reporting, compliance with statutory and regulatory requirements, and the implementation of strong internal controls. The Senior Executive, Accounts will work closely with school leaders, programme and unit leaders to support budgeting, forecasting, and financial analysis for sound decision-making aligned to the RC’s strategic goals. Key Tasks and Responsibilities Financial Accounting & Reporting Accounting Operations: Perform General Ledger function for assigned programmes and units, along with supporting tasks which may include Accounts Payable and Accounts Receivable; Cash and Fixed Asset Management; and Ad-hoc tasks as and when required Financial Reporting & Month-End Closing: Prepare accurate and timely monthly financial closing, monthly balance sheet schedules and reconciliations, monthly cash flow forecasts and annual statutory financial statements Subledger Reconciliation & Resolution: Reconcile subledger transactions with the Financial Accounting System, perform investigation on discrepancies (if any), and resolve discrepancies to ensure accurate and reliable reporting Banking Transactions Management: Record all banking transactions timely and accurately Management Reporting Support: Support Lead Manager, Finance Operations to prepare monthly management reports Budgeting, Financial Planning & Analysis Budget Preparation & Financial Forecasting: Assist in the preparation and consolidation of the annual budget and regular financial forecasts for assigned units Variance Analysis & Financial Planning Support: Support Lead Manager, Finance Operations in conducting monthly budget and forecast variance analysis to provide insights for financial planning and decision-making Cost Analysis & Strategic Insights: Generate cost analysis reports to support management in strategic financial decision-making Compliance & Internal Controls Regulatory & Compliance Monitoring: Stay updated with the financial aspect of funding requirements and ensure compliance with tax, legal and regulatory requirements Petty Cash Administration: Administer petty cash, including claim verification, processing, and reconciliation Documentation & Approvals: Review RC’s monthly financial transactions for proper documentation, accurate account assignment, and timely approvals Internal Controls Monitoring: Monitor the effectiveness of internal controls, promptly escalating risks or irregularities to Lead Manager, Financial Operations Audit Follow-Up & Implementation: Take follow-up actions on audit findings and recommendations, and ensure timely implementation of recommendations Asset Reconciliation: Safeguard RC’s assets (cash, inventory and equipment) through appropriate internal control procedures and periodic reconciliations Collaboration & Process Improvement Process Improvement & Efficiency: Work with other Finance Operations team members to streamline processes and improve efficiency Financial Systems & Controls Enhancement: Participate in financial system or workflow improvements, including documentation of procedures and sharing of best practices Job Holder Requirements Diploma or Degree in Accountancy / Finance with relevant professional accounting certification (ACCA) Minimum 2 years of relevant working experience Experience in budgeting, forecasting, and variance analysis, will be an advantage Prior working experience in Social Services or Special Education sector will be an advantage.

title: Corporate Finance Associate / Senior Associate
description: The Team Our Corporate Finance team is focused on assisting diverse client base with their M&A and capital raising requirements. The role provides opportunity to work in a dynamic environment and collaborate with other Grant Thornton advisory teams across Singapore and region. Responsibilities Assist in performing detailed financial analysis and modeling for M&A and capital raising transactions. Conduct company valuations using various methods, including discounted cash flow (DCF), comparable company analysis, and precedent transactions. Prepare pitch decks, proposals, presentations, and investment memorandums for internal and external stakeholders. Support due diligence processes by gathering, analysing, and interpreting financial data Conduct company, industry, market and valuation research Assist leaders in executing corporate finance transactions, including coordinating with clients, investors and other stakeholders. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or a related field For Associate, at least 1 year of M&A and/or capital raising deal experience in corporate finance, investment banking, or financial consulting. For Senior Associate, at least 2 – 3 years of experiences in M&A and/or capital raising deal experience in corporate finance, investment banking, or financial consulting Strong financial modeling skills and proficiency with valuation techniques Excellent analytical, problem-solving, communication and presentation skills, with the ability to convey financial concepts to diverse stakeholders Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint, and financial analysis tools Ability to work well in a team-oriented, fast-paced environment Detail-oriented with strong organisational skills

title: Business Analyst (Contract/Banking)
description: Application and Data Management: Collating and managing detailed information about applications within your assigned business vertical, including system interfaces, data content, and data storage. Testing Strategy and Execution: Assessing and proposing appropriate testing approaches and sign-off criteria, and actively performing User Acceptance Testing (UAT). Able to create Test Cases and perform the Testing using SQL scripting and excel is mandatory. Documentation: Creating comprehensive documentation for data flow, design, business solutioning, and testing approaches, adhering to predefined templates. title: Assistant Manager (Branding, Marketing & Admissions for EMBA-Chinese Programmes), MBA Programmes
description:
Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUS
Career Portal Your application will be processed only if you apply
via NUS
Career Portal We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Job Description: About NUS Business School Founded in the same year that Singapore gained independence, NUS Business School stands today among the world’s leading business schools. It is distinctive for offering the best of global business knowledge with deep Asian insights, preparing students to lead Asian businesses to international success and to help global businesses succeed in Asia. For more than 50 years, NUS Business School has offered a rigorous, relevant and rewarding business education to outstanding students from across the world. That would not have been possible without a group of dedicated professionals who are passionate about building a positive environment to grow and groom future business leaders. And we are looking to add to our BIZ family! To learn more about the NUS Business School, please visit https://bschool.nus.edu.sg/ Job Description 1. Branding and Communication Support brand positioning strategy to enhance the global visibility of NUS EMBA Chinese programme. Maintain brand tone consistency across content, communication and visual assets. Assist in developing creative narratives for campaigns, programme highlights and value propositions. 2. Content Marketing and Social Media Management Manage official Chinese social media platforms (WeChat, LinkedIn, Xiaohongshu, video channels) and update content daily/weekly. Excellent communication and coordination skills, with an organised, proactive, reliable, and responsive working style. title: Business Development Executive / Senior Business Development Executive
description: About OA Group of Companies (OA GOC) OA Group of Companies (OA GOC) is a regional professional services group headquartered in Singapore, with an expanding presence across Malaysia and the region. We partner with growth-minded business owners, CFOs, and leadership teams to build strong corporate foundations, enhance governance and compliance, and enable sustainable growth. Our multidisciplinary platform spans Audit & Assurance, Accounting & Outsourcing, Corporate Secretarial & Compliance, Tax Compliance & Advisory, Business Advisory, and Digital Transformation . By integrating compliance, financial insight, and advisory thinking, we help clients move from operational stability to strategic scale. At OA GOC, we believe that strong growth is built on professionalism, ownership, and long-term partnerships — both with our clients and our people. Why This Role Exists As OA GOC enters its next phase of growth, we are intentionally building a high-calibre Business Development team to drive long-term client partnerships and group-wide revenue expansion. This is not a transactional sales role. The Business Development function at OA GOC exists to work alongside business owners and finance leaders, understand their challenges early, and shape integrated solutions across the OA GOC platform. The role plays a direct and visible part in the firm’s growth trajectory. Role Overview We are hiring Business Development Executives and Senior Business Development Executives to support and accelerate OA GOC’s growth. This role is suitable for individuals who are commercially driven, disciplined, and motivated by measurable outcomes. You will be responsible for originating opportunities, building trusted relationships, and converting prospects into long-term clients. You will operate in a professional services environment where credibility, clarity, and relationship quality matter. Key Responsibilities Identify, qualify, and convert business opportunities across SMEs and mid-market clients Build trusted relationships with founders, CFOs, finance leaders, and senior executives Drive outreach through targeted campaigns, networking, events, referrals, and partnerships Prepare and present structured proposals and presentations aligned to client needs Own and manage your sales pipeline with discipline, forecasting accuracy, and accountability Work closely with internal delivery teams to shape solutions and support client success What Success Looks Like in This Role Building a consistent and high-quality opportunity pipeline within the first 3–6 months Developing repeat and referral relationships with clients Demonstrating strong conversion discipline and commercial judgement Progressing from opportunity execution to owning key accounts, sectors, or growth initiatives Making a visible contribution to group-level revenue growth Who We Are Looking For We welcome candidates from different professional backgrounds, particularly individuals who are: Former accountants, auditors, tax agents, or corporate secretarial professionals seeking a transition into business development Business development professionals looking for a more strategic, professional services-oriented role The ideal candidate will demonstrate: Strong drive, ownership mentality, and coachability Professional communication and relationship-building skills Confidence engaging with senior stakeholders Comfort being measured by outcomes and results Discipline in pipeline management and follow-through For Professionals Transitioning from Accounting, Audit, Tax or Corporate Secretarial This role is well-suited for professionals who understand financial, compliance, and governance frameworks and want to apply that knowledge commercially. OA GOC provides structured support to help you develop sales and client-engagement skills while retaining the professionalism and credibility you have built in your technical career. You will not be expected to “hard sell”, but to position value, ask the right questions, and build trust with clients. Career Development High performers in this role can progress into senior business development leadership, key account ownership, sector-focused growth roles, or group-level commercial functions . Advancement is merit-based and tied to performance, capability, and leadership potential. What We Offer Clear and structured career progression Mentorship and ongoing professional development Competitive base salary with uncapped incentive compensation A professional, performance-focused team environment A meaningful role in shaping the growth of OA GOC Why Join OA GOC This is an opportunity to build a long-term career in business development within a respected professional services group that values professionalism, ambition, and impact. This role is suited for individuals who take ownership of outcomes, are comfortable with accountability, and want to grow alongside the firm.

title: Manager – Corporate Outreach & Executive Education
description: Office Location: 10 Hyderabad Rd, HortPark, Singapore 119579 Reporting to: Head of Executive Education / CEO Key Responsibilities: Identify, engage, and convert corporate clients for executive education programs (customized and open enrolment) Manage end-to-end sales cycle: lead generation, pitching, proposal development, follow-up, and closure Build long-term relationships with HR, L&D, and senior leadership across industries Collaborate with academic and program teams to design relevant learning solutions Represent the institution at business events, forums, and conferences Maintain accurate and timely sales reporting in CRM system Key Performance Indicators (KPIs): Sales Targets: Achievement of quarterly and annual sales targets Lead Generation: Minimum number of qualified B2B leads/prospects Client Engagements: meaningful meetings or presentations conducted with decision-makers Client Relations: Maintain relations with existing clients and new ones Requirements: Bachelor’s degree 2 to 3 years of relevant B2B sales experience, preferably in executive education, corporate training, or consulting Excellent communication, presentation, and client management skills Strong network within the HR/L&D ecosystem is a plus Goal-driven, proactive, and comfortable in a target-based environment

title: Financial Accountant
description:
Lico Resources is partnering with a reputable financial institution known for its stability, disciplined operations, and long-standing presence in the industry. With a strong track record of compliance and financial stewardship, the organization offers a collaborative environment that values continuous learning and professional growth. This is an excellent opportunity for a finance professional with a strong foundation in external audit or financial accounting to transition into a commercial accounting role within the financial services industry. Based in Singapore, the role is ideal for individuals looking to build a long-term career in finance with exposure to a regulated and fast-paced environment. Key Responsibilities: Prepare timely and accurate monthly and quarterly financial reports in compliance with internal and regulatory requirements Perform general ledger accounting, including journal entries, accruals, and account reconciliations Support month-end and year-end closing activities across various business units Assist with external and internal audits by preparing required schedules and documentation Ensure adherence to accounting standards (e.g. IFRS) and internal financial      controls Participate in the preparation of budgets and forecasts, supporting variance analysis as required Maintain up-to-date documentation of accounting processes and policies Collaborate with internal stakeholders to resolve accounting queries and improve      financial processes Contribute to initiatives related to finance transformation, automation, and system enhancements Qualifications: Must-Have: Degree in Accounting, Finance or a related field 3 to 5 years of relevant experience, preferably from a Big 4 external audit firm or in a financial institution Solid understanding of accounting principles and financial reporting standards (e.g. IFRS) Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and accounting systems such as SAP, Oracle, or equivalent Strong attention to detail, good analytical skills, and a hands-on approach to problem solving Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work both independently and in a team setting Nice-to-Have: Professional certifications such as CPA, ACCA, or equivalent (completed or in progress) Familiarity with MAS reporting and regulatory requirements Exposure to the financial services sector or regulated environments Experience participating in process improvement or automation projects If you are interested in this role, please send us your updated resume today to nicole@licoresources.com quoting

title: Wealth Strategist
description: Responsibilities: 1. Portfolio Analysis: - Conduct in-depth analysis of investment portfolios and risk factors. - Utilize financial techniques to evaluate potential investment opportunities. 2. Market Research: - Stay abreast of financial market trends, economic indicators, and industry developments. - Analyze market data to provide insights into investment strategies and potential risks. 3. Client Interaction: - Collaborate with clients to understand their financial goals, risk tolerance, and investment preferences. - Provide regular updates on portfolio performance and recommend adjustments based on client objectives. 4. Asset Allocation: - Assist in developing and implementing asset allocation strategies tailored to clients' investment objectives. - Monitor and rebalance portfolios to ensure alignment with strategic asset allocation targets. 5. Due Diligence: - Conduct due diligence on potential investment opportunities, assessing financial statements, management teams, and market conditions. 6. Regulatory Compliance: - Ensure compliance with relevant financial regulations and internal policies. - Stay informed about changes in regulatory requirements and industry best practices. 7. Performance Reporting: - Generate and present performance reports to clients, highlighting investment returns, risks, and market conditions. - Make recommendations for optimizing portfolio performance. 8. Relationship Management: - Cultivate and maintain strong relationships with clients, addressing inquiries and providing exceptional service. 9. Technology Utilization: - Stay current on advancements in financial technology to enhance operational efficiency. 10. Continual Learning: - Engage in ongoing professional development to stay current on industry trends, investment strategies, and financial products. - Pursue relevant certifications to enhance knowledge and credibility in the field.

title: Senior (Quantitative Analytics and Treasury Services) - Financial Services FAAS
description: At EY, you’ll have the chance to build a career as unique as you are, with the global scale, support, inclusive culture and technology to become the best version of you. And we’re counting on your unique voice and perspective to help EY become even better, too. Join us and build an exceptional experience for yourself, and a better working world for all. The opportunity Over the last 20 years, innovative new products have dramatically increased the complexity of the financial markets, from complex preference shares issuance in venture capital world, to structured products allowing clients to repackage and transfer risk, or other more exotic instruments designed to meet almost any goal two parties can agree on. We are looking to grow our Quantitative Analytics and Treasury Services (“QATS”) team within the Financial Services - Financial Accounting Advisory Services (“FAAS”) service line. To be specific, we are looking for valuation and quant finance individuals to support Assurance teams with independent valuation and quantitative analysis on various financial instruments and investments into private enterprises. Included are opportunities to develop individual consulting skillsets and to provide services in model governance and valuation frameworks. The service we provide within EY covers a broad spectrum of responsibilities with respect to financial instruments and business valuation. You will experience ongoing professional development through your interactions with internal and external clients, in conjunction with exposure to various asset classes (e.g. FX, Fixed Income, Equity, Commodity, Interest Rate, Credit, etc.) and industry sectors (e.g. banking, private equity, hedge funds, transportation, commodity, utilities, oil & gas, REITS, e-commerce, fintech, etc). The QATS team at EY uses sophisticated techniques from the field of financial engineering and quantitative finance to assist clients in developing quantitative models for pricing, valuation, risk management and regulatory compliance purposes. Your key responsibilities: Financial Derivatives Valuation and Business Valuation Work together within a team on multiple client engagements in daily independent valuation for a diverse range of financial instruments Assist in developing valuation models to assess a variety of linear and non-linear financial instruments by adopting option pricing theory, fixed income and structured finance models, Monte Carlo simulation, statistical analysis, and other quantitative analysis Perform model validation on derivative pricing models, risk management models and model parameter calibration models Provide assistance in review of fair valuation approach, methodology, and assumptions adopted by private equity and venture capitalist clients Perform portfolio valuation review for private equity, venture capital, and asset management companies with investments Value debt instruments such as straight bonds, convertible bonds, redeemable convertible preference shares Build financial models, such as Option Pricing Model and Binomial Tree Model, to value hybrid instruments Credit Modelling Perform model development and model validations on credit risk models for Basel and IFRS 9 purposes Identify sources of model risk through comprehensive review of all model components and developmental evidence and challenging the assumptions and basis of which the components are being derived Employ statistical modelling methodologies in performing validation and produce results to be analyzed from statistical as well as business perspective. Keep updated on industry developments in terms of regulatory requirements and best practice modelling techniques Write high quality model validation reports and/or presentations Project Management Technically contribute to managing projects which includes packaging overall project findings into clear, concise and high-quality deliverables Ensure all modelling tasks are completed timely Data Analytics Design meaningful application of data analytics to be used for auditing financial numbers including developing toolkit for data mining and work paper documentation standards Use data analytics to provide actionable, timely and accurate data to drive business insights around customer, regulatory risk, compliance, operations and financial to achieve greater return on investment. General Maintain and develop positive, productive, and professional relationships with clients Build valuable relationships with external clients and internal peers Challenge yourself to continually learn and teach, mentoring others while developing your own career through various exposures in client projects Take ownership of your projects, while collaboratively working with other team members Skills and attributes for success Work effectively as a team member, sharing responsibility, providing support, maintaining communication and updating team members on progress Highly motivated, ability to multi-task, work independently under minimum supervision and deliver top-quality results in a fast-paced, dynamic environment Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills Strong written and verbal communication skills Advanced written and verbal communication skills Demonstrate integrity, values, principles, and work ethics within a professional environment Good project management skills The successful candidate must be able to display several achievements below. Within 6 months of joining, the candidate should Understand the mechanics and valuation methodology behind various derivative financial instruments and perform valuation for such financial instruments using EY developed tools Be able to explain the valuation differences between client’s and EY’s independent valuation through analyzing how various input parameters and model choices and assumptions drive the valuation output Be able to perform credit risk assessment, focusing on quantitative analysis based on financial standing, for both listed and non-listed companies Understand how various credit risk parameters are developed and validated Gain basic understanding on programming languages such as VBA and Numerix, and apply it on Lattice Model, Monte Carlo Simulation and other model implementations when working on valuation engagements and R, Python, SAS and VBA when working on credit modelling engagements. Be able to work independently with minimal supervision and take ownership of projects assigned to be under the candidate’s ownership Within 1 year of joining, the candidate should Confidently manage clients and resolve issues typically encountered by clients with regard to financial instrument and business valuations Understand the valuation approach and ability to explain the economics underpinning various types of interests and exposures in private equity and venture capitalist businesses (e.g. liquidation preferences of preference shares), and perform valuation for such financial instruments Understand the Hedge Accounting concept under Singapore Financial Reporting Standard (SFRS) framework and be able to perform the quantitative approach for the purpose of hedge effectiveness assessment Understand the Incremental Borrowing Rate concept under Singapore Financial Report Standard (SFRS) framework and be able to perform the quantitative approach for the purpose of Incremental Borrowing Rate assessment Be able to explain to general non-technical audiences on the basics of how various financial instruments function, and the benefits and risks that they bring Explain to general non-technical audiences on the rationale of the assumptions, methodologies and movements of various credit risk parameters Be adaptive and committed to an intense environment and tight deadlines in providing deliverables to stakeholders Possess excellent time management and prioritize various projects allocated to the candidate Be able to work together within the team and actively provide guidance to junior staff members in order to deliver high quality work products After 1 year, candidates who demonstrate strong competency in quantitative finance has the opportunity to join the Quantitative Development Team to develop pricing model templates To qualify for the role, you must have Bachelor's or master's degree in quantitative finance, financial engineering, finance, statistics, mathematical finance, or engineering with programming skills A good programming skill with demonstrated aptitude in quantitative analysis and understanding of at least one Finance software package (e.g. Numerix, Murex and Fincad), Bloomberg, Reuters, MS Excel/VBA Ideally, you’ll also have Prior working experience in the banking industry in valuation control, pricing/credit model development and validation, market risk function Prior working experience in global professional services organizations or mid-tier firms The Chartered Valuer and Appraiser designation or be on track to be a CVA holder all of which will be highly advantageous in your application What we look for Highly motivated individuals with excellent analytical skills and the ability to prioritize shifting workloads in a rapidly changing industry. An effective communicator, you’ll be a confident team player that collaborates with people from various teams while looking to develop your career in a dynamic organization. The successful candidate must also be committed to staying at the cutting edge of both the latest financial instruments valuation techniques, business developments and accounting standards. We're seeking leaders with the ability to quickly anticipate and respond to the issues of a networked and constantly changing global economy. What we offer Continuous learning: You’ll develop the mindset and skills to navigate whatever comes next. Success as defined by you: We’ll provide the tools and flexibility, so you can make a meaningful impact, your way. Transformative leadership: We’ll give you the insights, coaching and confidence to be the leader the world needs. Diverse and inclusive culture: You’ll be embraced for who you are and empowered to use your voice to help others find theirs.

title: FINANCIAL ANALYST
description: APJ Juweller Pte Ltd, is a trusted shopping destination for Silver and Gold jewellery in Singapore. Being in Jewellery field, they need to do small ornament repairs or quality check work on their own. Currently we are looking for FINANCIAL ANALYST  for our firm . Role Overview We are looking for a high-caliber Financial Analyst who can operate in the complex, high-value environment of the gold and diamond jewelry business . This role requires a sharp financial mind, strong banking or investment-sector fundamentals , and the ability to rapidly learn industry-specific dynamics such as gold pricing, inventory valuation, and margin structures. This is a hands-on, accountability-driven role . You will be expected to move quickly from learning to execution and to protect profitability in a business where price volatility and working capital risk are constant. Core Responsibilities (Common Across All Units) Perform detailed financial, margin, and variance analysis across products, channels, and geographies. Analyze working capital , cash flows, and inventory funding structures. Build and maintain financial models for pricing, forecasting, and scenario analysis. Track exposure to gold price volatility , foreign exchange (for exports) and interest costs. Support budgeting, forecasting, MIS, and management reporting . Act as a financial control partner to business teams, ensuring data integrity and discipline. Continuously learn and adapt to jewelry-specific costing, valuation, and operational workflows. Unit-Specific Responsibilities Retail Jewelry Focus Analyze store-level profitability , same-store sales growth, and category-wise margins. Track making charges, discounts, schemes, and promotional impact on margins. Monitor inventory aging, fast vs slow movers , and capital lock-in. Evaluate customer financing, gold exchange programs, and loyalty schemes . Manufacturing Unit Focus Analyze manufacturing costs , wastage, yield loss, and recovery rates. Monitor WIP inventory , production efficiency, and cost absorption. Partner with operations to improve bill-of-materials accuracy and process costing . Identify cost leakages and recommend process and control improvements . Banking / Investment Skill Integration (Critical Requirement) Apply banking-sector discipline to cash flow forecasting, credit utilization, and fund deployment. Analyze interest costs, loan structures, and inventory funding mechanisms . Support investment-style decision-making : ROI analysis, capital allocation,and risk-adjusted returns. Interface confidently with banks, lenders, and financial institutions . Leverage experience in equity research, credit analysis, treasury, or corporate banking to elevate financial insights. Required Qualifications MBA in Business Management , Business Administration preferred 4-6 years of experience in: Jewelry industry OR Banking, investments, corporate finance, or treasury with strong analytical      exposure Advanced Excel skills ; experience with ERPs or financial systems preferred Key Competencies Strong learning agility – ability to master industry-specific concepts  quickly Analytical mindset with a risk-aware approach Ability to work in a fast-moving, high-value environment Professional skepticism—comfortable questioning numbers and assumptions Strong adherence and Compliance to Singapore Statutory requirements. Performance Expectations Achieve working knowledge of gold, diamond, and jewelry costing within the first 60–90 days Deliver actionable financial insights that improve margins and cash flow Strengthen financial reporting quality and decision-making Who This Role Is Ideal For Analysts from banking, investment, or financial services seeking to move into a real-asset, operating business ** WE REGRET TO INFORM THAT ONLY SHORT-LISTED CANDIDATES WILL ONLY BE NOTIFIED ***

title: Assistant Manager, Finance (NEC - Hospitality)
description: The NTU Office of Commercial and Auxiliary Services (OCAS) is responsible for the strategic planning, management, and marketing of commercial real estate, hospitality (including Nanyang Executive Centre) and auxiliary services (car parks and shuttle bus services) in NTU main campus and NTU@one-north. The Nanyang Executive Centre (NEC) is located at the NTU Main Campus. Currently undergoing refurbishment, the NEC features 150 guestrooms and aims to offer a premier venue for corporate training, retreats and private events. We are looking for an experienced person to join us as Assistant Manager, Finance. The Assistant Manager, Finance will be an integral part of the NEC Executive Office, playing a critical role in ensuring accurate management of NEC’s financial operations, including Cost Control, General Ledger maintenance, procurement and receiving processes. This position is also pivotal in supporting NEC departments by providing timely, accurate financial information and advice that enable NEC to achieve its business objectives. Key Responsibilities: Oversee daily finance operations, ensuring accurate capture and reconciliation of transaction data from the various functions. Manage accounts payable and receivable, ensure timely payments and collections and maintain accurate records. Support month-end and year-end closing activities, and reconciliation of accounts. Prepare monthly and annual management reports, including budget variance analysis. Assist with budgeting, forecasting, and rolling estimates for NEC. Provide financial insights to support business strategies and operational decisions Identify trends, and extract actionable insights, such as Average Daily Rate (ADR) and Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) . Ensure adherence to local and international financial and tax regulations. Identify and mitigate financial risks, develop contingency plans, and ensure compliance with regulations. Conduct half-yearly inventory count and reconciliation. Review contracts and invoices and ensure basic contractual compliance for outsourced partners/ vendors. Implement and monitor internal finance controls. Identify opportunities to improve processes and enhance NEC efficiency. Ensure NEC’s adherence to NTU’s finance and procurement policies and guidelines. Provide guidance to departments on accounting and finance related queries. Coordinate and support internal and external audits. Perform other assigned finance and general administration duties as assigned by the General Manager. Requirements: Diploma or Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance or a related field. At least 4 years of experience in finance or accounting roles, ideally within the hospitality or accommodation industry. Working knowledge of hospitality finance systems, such as Opera, SAP. Proficient in Microsoft Word, Excel, and Power Point. Strong attention to detail, excellent organisational skills, and the ability to manage multiple tasks. Effective communication and interpersonal abilities, with a collaborative working style. The appointment title offered will be commensurate with candidate’s prior work experiences. We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: Accounts Assistant

title: Analyst, FP&A
description: Our client is a US MNC in FMCG industry: Primary Purpose: This role will support key FP&A processes across multiple countries, with a strong focus on APAC CapEx management, expense planning, forecasting, and delivering actionable insights to drive business performance. Key Responsibilities The successful candidate will leverage their finance expertise to: Manage APAC CapEx planning and governance across all BUs and categories , including CapEx project review and approval coordination, oversee cash flow planning and depreciation/amortization forecasts aligned with store construction timelines, and ensure proper accounting treatment for asset write-offs and disposals during store closures. Support the monthly close and quarterly outlook processes , ensuring clarity and actionable insights for senior management by utilizing Hyperion Planning (Oracle PBCS) and SAP tools to extract data, develop forecasts, and analyze business trends. Analyze segment-level trends and develop accurate forecasts , focusing on capital expenditure consolidation and optimization as well as operating costs (including Depreciation, Amortization, and Store Pop-up expenses). Deliver accurate segment reporting and partner with business teams to drive profitability , taking ownership of the region’s P&L pillar . Contribute to Long Range Planning (LRP) and Annual Operating Planning (AOP) processes. The accomplished individual will possess: Bachelor’s Degree in Finance or Accounting. 5+ years of finance experience , ideally within FP&A, working with commercial functions and headquarters groups. Strong analytical skills and ability to work across multiple geographies/time zones. Collaborative mindset and high attention to confidentiality.

Overall 20+ years of experience in Banking Projects implementations - At least 10+ years of experience in Project Management & Program Management - Strong Understanding of building the Data Layer for the reporting. - Subject Matter Expert in CASA and DATA - Understand the Tri party dependencies and manages risks and issues effectively. - Excellent leadership and management skills - Ability to work in a fast-paced, dynamic environment - title: Regional Senior Business Analyst for Corporate Credit Origination and Processing, AVP/VP
description: Job Description: ·  While this is a BA role in IT department, we are looking at Senior BA or Product Owner who has the knowledge and experience to conceptualize strategic business solution, drive transformation and support product development in the corporate credit origination/processing domain for the Group. ·  Drive ongoing, long-term program and product roadmap for continuous enhancement of the platform, domestically and across the region. ·  To be able to effectively and efficiently engage business to understand their business strategy and product requirement to propose a build & implementation strategy that can meet their needs. ·  Propose strategic recommendations to drive growth, usage, and adoption of the platform. ·  Play a consultant and strategic partner role to business to propose options for consideration and drive outcome. ·  Ensure harmony, scalability and compatibility of proposed solution across the Group with regional mindset. ·  Able to navigate various dependencies and streams to achieve best outcome for the business within stipulated timeframe with resources provided. ·  Stay abreast of market/industry trends and best practices to proactively recommend innovative solutions for business. ·  Be the strategic partner and bridge between Business and Technology team during solution and all phases of SDLC and/or Agile/SCRUM sprints/delivery and long-term technology roadmap planning. title: Chief Financial Officer
description: Role Overview We are seeking a seasoned Chief Financial Officer (CFO) with strong software industry experience to lead the organization’s end-to-end finance and accounting function. This role will drive financial strategy, operational excellence, and long-term business value. The ideal candidate brings deep technical accounting expertise, experience in listed companies or listing environments, strong business and operational acumen, and a proven track record in people leadership and mergers & acquisitions. Key Responsibilities Strategic & Business Leadership Provide financial leadership and strategic guidance across the organization, influencing business performance, capital planning, and risk management. Partner with senior management to support corporate strategy, growth initiatives, and operational decision-making through data-driven financial insights. End-to-End Finance & Accounting Operations Oversee the full spectrum of finance and accounting functions including GL, AP/AR, payroll, taxation, treasury, financial reporting, and compliance. Ensure timely and accurate monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reporting in accordance with statutory and regulatory requirements. Lead audits, tax matters, and governance processes with a high standard of integrity and control. Listing & Regulatory Compliance Manage financial governance and compliance requirements for a listed company or companies preparing for listing. Ensure adherence to relevant accounting standards, regulatory frameworks, and reporting obligations. Budgeting, Forecasting & Financial Planning Lead budgeting, forecasting, and long-term financial planning processes. Provide insightful analysis on performance, variances, and profitability to support executive decision-making. Mergers & Acquisitions Lead end-to-end M&A activities including financial due diligence, valuation, deal structuring, integration planning, and post-merger financial integration. Partner with internal and external stakeholders to ensure successful execution of transactions. Risk Management & Internal Controls Establish and maintain robust internal controls, financial policies, and risk management frameworks. Proactively identify financial, operational, and compliance risks and implement mitigation strategies. People Leadership & Team Development Lead, mentor, and develop a high-performing finance team. Build a strong finance organization with clear accountability, capability development, and succession planning. Foster strong collaboration with cross-functional leaders across the business. Requirements Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field. Professional accounting certification is mandatory (CPA, ACCA, CA, or equivalent). Minimum 10 years of senior finance leadership experience, preferably at CFO, Finance Director, or Controller level. Software business experience is required. Experience working in a listed company or with listing exposure is required. Proven people management experience leading finance teams. title: Senior Associate, Treasures Relationship Manager (Offshore - North Asia Market), Consumer Banking Group (WD80882)
description: DBS Treasures is a leading wealth management platform in Singapore, uniquely positioned to cater to the diverse requirements of both Onshore and Offshore affluent clientele. Our comprehensive offerings provide clients with a seamless and all-encompassing banking experience, complemented by an extensive array of wealth management solutions, spanning from investments to legacy planning. Our Treasures clients have access to personalised advice and support from proficient relationship managers, seasoned investment counsellors, specialised bancassurance professionals and dedicated assistant relationship managers. The DBS Treasures Singapore organisation, which is part of Consumer Banking Group - Singapore, is committed to helping our clients navigate the ever-evolving financial landscape to achieve their individual financial goals and aspirations. As a Treasures Relationship Manager, our clients’ success is ultimately your success. Who you are: You have one goal: creating, growing and strengthening the wealth of our high net-worth customers and their families. This simple yet sophisticated goal drives you to uncover and understand our clients’ every need and deliver the right product solutions to enable their success. You have a strong sense of ownership. You take pride in helping our clients achieve their financial goals at every life stage, and work closely with other specialists within your team to create their success. You’re obsessed about providing a stellar client experience. This means you’re constantly staying abreast of the latest industry trends, investment product & service offerings, and fintech developments. You have a good understanding of equities, unit trusts, insurance and other personal investment services. You demonstrate strong interpersonal and excellent communication skills. You’re focused, disciplined and exhibit high personal standards. For this particular role, specific focus will be to cover the North Asia market. Apply Now We offer a competitive salary and benefits package and the professional advantages of a dynamic environment that supports your development and recognises your achievements.
