title: Associate Director, Client Electronic Strategic Solutions
description: RESPONSIBILITIES Strategy •    Contribute to the overall eCommerce Sales business agenda to expand electronic distribution, grow volumes and accelerate digitisation across all client segments wherever possible. Business •    Contribute to the design of and support the delivery of a client-focused electronic distribution solution across flow asset classes such as FX, FXO, Rates, Credit and Commodities across all client segments •    Knowledge of and experience in dealing with clients on their electronic flows, has a client-focused approached to Sales, anticipates and satisfies client requirements •    Strong knowledge of electronic trading marketplace with particular focus on FX Cash especially in restricted markets, FX Forward/ Swaps & FXO on single dealer proprietary channels, APIs and multi dealer platforms/ ECNs •    Custody FX - Focus on capturing any securities related FX from custody or funds mandate originated by Securities Services and FM on electronic platforms including our single dealer platform •    3rd party FX - Spearhead the growth of 3rd party FX & Passive Currency Overlay globally and directly contribute to the growth of the 3rd party FX franchise on the electronic platforms including our single dealer platforms. •    FX Options and Commodities – Adopt a client-led perspective in the solutioning and building out of our FX Options and Commodities electronic offering especially on our single dealer platform •    Good understanding of the core issues relevant to the electronic flow businesses, able to discuss market structure and practical issues relating to proprietary and multi dealer platforms, API feeds, STP as well as topical issues such as mandatory clearing, margin and regulatory reform •    Represent Sales to lead discussions with Trading and Product owners to design the right electronic product/ solution to enable connectivity with clients •    Awareness of market developments and business environment which our clients and the Bank operate •    Deep experience with handling client tier setups •    Client Analytics – Collaborate with the Client Analytics and Product teams to design and deliver a client performance monitoring tool for electronic flows on channels Processes •    Always respond to requests in a timely and efficient manner. •    Collaborate with Trading, Product, COOs, Technology and Operations partners to achieve simplification of process and maximise STP as far as possible •    Take ownership of all aspects of service delivery to the client within Financial Markets including support functions such as operations. People & Talent •    Work in collaboration with risk and control partners. •    Adherence to all relevant regulatory regimes. Risk Management •    Drive a culture that has adherence to all Group policies and relevant legislation covering credit, operational, reputational risk among others •    Commitment to Group and Financial Markets Code of Conduct •    Timely completion of all mandatory e-learnings, attestations, compliance requirements Governance •    Awareness and understanding of the regulatory framework, in which the Group operates, and the regulatory requirements and expectations relevant to the role. •    Willingness to ‘speak up’ in the event of any realised or perceived breach of group conduct rules or laws by colleagues Regulatory & Business Conduct •    Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct. •    Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct. •    Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters. •    MAS RNF prescribed responsibilities. Key Stakeholders •    Investor, BBD & Corporate Sales desk heads •    Macro Trading teams •    Financial Markets Product teams •    ITO, Finance, Control and risk functions Other Responsibilities •    Embed Here for good and Group’s brand and values in daily behaviour •    Perform other responsibilities assigned under Group, Country, Business or Functional policies and procedures Our Ideal candidate •    Bachelor Degree in Banking or Finance •    6+ years of experience in client facing strategic solution role with a focus on custody and 3rd party FX in restricted markets •    Strong knowledge of electronic trading marketplace with particular focus on FX Cash especially in restricted markets, FX Forward/ Swaps & FXO on single dealer proprietary channels, APIs and multi dealer platforms/ ECNs •    Licenses and certification: Relevant regulatory certificates •    Stakeholder management skills •    Strong communication skills Role Specific Technical Competencies •    Customer Behaviour and Preferences •    Knowledge of Product Line •    Sales Proposals and Presentations About Standard Chartered We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can't wait to see the talents you can bring us. Our purpose, to drive commerce and prosperity through our unique diversity, together with our brand promise, to be here for good are achieved by how we each live our valued behaviours. When you work with us, you'll see how we value difference and advocate inclusion. Together we: • Do the right thing and are assertive, challenge one another, and live with integrity, while putting the client at the heart of what we do • Never settle , continuously striving to improve and innovate, keeping things simple and learning from doing well, and not so well • Are better together , we can be ourselves, be inclusive, see more good in others, and work collectively to build for the long term What we offer In line with our Fair Pay Charter , we offer a competitive salary and benefits to support your mental, physical, financial and social wellbeing. • Core bank funding for retirement savings, medical and life insurance , with flexible and voluntary benefits available in some locations. • Time-off including annual leave, parental/maternity (20 weeks), sabbatical (12 months maximum) and volunteering leave (3 days), along with minimum global standards for annual and public holiday, which is combined to 30 days minimum. • Flexible working options based around home and office locations, with flexible working patterns. • Proactive wellbeing support through Unmind, a market-leading digital wellbeing platform, development courses for resilience and other human skills, global Employee Assistance Programme, sick leave, mental health first-aiders and all sorts of self-help toolkits • A continuous learning culture to support your growth, with opportunities to reskill and upskill and access to physical, virtual and digital learning. • Being part of an inclusive and values driven organisation , one that embraces and celebrates our unique diversity, across our teams, business functions and geographies - everyone feels respected and can realise their full potential. title: Compliance Officer- Associate/Assistant Manager

Flexibility to liaise with various departments in India and offices in other jurisdiction Experience Asset Management/Hedge Fund/Mutual Fund company, MAS, Legal Min 2-3 years KYC/ AML/ compliance experience in the finance industry

This role plays a critical front-facing and leadership function — engaging parents, managing the admissions journey, and delivering impactful marketing initiatives that translate directly into enrolments. Responsibilities Lead the development and execution of targeted student recruitment strategies to increase enrolment numbers Engage parents and prospective students through clear communication and relationship-building to enhance the admissions experience Manage the end-to-end admissions process to ensure a seamless and positive journey for applicants Design and implement marketing campaigns that effectively promote the school’s brand and value proposition Analyze recruitment and marketing data to measure campaign effectiveness and inform continuous improvement Collaborate with cross-functional teams to align marketing initiatives with overall school objectives and growth targets

title: Lecturer (NUS College)
description: Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUSCareer Portal Your application will be processed only if you applyvia NUSCareer Portal We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Job Description NUS College is the honours college of the National University of Singapore. Our focus is a rigorous and stimulating interdisciplinary education designed to help students cultivate the necessary skills to work effectively across boundaries, We plan to hire a dedicated individual  to fill a Lecturer’s position.  This is a full-time position that includes opportunities for pedagogical research funding and for promotion to an Associate Professor on the NUS Educator Track. The primary responsibility is to offer our mandatory courses, to which you will be expected to bring your own academic and intellectual expertise. Duties & Responsibilities For a start, you will be expected to offer the following, Computational Problem Solving (NPS2001) The CPS is a common curricular course designed to introduce NUS College students to the fundamental concepts, computational tools and techniques necessary to solve complex problems. The focus is on computer science and information technology, but course objectives are more general in nature, with the goal of developing essential skills for approaching and understanding new technology to critically assess potential benefits and risks. By having a comprehensive understanding of the potential of new technologies, students will be able to formulate a problem, outline potential technological solutions and set up a team to implement the solutions that can help achieve project goals. Impact Experience Project (NEP3001) The Impact Experience (IEx) is a eight-unit capstone course that all NUS College students will have to undertake for two years. Students will work in interdisciplinary teams to collaborate with non-profit partners and innovate future solutions for the benefit of communities and society in Southeast Asia, including Singapore. Each team of 6-7 students is supervised by a lecturer who specializes in the area they are working with. Students would choose to work with lecturers clustered into three broad areas: Science and Technology, Arts and Design, Civic and Community. Applicants from any field representing the Computer Sciences are welcome to apply for this position. We are interested in candidates whose work is cross-disciplinary and can be applied to better understand and tackle social, environmental and technological problems in the Singaporean context. The selected candidate's primary responsibility will be to teach the CPS together with other course instructors to ensure that students have a common curricular experience while allowing for individual instructor creativity in their seminars. Applicants should demonstrate their ability to teach such a course by providing evidence of teaching interdisciplinary computing courses in the university. The other responsibility will be to supervise IEx student teams working on projects that involve computing for social good. Successful candidates are expected to offer 2-3 sections of Computational Problem Solving in the academic year, an elective of their own design, supervise 3-6 IEx teams, and take on a small supervision load of independent study modules. Qualifications Candidates must hold a PhD. Remuneration and benefits are highly competitive on the global research university level. Expatriate benefits may include travel assistance from overseas and limited housing benefits. Appointments begin at the level of Lecturer on the Educator Track. Although the NUS Educator Track is not a tenurable line, it offers a pathway to promotion to Associate Professorship on an open contract (and beyond), as well as opportunities for pedagogical research funding, including overseas research and conference travel. The contract is for three years in the first instance and is renewable subject to good performance. Interested applicants should prepare and submit the following documents when they apply: •    A cover letter, clearly specifying which position they are applying for. •    CV: An abridged CV (5-page maximum), including details on education, employment history, past experience of teaching and supervision, research activities (including publications) and achievements, honours and awards, and service accomplishments. •    Qualifications: Include scanned copies of academic degrees. •    Teaching Portfolio: o    A Teaching Statement that commences with a preface (maximum 300 words) that makes the case for  appointment. This should be a highly distilled summary of key contributions to student learning and, if applicable, educational leadership, guided by your teaching philosophy. The rest of the Statement, of not more than 2,000 words, should outline your teaching philosophy and your educational contribution across multiple dimensions and roles. Please include evidence on how you have impact students' learning  experience and outcomes as well as how you have influenced peers and the community. o    Teaching evaluations and peer review reports, if available. Other evidence of teaching excellence and/or other contributions to education, e.g., curriculum development, pedagogical innovation etc., should also be provided. •    A proposal, of not more than 500 words, describing a prospective syllabus for the target course. •    A statement, of not more than 500 words, highlighting how you plan to contribute to pedagogical innovation and/or advance interdisciplinary honours education at NUSC. •    FOUR referees' letters (one of whom must be the applicant's PhD supervisor). Interviews will normally be conducted online. Only shortlisted applicants will be notified. More information about NUSC and our curriculum can be found on our website: https://nuscollege.nus.edu.sg/ Additional information about Academic Appointments at NUS can be found here: https://www.nus.edu.sg/careers/

title: Partnership Executive
description: Our Client The Talent People is representing a fast‑growing organization in Singapore that partners with leading brands to deliver high‑impact digital and social‑led marketing solutions. With a strong focus on creativity, data‑driven execution, and community‑centric storytelling, our client works across diverse sectors to help brands engage modern audiences in meaningful ways. About The Role As the Business Development Executive , you will be part of the partnerships function responsible for building new client pipelines, supporting commercial activations, and ensuring strong client satisfaction that drives repeat business. You will work closely with senior partnership leaders to convert opportunities into active projects and ensure smooth collaboration across internal teams. Key Responsibilities: Pipeline Development Research and identify potential clients across multiple industries. Map key decision‑makers and organizational structures. Drive outbound prospecting through digital outreach, referrals, and internal databases. Maintain accurate CRM records and track lead progression. Support senior team members with early‑stage outreach, proposal preparation, and coordination. Sales Activation Support Co‑develop proposals and pitch materials with the partnerships team. Coordinate with strategy and creator‑related teams to align with client briefs. Prepare timelines, talent recommendations, and supporting documents for presentations. Participate in partnership calls, document key actions, and ensure timely follow‑through. Campaign & Client Servicing Support Work with internal operations teams to ensure deliverables remain on schedule. Maintain regular client touchpoints, manage minor updates, and monitor satisfaction. Gather post‑campaign results and assist in developing case studies. Internal Process & Reporting Provide weekly updates on pipeline status and lead progression. Track individual performance metrics through CRM dashboards. Experience Required: Strong understanding of social platforms, digital behaviour, and online trends. Minimum 2 years in a client‑facing or commercial role. Experience in Sales Development Representative (SDR) roles or similar business development functions. Background in media, digital, or technology‑related industries is preferred. Strong communication skills, close attention to detail, and comfort working in a fast‑paced environment Come from a creative marketing agency is highly desirable Education/Qualification A relevant tertiary qualification in Business, Marketing, Communications, or a related field. Additional training in sales, digital marketing, or CRM systems will be advantageous. Why This Role? This is an exciting opportunity to join a dynamic and expanding organization where you will play a key role in driving commercial growth and shaping client relationships. If you are proactive, relationship‑driven, and passionate about the digital landscape, we’d love to hear from you! The Talent People 24C2589

title: ASSISTANT COMPLIANCE OFFICER
description: JOB DESCRIPTION: ASSISTANT COMPLIANCE OFFICER We are a well-established Wealth Advisory and Asset Management Firm in Asia Pacific. We are looking for a proactive and detail-driven Compliance Assistant to support our compliance and operations functions in a fast-paced environment. You will play a key role in ensuring compliance with MAS regulations and company policies, while working closely with cross-functional teams for direct hands-on execution of operational and compliance deliverables. The role offers the opportunity for growth and self-development within a rapidly growing business environment. Key Responsibilities Operations Business Tie Ups and Relationships •Facilitate the operational setup for new partnerships •Assist in engaging with our third party partner arrangements (such as Fund Managers, Banks, etc.) towards ensuring the extension of holistic solutions to meet our Clients’ wealth management needs •Assist in tie up of relationships with business partners including but not limited to coordination for due diligence, agreements and necessary set up •Support the running of business development initiatives and projects such as maintaining a firm wide central pipeline, tracking adoption and efficacy of recommendations made by the Firm, assisting with the running of any key control checks specified for sales management •Assist on periodic reviews of external partners Fund Operations •Manage the end-to-end setup of new Variable Capital Companies •Support all ongoing fund operations Operations support for Other Investments •Lead and execute structuring and facilitation of investments and/or products including but not limited to private equity deals introduction •Lead and support all ongoing work relating to structuring and facilitation of investments and/or products including but not limited to private equity deals •Support all ongoing operations for other investments relating to private equity deals introduction General and Ad-Hoc Support •Provide support to Financial Advisors in all matters relating to clients and prospective clients ranging from client queries and any ad hoc requests. •Liaise with Financial Advisors, Investment Counsellors, Operations Team, Clients, prospects, banks and other external service providers Compliance •Conduct thorough due diligence, KYC checks, and ongoing monitoring on new business tie-ups, partners, investors, clients, investees etc •Review agreements and all related documents including but not limited to Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDAs), Collaboration Agreements etc as required •Draft agreements, addendums, side letters, commitment letters etc as required for clients, business partners, employees etc •Review new business initiatives to ensure adherence to compliance in all aspects including but not limited to relevant government regulations, CRS, FATCA, and other jurisdictional reporting requirements. •Design and implement scalable SOPs, compliance procedures and protocols required for new business initiatives. •Assist in policy reviews, process improvements, and the enhancement of internal controls across compliance and operations. •Prepare and submit reports to relevant regulatory bodies as required – including but not limited to MAS filings, CISNet filings etc •Support audit of group entities and funds •Review and approve marketing decks, advertising materials, factsheets etc in accordance with MAS Guidelines and industry norms. •Coordinate board meetings for group entities •Conduct ongoing risk management assessment as required •Conduct ongoing transactions monitoring - Monitor transactions, identify potential suspicious activities, and escalate when required. •Conduct Outsourcing assessment on vendors and business partners as required •Conduct and document continuity planning (BCP) and disaster recovery (DR) drills, implementing corrective measures •Ensure compliance with industry security standards and regulatory expectations (MAS, FATF, PDPC) •Stay updated on industry developments and best practices in compliance What We’re Looking For •Strong familiarity with MAS regulations, VCC structures, and AML/CFT frameworks •Ability to draft and/or review legal agreements and documents •High standards of ethics and conduct; ensures confidentiality of Clients’ assets & information •Proven track record in compliance, fund operations and financial reporting systems. •Strong knowledge of compliance frameworks and operational risk management. •Ability to personally execute operational and compliance deliverables, not just oversee. •Strong organisational skills with initiative while completing mission-critical tasks. •Excellent problem-solving skills with resilience to manage escalations under pressure. Why Join Us? This is a unique opportunity to step into a hybrid operations + compliance support role where you get wide exposure and deliver directly on tasks that impact clients and the business. If you aspire to grow and excel with a high growth firm, please forward us your CV to Recruitment@tauruswealth.com.sg. We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: Director, Transactions Management
description: About the role You will deliver Portfolio Strategy and Transaction Management (TM) services to some of the world’s largest companies. This position calls for an energetic, detail-oriented professional with excellent project management, analytical, communication and collaboration skills as well as experience of managing real estate strategies and transactions across Asia Pacific. Comfortable in working towards achieving performance targets, you will be responsible to deliver a range of possible real estate and workplace solutions to clients looking for the next generation benefits that come from a vested partnership with JLL. Portfolio Strategy & Planning – Country, City and Metro plans, Global Initiatives Assessment of portfolio performance, portfolio rationalization and divestment, lease vs. ownership structures, cost reduction initiatives and bid support to achieve business change. Lead planning process to develop strategies to lower real estate costs and improve workplace effectiveness. Identify opportunities for optimization, cost saving and improved ROI in the Client portfolio Conduct fact-based analysis using occupancy, portfolio, real estate, and business data to align stakeholders on informed real estate plans. Through strong business relationships and clear, compelling business cases, challenge status quo thinking to drive unexpected portfolio optimization opportunities Develop overarching strategies related to workplace, organization, process, sustainability, operations and finance to govern the portfolio and set a foundation for city master plans Analyze inputs from the strategic planning process and on-account systems (business requirements, costs, facility information, cap-ex requirements, forecasts, occupancy data, lease and market data) Analyze and make recommendations with respect to strategic dispositions, investment and market opportunities, hold versus sell analysis, feasibility studies, asset review and market studies, highest and best use analysis, acquisition and advice analysis and valuations for sale/leaseback purposes Develop multiple practical options supported by strong business cases that optimize NPV and enhance productivity and innovation Be a SPOC for all Portfolio related client inquiries and a representative in global calls and initiatives Manage expectations, timelines and delivery against Client requirements, represent both JLL and the Client’s interest on the long term Transaction Management You will execute a wide variety of real estate transactions, including capital and lease acquisitions, sale and leasebacks and disposition transactions through local Markets teams across Asia, demonstrating a high level of proficiency and knowledge. You will actively engage with client’s senior stakeholders to define objectives and agree requirements at the outset of a strategy and transaction. Financial Acumen You will need to: Prepare and evaluate financial analysis documents. Support and contribute to the client’s budget forecasting. Forecast revenue and initiate and close-out projects to meet monthly cashflow plan. Monitor and closely control legal budgets and resolve billing queries. Relationship Management You will build strong and sustainable working relationships and help the client navigate through the JLL delivery platform in an efficient and productive manner. You will be the face of Portfolio Strategy and Transaction Management towards the Client both regionally and globally. People Management You possess strong leadership skills to manage brokers across geographies and actively engage across the client’s wider corporate real estate team and internal stakeholders in a collaborative manner. You demonstrate a high level of proficiency and knowledge in portfolio management and possesses the ability to orchestrate complex activities across multiple countries/properties/services at once to accomplish goals and objectives. Commercial Acumen You will understand account profitability, formulate an account plan to achieve revenue and performance targets whilst meeting client service standards. Armed with a sharp business acumen, you will leverage on technology and new products within JLL to deliver the next generation of benefits for our client. Operating a Global Account Can you see the bigger picture? Can you juggle regional delivery that aligns to a global strategy? As the TM lead for Asia on a Global Account, you are proactive in bringing Asia into the forefront, highlighting successes or potential issues whilst providing tangible solutions for the client. Can you communicate clearly and guide the client and your team on the best way forward for best in class processes and portfolio strategies at an account level? Sound like you? Here is what we’re looking for: Great Communicator Can you get your ideas across clearly both verbally and in writing? You’ll be working with many diverse interest groups so the way you share information and your ability to persuasively convey information is highly important. A high EQ is must for this role. You will have exceptional client management, communication and presentation skills and the ability to absorb and translate broad concepts and ideas into clear and logical communications. Ambitious You will be results-oriented with confidence to liaise effectively with all levels of management. Have commitment to collaboration and teamwork, and would thrive to exceed team goals. Resilient Transaction Management is a highly dynamic part of JLL’s business. It’s fast paced and you’ll need to be fast paced as well. You’ll need a positive mind set and a ‘can do’ attitude when multi-tasking driving change and delivering efficiencies. Naturally proactive, you’ll possess the innate ability to identify risks in delivery before it becomes an issue. At JLL, we have a continuous improvement mentality across our business and we’ll be looking for you to have the same. A flexible, resourceful and proactive individual who is able to produce results and guide the team to deliver the same. You will have the ability to work well under pressure and support the team without compromising quality. Experienced You will be an experienced real estate professional with a minimum of 10-12 years’ experience who has delivered positive results across the APAC region. You will be familiar with working with fast moving MNC clients with a regional presence and multiple service lines and possess prior experience in managing a team.

title: Lead/Senior Application Support Engineer (2 Year Contract)
description: Job Summary You will be working with our client who are well renowned in the public and financial sector. You will be part of the payments team responsible for managing systems that support electronic payments, domestic securities, and government securities operations. Your role will involve supporting interbank payments, securities, and trade settlements, while understanding and maintaining the connections between these systems and other market infrastructures. Responsibilities Lead the development, deployment, and maintenance of application systems, ensuring alignment with prescribed service levels and timely incident resolution Evaluate and recommend Systems Development & Maintenance Methodologies for projects, analysing the impact of IT changes on business operations Facilitate Agile ceremonies and translate user needs into actionable user stories, while improving processes and analysing project metrics Manage the agency's application portfolio, recommending new applications, consolidations, and decommissioning as needed Oversee application testing processes, including assessment of test scenarios, environments, and results, proposing improvements and changes as necessary Develop tender specifications for Agile projects, evaluate proposals, and manage outsourced vendors to ensure compliance with Agile methodologies and ICT governance policies Apply design thinking and service design principles to identify transformation opportunities and develop service experience prototype Establish reliable operations of the applications and digital service in the portfolio and conduct maintenance and technology lifecycle management on the applications and systems Establish quality and mechanisms for systemic quality across all applications and systems to ensure compliance with ICT Governance policies and standards Establish risk management for applications systems Requirements Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field; or equivalent work experience 8+ years’ experience in payments and securities settlement systems, including 2–4 years in application support, operations, or project management in a tech environment. Proven application management experience: deployment, monitoring, incident handling, and maintenance of complex systems. Experience in designing and implementing J2EE and/or .NET applications, SQL skills, and shell scripting Strong understanding of Server-side, .Net and/or Java/microservices, web services, RESTful API, containers and Kubernetes Experience with RHEL, JBOSS EAP, OpenShift, OpenStack, CICD, Watermelon, Maven, Oracle Database, Microsoft SQL Server and fault-tolerant infrastructure concepts Experience installing, configuring, and implementing new applications and system on AWS, preferably on Experience in GCC will be an added advantage Strong understanding and experience handling file transfers - interfaces, interfaces resiliency, connectivity protocols e.g., Rest, SFTP, MQ Experience with integration of Autosys, designing job scheduling, CI/CD, Datawarehouse, and DMS Experience with setup, and integration of Observability platforms, monitoring, and alerts. Experience with the S.W.I.F.T messaging platform and interfaces Certifications in relevant skills (e.g., Java, AWS solution architect, Kubernetes, etc.) will be an advantage Strong knowledge of Agile methodologies and tools. Expertise in business analysis: eliciting requirements, developing business cases, and translating between technical and business needs. Proficiency in application testing, assessment, and improvement. Experience managing vendors and developing IT project tender specifications. Familiarity with ICT governance, service design principles, and digital transformation initiatives. Strong problem-solving skills and ability to recommend process improvements and digitalization. Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills. Driven, results and customer oriented and able to work independently Organised and comfortable managing multiple workstreams simultaneously and have good interpersonal skills Articulate with strong written, presentation skills Detailed and capable of handling time-sensitive and high-pressure situations Natural problem solver who thrives in ambiguity and can design processes where none exist This role is a direct 2-year contract with the client. If you are interested in this role and would like to discuss the opportunity further please click apply now or email Chew Kai-Xinn at kaixinnchew@morganmckinley.com for more information. Only shortlisted candidates will be responded to, therefore if you do not receive a reply within 14 days please accept this as notification that you have not been shortlisted. title: Service Delivery Manager/Service Senior Manager
description: NCS is a leading technology services firm that operates across the Asia Pacific region in over 20 cities, providing consulting, digital services, technology solutions, and more. We believe in harnessing the power of technology to achieve extraordinary things, creating lasting value and impact for our communities, partners, and people. Our diverse workforce of 14,000 has delivered large-scale, mission-critical, and multi-platform projects for governments and enterprises in Singapore and the APAC region. As a Service Senior Manager, you will manage and govern application and systems operations (Application Manager Services, AMS) for NCS Telco+ SBG’s clients. The role carries primary accountability for AMS delivery excellence, while also supporting platform evolution, customer success, solution delivery, and growth initiatives. The SSM will operate as a techno-functional leader, combining strong understanding of procurement business processes with hands-on experience across different systems and SaaS platforms. The role requires the ability to manage multiple customers simultaneously, each with different maturity levels, priorities, and operating models. What will you do? Service Delivery & AMS Governance (Primary KPI) Own end-to-end AMS delivery and governance for multiple e-procurement customers. Ensure delivery against SLAs, KPIs, and contractual commitments across accounts. Lead L2 governance covering incident, problem, change, and release management. Drive operational reviews, service improvement plans, and risk mitigation. Act as the escalation point for delivery, service quality, and customer satisfaction. Multi-Customer & Stakeholder Management Manage multiple customer accounts concurrently with clear prioritization and governance. Serve as the primary delivery interface for senior stakeholders across all domains and functional areas per client setup. Balance competing priorities while ensuring consistent service quality across customers. Build trusted-advisor relationships and support long-term customer success. Onshore–Offshore Delivery Leadership Lead and coordinate delivery with offshore AMS and delivery teams across India, Philippines and China. Ensure effective planning, workload management, quality assurance, and documentation. Support capability building, mentoring, and performance management of delivery teams. Drive standardization, best practices, and continuous improvement. Solutioning, Delivery & Growth Support Support pre-sales activities including solutioning, estimations, pricing, and proposal development. Contribute to delivery planning for enhancements, rollouts, and transformation initiatives. Provide advisory input on digital procurement operating models and best practices. Support internal leadership initiatives and cross-account collaboration within NCS. The ideal candidate should possess: 10+ years of experience in IT Systems and Application Management support services. Proven experience managing AMS delivery for multiple customers. Strong techno-functional expertise in Payment / Payment wallet and other IT systems. Hands-on experience with IT systems, having completed entire SLDC / Agile implementations of at least 5m SGD in Implementation value and Managed operations of multi-year AMS contracts worth at least 10m SGD. Solid understanding of Managed Operations / AMS methodologies such as ITIL v4, COBIT, SRE Certifications, FitSM, ISO/IEC 20000 and similar. Experience leading onshore–offshore delivery models, preferably with India-based teams. Strong customer-facing, governance, and stakeholder management skills – preparation and presentation of Project and Executive Steerco. Hands on experience managing at least team size of 8 persons (direct reports) Must have demonstrated application of AMS governance via best practice ITSM tooling and have deep understanding and experience in Service Requests, Change Management, Cyber Security and Enhancement and Development. Used industry leading ITSM tool and ITIL v4, COBIT, SRE Certifications, FitSM, ISO/IEC 20000 certifications or equivalent Experience with RFPs, pre-sales solutioning, and pricing models. Background in large enterprise, regulated, or multi-country environments. Familiarity with APAC customer landscapes (Singapore, Australia preferred). Ideally have had at least 4 years of experience managing and running Managed Operations at Singtel or Singtel Group Leadership & Behavioural Competencies Ability to manage complex, multi-customer delivery environments. Strong ownership and accountability mindset. Excellent communication and executive presence. Ability to bridge business, functional, and technical perspectives. Collaborative, adaptable, and delivery-driven leadership style. We are driven by our AEIOU beliefs—Adventure, Excellence, Integrity, Ownership, and Unity—and we seek individuals who embody these values in both their professional and personal lives. We are committed to our Impact: Valuing our clients, Growing our people, and Creating our future. Together, we make the extraordinary happen. Learn more about us at ncs.co and visit our LinkedIn career site. Scam Alert We are aware of fraudulent job offers and impersonations of NCS recruiters. Phishing emails using convincing-looking but fake addresses are also commonly used to trick you into thinking that they come from official NCS sources. Please note that all official communications from NCS Group will only be sent from verified corporate email addresses. Always check that the sender’s email address ends with the genuine NCS domain, @ncs.com.sg and beware of extra letters, symbols or misspellings. When in doubt, verify the sender’s identity by contacting us at reachus@ncs.com.sg .

title: Brand Marketing Executive
description: Brand Marketing Executive (Finance Sector) TVG Management Pte. Ltd. Role Overview : We are looking for a Brand Marketing Executive to support and strengthen TVG Management’s brand positioning within the financial services industry. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys building brand presence, crafting compelling messages, and executing marketing initiatives that support business growth. Plan and manage marketing campaigns across digital, social media, and offline channels. Create and manage content such as marketing materials, social media posts, and campaign visuals. Ensure brand consistency across all touchpoints and communications. Support recruitment branding and corporate branding initiatives Track and analyse campaign performance and recommend improvements. Coordinate with internal stakeholders to support marketing and business objectives. Requirements : Min diploma of any related field. 1–3 years of experience in branding, marketing, or related roles (finance or professional services is a plus). Familiar with social media platforms and relevant marketing tools. Strong communication and copywriting skills. Creative, organised, and able to manage multiple projects. Career development and learning opportunities. Supportive and collaborative team environment. Interested candidates are welcome to apply.

title: Marketing Executive
description: About Cognitus Academy Cognitus Academy is committed to delivering high-quality English education for both Primary and Secondary School students, specialising in student-centric learning experiences. title: Senior Accountant
description: Objectives of the position We are seeking a detail-oriented and proactive Senior Accountant to join our Finance team. This role is responsible for managing the full spectrum of accounting activities, with a strong focus on month-end closing, general accounting, fixed assets, tax compliance, and process improvement. The ideal candidate is analytical, organized, and able to work independently within a fast-paced environment. Key Responsibilities: Ensure a timely and accurate financial month end closing and reporting. Prepare journal entries and reconciliations to ensure accuracy and compliance with accounting policies. Manage day-to-day general accounting functions, including general ledger maintenance, fixed asset accounting, and staff expense claims Maintain and update the fixed asset register, including additions, disposals, transfers, and depreciation. Analyze fluctuations in key balance sheet and P&L accounts to ensure data accuracy and completeness Prepare audit schedules and supporting documentation Liaise with internal and external auditors to ensure smooth audit processes. Support the preparation of corporate income tax, GST, and other statutory filings, including statutory surveys. Actively seeking areas of improvement and introducing automation and other measures to improve process efficiency. Any other adhoc duties assigned. Skills & Qualification: Diploma/Degree in Accounting or equivalent professional qualification. Candidate with at least 8 years working experience in Financial Accounting. High attention to detail, strong analytical skills, and a proactive work attitude. Strong knowledge of IFRS/SFRS and financial reporting principles, Good Knowledge of SAP-FICO would be highly preferred. Experience in setting up Shared Service Centre is a plus. Mature personality with excellent interpersonal and communication skills. Ability to work independently with emphasis on accuracy and timeliness.

title: FINANCE CUM ADMIN EXECUTIVE
description: We are seeking a motivated and detail-oriented Finance cum Admin Executive to join our team. This role is ideal for individuals who thrive in a fast-paced environment and are looking to grow their career in the F&B industry. You will play a key role in supporting the company’s finance and administrative functions, contributing to efficient operations and financial accuracy. Key Responsibilities: Handle full sets of accounts independently, including AR, AP, GL, bank reconciliations, and month-end closing. Prepare and monitor budgets, forecasts and profit & loss reports. Perform daily and monthly finance-related administrative duties. Support outlet-level stock audits and ensure proper  documentation. Maintain accurate financial records using Xero accounting software. Perform general administrative tasks such as data entry, document filing and handling outlet-related documentation. Work closely with the outsourced accounting firm to assist with Accounts Payable (AP) tasks, including verifying supplier invoices, matching them to delivery orders and purchase orders, and preparing payment summaries for processing. Work closely with the HR department on payroll-related matters to ensure timely and accurate salary disbursements. title: Public Relations - Managing Director
description: As a dynamic, collaborative, and extremely talented group of storytellers spread across 20 countries worldwide, headquartered in Singapore, we are committed to creating the best for our clients, delivering excellence to our employees, and making a positive impact on our societies. If you are someone who strives to raise the bar and consistently gives your best, we invite you to join our growing team! The Managing Director based in Singapore , is responsible for driving sustainable growth, strengthening market leadership, and delivering financial performance across the Singapore business. This role requires approximately 70% focus on new business development and 30% on managing and leading teams , while collaborating closely with regional and global stakeholders within the Redhill Group. Key Responsibilities New Business Development & Growth: Lead the identification, development, and acquisition of new business opportunities to expand the company’s footprint across all Business Units in Singapore, with involvement in other markets as required. Proactively source and pursue new business opportunities, excluding those generated via incoming GeBiz tenders, general inbound email enquiries, and internally referred prospects. Take full ownership of the end-to-end new business process, including: Developing compelling proposals and strategic solutions Leading pitch presentations and negotiations Managing all contractual and legal documentation (e.g. MSA, SOW, and related agreements) through to deal closure Drive new business across clients requiring Public Relations, and integrated communications solutions. This role is focused on deal origination and closure and does not extend to ongoing client servicing beyond account handover to the relevant Business Units. Singapore & Regional Market Expansion Develop and execute market-specific growth strategies for Singapore, aligned with industry trends, competitive dynamics, and evolving client needs. Support expansion into other markets as required, in close collaboration with regional and global leadership. Strengthen Redhill’s brand positioning, visibility, and leadership within the local communications ecosystem. Partnerships, Stakeholder Engagement & Networking Build and nurture strong relationships with industry leaders, partners, media stakeholders, and key decision-makers to enhance credibility and unlock new business opportunities. Represent the company at relevant industry events, forums, conferences, and networking platforms. Revenue Growth, Financial Performance & Profitability Deliver annual financial targets set by management, including topline revenue, net revenue, and profit margins. Ensure profitability and commercial viability of new business accounts. Contribute to forecasting, budgeting, and pipeline management for the Singapore market, with additional market support as required. Own and manage the business unit portfolio’s P&L, taking proactive steps to improve profitability, manage costs, optimise resourcing, and identify efficiency opportunities. Ensure fair, competitive, and value-based pricing of services and solutions. Partner closely with Finance and Business Managers on all financial and commercial matters. Market Insights & Competitive Intelligence Monitor industry developments, market trends, and competitor activities to identify growth opportunities and potential risks. Provide timely, data-driven insights and recommendations to leadership to support strategic decision-making. Client Leadership & Strategic Advisory Act as a Business Unit Leader by setting a clear vision, strategic direction, and growth agenda. Provide senior-level strategic public relations counsel and communications guidance to direct reports and clients as required. Lead and advise on crisis communications when necessary. Collaborate cross-border with Redhill Group entities to deliver integrated and regional client solutions. People Leadership & Talent Development Lead, motivate, and inspire direct reports through strong leadership, clarity of direction, and accountability. Set clear objectives, manage performance, and conduct regular performance reviews. Foster a culture of learning, development, and continuous improvement within the team. Support talent growth, succession planning, and capability building across the business unit. We are looking for a dynamic and experienced leader who: Has 8–10 years of experience in Public Relations, preferably within a PR agency environment. Has a proven track record in driving new business development, including deal origination, pitching, and closing. Is able to lead and inspire teams, with strong people management and talent development skills. Has experience managing P&L, budgeting, and financial performance for a business unit. Demonstrates excellent stakeholder management and client relationship skills, with the ability to build credibility with senior clients and partners. Is strategic, commercially minded, and capable of balancing revenue growth with operational excellence. Has strong communication, negotiation, and problem-solving skills, with the ability to operate across regional and global teams. Preferred: Exposure to integrated communications, cross-border client management, and knowledge of industry trends and competitor landscapes.

title: Manager | Credit Risk
description: Join our Credit Risk division, which is responsible for analysing and assessing all credit and equity exposures taken by Macquarie to minimise the risk of loss arising from failure of counterparties to repay loans or honour contracts. Our division is also responsible for undertaking reviews of counterparties and transactions, and establishing appropriate limit frameworks. On principal transactions, we work with the deal team to understand risk versus reward profiles and develop risk mitigation strategies. At Macquarie, our advantage is bringing together diverse people and empowering them to shape all kinds of possibilities. We are a global financial services group operating in 31 markets and with 56 years of unbroken profitability. You’ll be part of a friendly and supportive team where everyone - no matter what role - contributes ideas and drives outcomes. What role will you play? You will conduct in-depth credit reviews and analyses of Commodities and Global Markets' counterparties in the Asia region, focusing on corporate clients. You will collaborate with global credit offices to secure credit approvals, partner with Macquarie's business groups to evaluate new products and transactions, and provide well-founded recommendations to credit approvers. Additionally, you will assist in structuring transactions, reviewing legal terms, and offering general advice and analysis. You will perform annual and new reviews of counterparties, stay updated on portfolio developments, and address ad-hoc credit requests. You will also maintain strong connections across the commodities market to stay informed of the latest news and developments. What you offer Over 5 years of regional Asia credit risk experience in a large financial institution and/or commodity trading house Physical commodities trading experience including energy products and trade finance Strong background in transactional credit risk analysis and approvals of OTC derivatives across commodities, including familiarity with negotiating ISDA and CSA documentation Proven experience in preparing counterparty credit reviews, credit recommendations and presenting to senior risk management Comfortable planning and re-prioritising workload in an unpredictable environment Confident and able to provide independent credit judgement and articulate this succinctly to the senior management team High degree of ownership, delivery and timeliness in a fast-paced, output focused environment Ability to build strong, open, dialogue-based relationships across a range of stakeholders. We love hearing from anyone inspired to build a better future with us, if you're excited about the role or working at Macquarie we encourage you to apply. What we offer At Macquarie, you’re empowered to shape a career that’s rewarding in all the ways that matter most to you. Macquarie employees can access a wide range of benefits which, depending on eligibility criteria, include: 1 wellbeing leave day and 5 additional paid Flexi-Leave days per year 20 weeks’ paid parental leave for primary caregivers along with 12 days of paid transition leave upon return to work and 6 weeks’ paid leave for secondary caregivers 2 days of paid volunteer leave per annum and donation matching Option to purchase additional annual leave Benefits and initiatives to support your physical, psychological and financial wellbeing including, comprehensive medical and life insurance cover and additional flexible wellbeing credits Access to a wide range of learning and development opportunities, including reimbursement for professional membership or subscription Hybrid and flexible working arrangements, dependent on role Reimbursement for work from home equipment About the Risk Management Group Our Risk Management Group works as an independent, and centralised function, responsible for independent and objective review and challenge, oversight, monitoring and reporting in relation to Macquarie’s material risks. We are a global team that aims to manage the risks of today and anticipate the risks of tomorrow. Our divisions include compliance, credit, financial crime risk, market risk, operational risk, aggregate risk and prudential, and central. title: Operations Officer (Banking – Cards)
description:
About Us Green Link Digital Bank is Singapore's inaugural wholesale digital bank focusing on supply chain finance, mainly serving MSMEs and aiming to help MSMEs grow and improve digitization. We are looking for an Operations Officer to grow our business and manage our Card Issuing business. Responsibilities Card Lifecycle Management: Manage the end-to-end card issuance lifecycle, including card embossing files generation, digital card provisioning, PIN management, card renewals, and replacements. Corporate Client Onboarding: Review and process Corporate Card applications, ensuring strict adherence to KYC/KYB (Know Your Business) requirements and internal credit policies. Vendor Management: Act as a liaison with card personalization bureaus to ensure timely printing and delivery of physical cards to customers. Settlement & Reconciliation: Perform daily clearing and settlement reconciliation with payment schemes (VISA/Mastercard) and internal General Ledger (GL) accounts. Investigate and resolve settlement breaks. Dispute Resolution (Chargebacks): Manage the end-to-end dispute resolution process. Review customer transaction disputes, initiate chargebacks where applicable, and ensure compliance with VISA/Mastercard dispute timelines and regulations. Fraud Monitoring: Monitor real-time transaction alerts using fraud detection tools; contact customers regarding suspicious activity and block/unblock cards as necessary. Process Improvement: Help establish Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) for the new Card business. Identify gaps in the Card Management System (CMS) and work with Product teams to implement the fixes (UAT support). Reporting: Prepare regulatory and management reports regarding card issuance volumes, transaction spend, active rates and fraud losses. Other responsibilities: Performing daily, weekly and monthly reconciliation of Nostro and Securities Custody accounts. Requirements Bachelor's degree in Banking, Finance, or equivalent. Minimum 4 years of prior work experience in similar function in banking industry. Strong awareness of KYC/AML regulations and full understanding of Risks and Control requirements. Strong working compliance knowledge in card operations. Strong working knowledge of applicable card products and best practices of card processes. Excellent communication skills with ability to multi-task while staying detailed oriented and having effective
time management skill. Ability to work independently while being an effective team player, with ability to manage work under pressure. Technological orientation with proficiency in MS Excel VBA an added advantage. By submitting your application, you have given your consent for us to collect, use and retain your data for a year as according to our prevailing data retention and PDPA policies. Should you have any enquiries on our data policy, please contact us at CustomerSupport@glbank.com.

title: Senior Finance Manager / Deputy Finance Controller
description: Role Overview The Senior Finance Manager / Deputy Finance Controller is a senior finance role responsible for overseeing end-to-end finance functions while supporting the CFO or Finance Controller in strategic, financing, and governance matters. The role covers full-spectrum financial management including accounting, reporting, budgeting, business finance, and capital management, with selected people management responsibilities. While the Group is not currently listed, the role requires experience in listed-company environments and a solid understanding of capital markets, governance, and reporting standards to support future readiness. Key Responsibilities 1. Financial Management & Reporting (Full-Scope) •	Oversee core finance functions including financial accounting, management reporting, budgeting, forecasting, and variance analysis. •	Ensure timely and accurate monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reporting in compliance with applicable accounting standards and internal policies. •	Work closely with the Finance Controller on statutory reporting, audits, and financial close processes. 2. Business Finance & Performance Management •	Partner with business units and senior management to provide financial analysis, performance insights, and decision support. •	Support business planning, cost control, capital expenditure evaluation, and profitability analysis. •	Provide forward-looking analysis and scenario planning to support management decision-making. 3. Capital, Financing & Treasury Support •	Support and execute financing activities including bank facilities, refinancing, and funding arrangements. •	Prepare cash flow forecasts, funding analyses, and financial models to support liquidity and capital planning. •	Monitor banking facilities, covenant compliance, and treasury-related matters. •	Maintain working relationships with banks, financial institutions, and external advisors. 4. Financial Governance, Compliance & Listed-Company Readiness •	Ensure strong financial governance, internal controls, and compliance with regulatory and group requirements. •	Support audit processes, including coordination with external auditors and advisors. •	Apply listed-company standards and best practices in financial reporting, disclosures, internal controls, and governance, where relevant. •	Support preparation of board and senior management materials with appropriate governance standards. 5. Cross-Functional & Stakeholder Engagement •	Work closely with the CFO, Finance Controller, and internal teams to support finance operations and strategic initiatives. •	Support communication and relationship management with key external stakeholders, including but not limited to banks, shareholders, auditors, and government-related agencies, on finance-related matters. •	Coordinate and prepare financial information, analysis, and materials for stakeholder discussions and engagements. •	Contribute to continuous improvement of finance processes, controls, and reporting standards. •	Provide ad-hoc financial support and analysis for management requests and special projects as required. Key Requirements Professional Background •	Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related discipline from a reputable local university is strongly preferred; a Master’s degree is an advantage. •	Professional qualification such as CPA, CA, ACCA, or equivalent is preferred. •	Prior experience in Big 4 accounting firms in Singapore is a strong advantage. •	Experience in listed companies is required, with exposure to financial reporting, governance, and capital market-related practices. •	Experience in China or PRC-related business environments is an advantage. Experience & Skills •	Strong hands-on experience across full finance functions, including accounting, reporting, budgeting, and business finance. •	Working knowledge of financing, treasury, and banking arrangements. •	Solid understanding of Singapore’s regulatory framework, corporate governance, and listed-company standards. •	Strong analytical, financial modelling, and presentation skills. •	Able to communicate effectively with senior management, auditors, banks, and external advisors. Personal Attributes •	Strong sense of ownership with a practical, hands-on approach. •	Able to balance operational detail with strategic perspective. •	High level of professionalism, discretion, and accountability. •	Comfortable working independently while supporting senior finance leadership. •	Fluent in English; proficiency in Mandarin is an advantage.

title: AVP, Risk Manager – Wealth Management
description: Position Overview - The role is focused on establishing the Conduct & Control Department as a proactive business partner and a key contributor to the Bank’s business strategy. - Acts as the first line of defense to ensure risk-based, sustainable, and efficient strategies are implemented to address Operational, Business, and Conduct risks in the APAC region. - Works closely with the Wealth Management (WM) business in APAC to manage and mitigate risks. Primary Role Responsibilities - The candidate will support the Head of Governance, Advisory & Surveillance, WM APAC Conduct and Control, in maintaining a robust risk framework for Operational and Business risks in collaboration with WM in APAC (Hong Kong and Singapore). - The position is based in Singapore and operates within the first line of defense (1 LoD). Key Responsibilities 1. Incident Investigation and Analysis - Ensure timely lodging and escalation of incidents. - Lead and coordinate in-depth investigations of operational risk incidents across Wealth Management in Singapore and Hong Kong. - Conduct structured fact-finding and evidence analysis to determine root causes and contributing factors. - Assess downstream and upstream impacts, ensuring proposed actions address immediate, past, and future issues. - Evaluate the adequacy and effectiveness of remediation actions and preventive measures. - Perform trend analysis to identify opportunities to strengthen the control framework. 2. Escalation and Governance - Ensure timely escalation of incidents to Senior Management in accordance with defined thresholds and governance requirements. - Prepare clear and concise incident summaries, analyses, and reporting materials for management and governance committees (e.g., ICC, ORCC). 3. Stakeholder Coordination - Serve as a key coordination point between Front Office, Operations, Compliance, Legal, Finance, IT, and other stakeholders for incident matters. - Liaise with Head Office and regional counterparts to ensure consistency in incident handling and alignment with Group expectations. - Facilitate effective ownership and accountability for incident remediation. 4. Reporting and Continuous Improvement - Produce incident reports (e.g., quarterly reporting) and trend analyses for management. - Contribute to the review and enhancement of incident-related procedures, escalation matrices, and governance frameworks. - Support internal audits, inspections, and ad-hoc risk reviews related to operational incidents. - Assist in various risk reporting requirements for different forums and to Head Office. 5. Risk Assessment and Reporting - Participate in the review and collation of annual Enterprise Risk Assessments, such as Risk Cartography Self-Assessment and Risk Identification. 6. Ad Hoc Projects - Undertake ad hoc projects as assigned by the Head of Conduct and Control – Governance, Advisory and Surveillance. Requirements - Strong understanding of risk control frameworks and operational risk management. - Excellent stakeholder management and collaboration skills. - Structured, detail-oriented, and methodical approach to work. - Strong analytical skills with the ability to assess and articulate risk impacts. - Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to present investigation findings clearly and concisely to governance forums. - At least 5 to 8 years (or more) of relevant experience in Operational Risk, Incident Management, Internal Control, Audit, or related risk functions, preferably within private banking or wealth management. - Proven experience in operational incident investigation, including fact-finding, timeline reconstruction, root cause analysis, and remediation assessment. - Sound understanding of Operational Risk Management frameworks, internal controls, and governance within the private banking sector. - Strong data analysis skills, with the ability to work on various dashboard reporting for Management and investigative work.

title: Financial Controller
description: Role Overview We are seeking an experienced Financial Controller to oversee the company’s finance, accounting, compliance, and human resources functions, while providing strategic financial insights to support sustainable business performance. This is a key role requiring strong professional judgment, independence, and ownership of financial governance, cost management, and people administration. Key Responsibilities Lead and take overall accountability for the integrity, accuracy, and timeliness of financial reporting, including management accounts, balance sheets, and profit & loss statements. Provide strategic oversight of the company’s accounting operations, cash flow position, and financial controls. Drive financial planning, budgeting, forecasting, and cost management initiatives to support sustainable business performance. Design, implement, and refine costing frameworks and cost control strategies to enhance financial visibility and decision-making. Ensure full compliance with all applicable accounting standards and statutory requirements in Singapore. Oversee GST, corporate tax, CPF, and statutory employee income reporting, ensuring timely and accurate submissions. Act as the primary liaison with auditors, tax agents, corporate secretaries, bankers, and regulatory bodies. Establish, enforce, and continuously improve accounting policies, procedures, and internal control systems to manage financial risk. Review and approve key financial transactions, payment runs, and expense claims with appropriate governance. Provide independent financial analysis, insights, and recommendations to management on business performance and strategic matters. Oversee human resources governance, including payroll oversight, statutory compliance, and alignment with MOM and Tripartite guidelines. Provide oversight of foreign workforce administration and labor compliance matters. Support overall business operations by ensuring effective administrative controls and compliance frameworks are in place. Requirements Bachelor’s degree in Accountancy, Finance, or a related discipline. Minimum 7 years of relevant hands-on experience, preferably in an SME environment, with exposure to finance, accounting, and HR functions. Strong knowledge of Singapore statutory and regulatory requirements (IRAS, CPF, ACRA, MOM). High level of accuracy, integrity, and professional judgment. Strong analytical, planning, and problem-solving skills. Ability to work independently, manage multiple priorities, and meet deadlines. Good communication and stakeholder management skills; ability to liaise with Chinese-speaking stakeholders is an advantage. Self-motivated, dependable, and committed to long-term business sustainability.

title: Senior Audit Manager, Third Parties and Operational Risk
description: JOB SUMMARY Group Internal Audit (GIA) in Standard Chartered Bank is looking for an exceptional and dynamic auditor to join our Third Parties and Operational Risk Team. The successful candidate will need to have core skillset in either Third Party Risk Management (TPRM), Source to Pay or familiar with the requirements for Operational Risk Management as set out in Capital Requirements Regulations and the BCBS 515 Sound Principles for Operational Risk Management. TPRM We engage Third Parties for a wide variety of goods and services to effectively run our business. These arrangements vary in complexity and risk.  All Third Party engagements must be managed appropriately in accordance with the underlying risks throughout the lifecycle - from sourcing, onboarding, vendor management to exit. Country regulatory requirements may apply for outsourcing arrangements in some markets. Operational Risk Management Operational Risk is an inherent part of the Group’s business. The 2LoD role is undertaken by Risk Framework Owners (for PRTs), Subject Matter Experts (for Operational Risk sub-types) and by Operational Risk Officers who look across multiple risk types. 2LoD Operational Risk performs independent reviews, oversight and challenge on the 1LoD Business and Functions. This is a role that provides exposure to auditing and risk management of Third Party and 2LoD Operational risk at Country and Group level. RESPONSIBILITIES •    To act as a Team Leader and take responsibility for overseeing the planning and execution of the audit, maintaining the audit budget and audit team. •    Ensure that the scoping of audit work assigned addresses the key risks identified in the detailed risk assessment and in the audit planning process and meets relevant regulatory requirements and expectations that are required to be covered by GIA. •    Drafting of the audit programme, audit observations/ issues and audit report. •    Provide technical input and challenge on audit work being undertaken within the scope of assigned product / country area of responsibility. This will include working with the audit team to produce outputs of high quality which address the areas of greatest risk. •    Using data analytics, evaluate quantitative and qualitative data to diagnose underlying issues, patterns, and root causes. •    Monitor/track assigned audit issues and action plans, and report overdue items with resolution. •    Support HOA in audit risk assessments and committee reporting, such as Audit Committees reporting. •    Support GIA audit teams by providing SME knowledge and expertise for their audits. •    Provide ongoing continuous monitoring support to Head of Audit (HOA) and raise issues and observations outside of formal audit work to expedite rectification of control weaknesses. •    Demonstrate leadership and ability to motivate and guide audit team members Processes •    Third Party Risk Management (TPRM), Source to Pay, Supply Chain Management or familiar with the requirements for Operational Risk Management as set out in Capital Requirements Regulations and the BCBS 515 Sound Principles for Operational Risk Management. Operational Risk Management Framework such as RCSA, Scenerio Analysis, Risk Taxonomies •    Champion innovation and increase the use of leading edge methods through data analytics and use of technology, experimentation & innovation and use of dynamic auditing. People & Talent •    Build good working relationships with the business senior stakeholders to facilitate execution of audit work, help improve the control environment and keep updated with changes in the risk profile of the business. •    Build a collaborative and inclusive culture that creates psychological safety, treating people with courtesy / respect and promote wellbeing. Foster a culture of learning and build the learning habit. •    Develop skills and competencies aligned to your role, and support the development of junior staff. Regulatory & Business Conduct •    Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct. •    Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct. •    Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters. Skills and Experience Our Ideal Candidate •    12+ years of experience in auditing or a risk and governance experience in Operational Risk Management or Third Party risks such as Outsourcing, Procurement or Third Party Risk Management with a Bank of global scale, MNC or Big-4 Accounting firm. •    Experienced in performing vendor onsite reviews across various types of vendor services or experiences in working with technology vendors such as cloud vendors will be an advantage. •    Understanding of Third Party or Outsourcing risks across business strategy, plans, products, performance and related issues. •    Strong communication skills, both written and verbal, with ability to influence business management, other stakeholders and peers. •    Confident and courageous to raise/escalate issues in a proactive, professional and timely manner. •    Track record of performing work independently with minimal supervision and meeting stretch timelines; comfortable to deal with ambiguity and solve problems. •    Ability to collect and objectively analyse and apply information, “thinking out of the box” where necessary, in supporting the planning, fieldwork or reporting of results of audit engagements. •    Displays initiative and knows when and how to seek guidance. Willingness and capacity to learn on the job. •    Responds enthusiastically to tasks allocated, accepts responsibility readily and demonstrates business professionalism. •    Good time management, well-organised with the ability to prioritise and plan workload to ensure delivery to timescales, with a good track record of delivery large-scale and complex audits. •    Certified Internal Auditor (CIA) / Certified Information Systems Auditor (CISA) / ORM certification is preferred Role Specific Technical Competencies •    Operational Risk Management •    Third Party Risk Management •    Audit About Standard Chartered We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can't wait to see the talents you can bring us. Our purpose, to drive commerce and prosperity through our unique diversity, together with our brand promise, to be here for good are achieved by how we each live our valued behaviours. When you work with us, you'll see how we value difference and advocate inclusion. Together we: • Do the right thing and are assertive, challenge one another, and live with integrity, while putting the client at the heart of what we do • Never settle , continuously striving to improve and innovate, keeping things simple and learning from doing well, and not so well • Are better together , we can be ourselves, be inclusive, see more good in others, and work collectively to build for the long term What we offer In line with our Fair Pay Charter , we offer a competitive salary and benefits to support your mental, physical, financial and social wellbeing. • Core bank funding for retirement savings, medical and life insurance , with flexible and voluntary benefits available in some locations. • Time-off including annual leave, parental/maternity (20 weeks), sabbatical (12 months maximum) and volunteering leave (3 days), along with minimum global standards for annual and public holiday, which is combined to 30 days minimum. • Flexible working options based around home and office locations, with flexible working patterns. • Proactive wellbeing support through Unmind, a market-leading digital wellbeing platform, development courses for resilience and other human skills, global Employee Assistance Programme, sick leave, mental health first-aiders and all sorts of self-help toolkits • A continuous learning culture to support your growth, with opportunities to reskill and upskill and access to physical, virtual and digital learning. • Being part of an inclusive and values driven organisation , one that embraces and celebrates our unique diversity, across our teams, business functions and geographies - everyone feels respected and can realise their full potential. Recruitment Assessments Some of our roles use assessments to help us understand how suitable you are for the role you've applied to. If you are invited to take an assessment, this is great news. It means your application has progressed to an important stage of our recruitment process. Visit our careers website www.sc.com/careers

description: Staff Data Platform Engineer, Singapore Airwallex is the leading financial technology platform for modern businesses growing beyond borders. Capability to identify genuine project opportunities through exhibitions, consultants, partners, and internal resources. Preferred competencies and qualifications Proven ability to coordinate multiple departments to complete bidding and negotiation processes effectively. Strong professional attitude characterized by transparency, trustworthiness, and stability under pressure.

title: Business Analyst (Digital Operations)
description: About the Role: We are seeking a skilled and analytical Business Analyst to play a critical role in our digital transformation journey with regional responsibilities.
