title: Business Manager
description: Your career at NTT DATA Business Solutions Empowering You – to feel our passion for SAP / Analytic Solutions / ServiceNow As NTT DATA Business Solutions, we are more than just a company. We are a team of innovative and passionate people who specialize in creating value-added SAP and Analytic solutions and guiding our customers on their journey to becoming intelligent businesses. We focus on SAP Consulting, SAP Development, Data Analytics, ServiceNow, Managed Cloud Services, Application Management Services (AMS) & Technology and beyond. Our success is based on TOGETHERNESS, INNOVATION, TRUST, and SUSTAINABILITY – this is the heart of our corporate culture. As part of the global NTT DATA Group, one of the top 10 IT service providers worldwide, we stand for our open and sustainable acting. More than 15,000 employees from 64 nations in over 30 countries show that diversity is integral to our DNA and that we live tolerance and respect every day. People are the key to our success. We believe that every individual can make an important contribution and are therefore constantly on the lookout for talented people who can actively strengthen our team. Are you ready to break new ground? Responsibilities: · The company is growing, and we are looking for a Business Manager that will be responsible for the development and implementation of Sales /Marketing strategies across the country. The purpose of the role is to sell the annual sales quota for the country with the right mix of licence, services IP and maintenance to deliver on the annual revenue and EBIT budget with the following key responsibilities: · Financial Performance: · Meet or exceed sales quota by region and revenue streams · Increasing market share in existing markets and maximize new business development opportunities · Meet or exceed cost management and operational performance as agreed in Sales budget through monitoring and controlling the Sales Team performance, taking responsibility for profit and loss including cost management of investment in Presales and Solutions to budget · Deliver Sales revenue and profit forecasts that are accurate and on time · Identify and implement actions to address any variances to budget · Communicate regularly with the leadership on revenue performance · Ensure the desired balance between licence sales and services sales is maintained · Develop and implement sales strategies that ensure that balance align with business strategies · Sales Excellence: · Proactively participate and contribute to organizational development · Work with peers to ensure sales and delivery teams work effectively together · Deliver operational improvement plans that ensure delivery of strategy · Build a consistent sales & marketing framework across all geographies, products and service offerings. · Exploit the true value of a combined approach to brand building, lead generation and sales conversion · Develop & execute Marketing Strategy to support & underpin annual business goals · Set annual sales strategy in line with business goals · Identify potential improvement areas in internal performance or solution offerings to market and where appropriate, recommend and manage the implementation strategy · Identification and exploitation of target sectors · Develop innovative approaches to support lead generation · To work closely with the consulting business to ensure smooth delivery to customers and customer satisfaction · Leadership: · Support Consulting Manager(s) in the sales/uplift of general services that are delivered by consulting · Implement a strong culture of discipline through effective hands-on operational management including half yearly performance reviews. · Provide coaching, mentoring & training to direct reports to guide them with career planning & development · Promote a professional environment of timely and open communication to ensure team members are aware of news & changes through regular information sharing meetings · Demonstrate and uphold company values · Partnership & Collaboration: · Deliver sales by creating long term partnerships and to strengthen ongoing business relationships · Work collaboratively within the NTTD Group to maximise opportunities · Manage relationship with business partners and principal partners across the country · Key Performance Measures (KPM) · Customer Retention · Customer Satisfaction · Sales Quota · EBIT · Number of Leads · Conversion Ratio Requirement : · 4 – 10 years’ sales experience ( IT / Enterprise sales experience ) · Bachelor’s degree (or equivalent) in Information Technology or related field of study · Effective Pipeline Management · Maximizing customer value and customer satisfaction Track sales record of Analytics Solutions and SAP Enterprise and Cloud solution Track sales record of SAP Enterprise and Cloud solutions, Managed Services Must have knowledge of IT Services Proven track record of driving profitable revenue growth in applications sales Deep technology industry experience · Excellent people and communication skills · Developed & managed a geographically diverse team · Developed & implemented internal processes & procedures · Developed & maintained alliances & partnerships · Previous senior leadership experience · Sales & marketing skills · Negotiation skills

title: Assistant Vice President, Post Trade Business Analyst Project Manager
description: Purpose of the role To support the organisation, achieve its strategic objectives by the identification of business requirements and solutions that address business problems and opportunities. Accountabilities Identification and analysis of business problems and client requirements that require change within the organisation. Development of business requirements that will address business problems and opportunities. Collaboration with stakeholders to ensure that proposed solutions meet their needs and expectations. Support the creation of business cases that justify investment in proposed solutions. Conduct feasibility studies to determine the viability of proposed solutions. Support the creation of reports on project progress to ensure proposed solutions are delivered on time and within budget. Creation of operational design and process design to ensure that proposed solutions are delivered within the agreed scope. Support to change management activities, including development of a traceability matrix to ensure proposed solutions are successfully implemented and embedded in the organisation. Assistant Vice President Expectations To advise and influence decision making, contribute to policy development and take responsibility for operational effectiveness. Collaborate closely with other functions/ business divisions. Lead a team performing complex tasks, using well developed professional knowledge and skills  to deliver on work that impacts the whole business function. Set objectives and coach employees in pursuit of those objectives, appraisal of performance relative to objectives and determination of reward outcomes If the position has leadership responsibilities, People Leaders are expected to demonstrate a clear set of leadership behaviours to create an environment for colleagues to thrive and deliver to a consistently excellent standard. The four LEAD behaviours are: L – Listen and be authentic, E – Energise and inspire, A – Align across the enterprise, D – Develop others. OR for an individual contributor, they will lead collaborative assignments and guide team members through structured assignments, identify the need for the inclusion of other areas of specialisation to complete assignments. They will identify new directions for assignments and/ or projects, identifying a combination of cross functional methodologies or practices to meet required outcomes. Consult on complex issues; providing advice to People Leaders to support the resolution of escalated issues. Identify ways to mitigate risk and developing new policies/procedures in support of the control and governance agenda. Take ownership for managing risk and strengthening controls in relation to the work done. Perform work that is closely related to that of other areas, which requires understanding of how areas coordinate and contribute to the achievement of the objectives of the organisation sub-function. Collaborate with other areas of work, for business aligned support areas to keep up to speed with business activity and the business strategy. Engage in complex analysis of data from multiple sources of information, internal and external sources such as procedures and practises (in other areas, teams, companies, etc).to solve problems creatively and effectively. Communicate complex information. 'Complex' information could include sensitive information or information that is difficult to communicate because of its content or its audience. Influence or convince stakeholders to achieve outcomes. All colleagues will be expected to demonstrate the Barclays Values of Respect, Integrity, Service, Excellence and Stewardship – our moral compass, helping us do what we believe is right. They will also be expected to demonstrate the Barclays Mindset – to Empower, Challenge and Drive – the operating manual for how we behave. Join Barclays as a Technology Business Analyst and Project Manager in Singapore, where you’ll: Manage requirements analysis and change delivery covering growth initiatives, regulatory mandates, simplification and controls within the APAC region including Singapore, Hong Kong, Japan, India and China. You will work within the Markets Post Trade Team facing off to Operations / Front Office and internal technology management stakeholders across locations and business lines. Key Accountabilities: Work with Front, Middle and Back office users as well the Development and Quality Assurance (QA) teams to deliver change on time and with high quality. Understand and document the requirements of the respective business and operational. Gain and maintain in-depth knowledge of post trade applications and regional flows for each business and product class as a Subject Matter Expert. Work closely with software development teams to ensure user requirements are clearly understood and developed as specified in the documentation. Gather development and testing estimates and work with the relevant product owners or project managers to include these in formal project plans. Manage various stakeholders across Front Office, Operations, Risk, Finance and Global Technology. Be pro-active in articulating bottlenecks, conflicting prioritization, and known/unknown gaps. Conduct comprehensive functional testing during the development process and work closely with the relevant users to complete user acceptance testing (UAT). Specific knowledge/experience: Prior experience within Markets Post Trade areas like payments, settlements, margining, confirmations or regulatory reporting and knowledge of how projects are run and managed in both waterfall and agile based environments. To be successful in this role: Essential Skills/Basic Qualifications: Knowledge of full cycle Investment Bank end to end processing. Project management and Business analysis skills and development lifecycle experience, working within IT, with proven delivery record and an ability to work in a demanding environment. Ease in working with matrixed and global environments. A proactive attitude towards problem solving and willingness to be a hands-on technologist. Desirable skills/Preferred Qualifications: Strong time management and ability to coordinate with various teams to action items. Ability to build ongoing work relationships across locations and teams. Experience with MS Visio, Project, Excel, Word, SharePoint, JIRA, Confluence, MS Teams etc.

title: Senior SME Mortgage Sales Specialist, Small Business Clients, SME Banking
description: Job Summary •    The Small Business Clients segment in SME Banking within Standard Chartered Bank aims to provide companies with turnover of up to US$25m in Singapore a suite of product that meets their financing requirements. •    As a SME Mortgage Sales Specialist, in addition to providing team support to your counterparts, you will be expected to actively acquire New to Bank (NTB) SME Mortgage relationships and cross sell the entire suite of SME Banking products to meet and exceed quarterly sales targets as stipulated in the “Quarterly Scorecard” as well as ensure that the NTBs acquired by yourself consistently comment on how amazing your service is. Performance Measures •    To achieve quarterly New Sales Revenue (NSR) targets through New to Bank SME Mortgage client acquisitions, and active cross-selling of SME Banking products Risk Measures To ensure complete compliance and quality of work that meets internal and regulatory requirements. •    Bank’s interest must be protected at all times. Raise any risk concerns in writing immediately to seniors. Ensure Bank’s is never at risk from your action, or inaction. •    Compliance with all the Bank’s policies without any delays. •    To engage in active cross selling of SME Banking suite of products to ensure that each NTB holds at least 2 Products Per Client (PPC). •    Drive cross product sales of all the Bank’s products and services for SME Banking and active referrals to Affluent (Individual) level. •    On a reasonable basis, to revert to all customer queries within 2 business days. Key Responsibilities Work Ethic •    Punctual at work and when visiting customers •    Self driven, self motivated, and a pro active approach toward resolving issues. •    Responsive, contactable and accountable to clients and internal stake holders. •    Team player ready to grow business, and ready cover team members duties as necessary. •    Appropriate dressing that must meet Bank’s standards. Regulatory and Business Conduct •    Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct. •    Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct. •    Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters. Skills and Experience •    Familiar with customer due diligence processes including FATCA adherence. •    Familiar with basic working capital financing cycle and products for operating entities. •    Familiar with basic mortgage financing principles including TDSR framework and applications. •    Keen understanding of adherence to credit underwriting requirements. •    Knowledge of competitive environment in order to position Bank’s products appropriately. •    Keen negotiation and closing skills. Our Ideal Candidate •    Tertiary education •    3+ years of working experience in Banking Role Specific Technical Competencies •    Familiar with customer due diligence processes including FATCA adherence •    Familiar or experienced in Business Banking •    Familiar with basic working capital financing cycle and products for operating entities About Standard Chartered We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can't wait to see the talents you can bring us. Our purpose, to drive commerce and prosperity through our unique diversity, together with our brand promise, to be here for good are achieved by how we each live our valued behaviours. When you work with us, you'll see how we value difference and advocate inclusion. Together we: • Do the right thing and are assertive, challenge one another, and live with integrity, while putting the client at the heart of what we do • Never settle , continuously striving to improve and innovate, keeping things simple and learning from doing well, and not so well • Are better together , we can be ourselves, be inclusive, see more good in others, and work collectively to build for the long term What we offer In line with our Fair Pay Charter , we offer a competitive salary and benefits to support your mental, physical, financial and social wellbeing. • Core bank funding for retirement savings, medical and life insurance , with flexible and voluntary benefits available in some locations. • Time-off including annual leave, parental/maternity (20 weeks), sabbatical (12 months maximum) and volunteering leave (3 days), along with minimum global standards for annual and public holiday, which is combined to 30 days minimum. • Flexible working options based around home and office locations, with flexible working patterns. • Proactive wellbeing support through Unmind, a market-leading digital wellbeing platform, development courses for resilience and other human skills, global Employee Assistance Programme, sick leave, mental health first-aiders and all sorts of self-help toolkits • A continuous learning culture to support your growth, with opportunities to reskill and upskill and access to physical, virtual and digital learning. • Being part of an inclusive and values driven organisation , one that embraces and celebrates our unique diversity, across our teams, business functions and geographies - everyone feels respected and can realise their full potential. Recruitment Assessments Some of our roles use assessments to help us understand how suitable you are for the role you've applied to. If you are invited to take an assessment, this is great news. It means your application has progressed to an important stage of our recruitment process. Visit our careers website www.sc.com/careers

title: Head of Marketing, Singapore
description: IG Singapore is looking for a Head of Marketing, becoming a permanent member of our team. Working in the heart of the CBD on Raffles Place, you will be joining a team of passionate individuals seeking to grow our Singapore business and provide exceptional client service to our clients. So, who are we? Hello, we’re IG Group. No, not Instagram – though we're a pretty big deal ourselves. We’re a global, FTSE 250-listed company made up of a collection of progressive fintech brands in the world of online trading and investing. The best part? We’ve snapped up many awards for our top-class platforms, forward-thinking products, and incredible employee experiences. We believe in financial freedom. And our purpose is to help ambitious people achieve it. Around 400,000 people use our groundbreaking technology and forward-thinking platforms to trade the financial products they know and love. Did we mention we have offices in 18 countries? Yes, when you join IG Group, you'll have the chance to work across multiple brands, with people all over the world. Join us for an exciting future and let’s innovate together! Your role in the team We are seeking a strategic and results-driven Head of Marketing to lead our marketing efforts. Reporting directly to the CEO, this role will oversee a team and be responsible for driving the demanding growth of our new brokerage product while continuing to scale the existing CFD business. This is a key leadership position that requires both strategic vision and hands-on execution. You will own all marketing channels—digital, affiliates, social, PR, partnerships, and offline—and partner closely with sales, client servicing, and product teams to deliver measurable growth. The role is critical in shaping our regional brand presence, client acquisition, and engagement strategies, while ensuring marketing initiatives are compliant, data-driven, and aligned with business priorities. What you’ll do Lead and oversee all marketing channels, ensuring strategies are tailored to local market dynamics while aligned with global brand positioning. Manage the budget for markets and being responsible for cost effectiveness, resource allocation and measurable ROI. Drive the demanding growth of IG’s new brokerage product, while continuing to scale the existing CFD business. Partner closely with regional teams, performance team, sales, client servicing,  and product teams to ensure marketing initiatives directly support business objectives and client needs. Develop and execute integrated marketing strategies to drive awareness, acquisition, engagement, and retention across digital, performance, social, partnerships, PR, and offline channels. Manage agencies on record for communications, creative development and media buys. Continuously  track market trends and competitor activity to adapt strategies, seize  opportunities, and mitigate risks. Oversee  and inspire a team of marketers, building a high-performance, innovative, and collaborative culture. Uphold  regulatory and brand compliance standards across all campaigns and communications. Who we’re looking for You’re curious about things like the client experience, the rapid developments in tech, and the complex world of fintech regulation. You’re also a confident, creative thinker with a knack for innovating. We know that you know every problem has a solution. Here, you can try new ideas, and lead the way in creating inspiring experiences for our clients and everyone around you. We don’t fit the corporate stereotype. If you want to work for a traditional, suit-and-tie corporate that just gives you a pay cheque at the end of the month, we might not be for you. But, if you have that IG Group energy and you can stand behind what we believe in, let’s raise the bar together. What you’ll need for this role Minimum 10 years in tech or finance marketing, across traditional, social and digital channels. Minimum 3 years of equivalent leadership experience managing a team and a budget of US>$10M. Degree in marketing, business or finance. Strong understanding of investment & trading products (CFDs, FX, Crypto, Stocks) as well as an interest in the financial markets. Familiar with Gen AI tools and CRM systems such as Braze & Salesforce. Strategic mindset with a bias for action. Strong analytical skills, with a demonstrated ability to optimise and pivot on marketing plans for maximum effectiveness. Regional experience in Malaysia, HongKong, Taiwan, Thailand Fluency in English is a requirement, proficiency in Mandarin or Bahasa is a significant advantage to engage customers from Chinese/Bahasa native countries. How you’ll grow When you join IG Group, we want you to have more than a job – we want you to have a career. And you can. If you spot an opportunity, we want you to chase it. Stretch yourself, challenge your self-beliefs and go for the things you dream of. With internal and external learning opportunities, and the tools to help you skyrocket to success, we’ll support you all the way. And these opportunities truly are endless because we have some bold targets. We plan to expand our global presence, increase revenue growth, and ultimately deliver the world’s best trading experience. We’d love to have you along for the ride. The perks It really is more than a job. We’ll recognise your talent and make sure that you can still have a life – at work, and outside of it. Networks, committees, awards, sports and social clubs, mentorships, volunteering opportunities, extra time off… the list goes on. Performance-related bonuses Insurance and medical plans Career-focused technical and leadership training Two volunteering days per year One day of birthday leave per year Contribution to gym memberships and more Flexible working hours and work-from–home opportunities Where you’ll work We follow a hybrid working model; we reckon it’s the best of both worlds. This model also feeds into our secret ingredients for innovation: diversity, flexibility, and close connection. Plus, you’ll be welcomed into a diverse and inclusive workforce with a lot of creative energy. Ask our employees what their favourite thing is about working at IG, and you’ll hear an echo of ‘our culture’! That’s because you can come to work as your authentic self. The things that make you, you – like your ethnicity, sexual orientation, faith, age, gender identity/expression or physical capacity – can bring a fresh perspective or new skill to our business. That’s why we welcome people from various walks of life; and anyone who wants to help us realise our vision and strategy. So, if you’re keen to connect with our values, and lead the charge on innovation, you know what to do. Apply now

title: MARKETING MANAGER
description: E-commerce Strategy Development : Develop and implement a comprehensive e-commerce strategy, including business goals, marketing plans, and sales targets. Strengthen the Online Presence : Expand the business to e-commerce field and build brand awareness on multiple channels such as Shopee, Lazada, TikTok, Instagram and other marketplaces. Product Management : Manage the online product catalogue, including product listings, descriptions, pricing, and inventory levels. Ensure accurate and up-to-date product information. Customer Experience : Enhance the online shopping experience to drive customer satisfaction and retention. This may involve improving website navigation, optimising checkout processes, and providing excellent customer support. Digital Marketing : Develop and execute digital marketing strategies, including social media marketing, and content marketing to drive traffic and sales. Traditional Marketing : Use offline media such as flyers, mail advertisements or other print ads to reach a broader audience. Data Analysis : Use web analytics tools to track website performance, sales, and customer behaviour. Analyse data to make informed decisions and adjustments to the e-commerce strategy. Inventory Management : Monitor and manage inventory levels, ensuring that products are in stock and available for customers. Implement inventory control strategies to reduce overstock and understock situations. Supply Chain and Fulfillment : Coordinate with suppliers, retail staff, warehouse staff and logistics partners to ensure smooth order processing, shipping, and delivery. Customer Service : Oversee customer service and support functions, addressing customer inquiries, issues, and returns promptly and professionally. Assist in retail operations to enrich customer online experience. Budget Management : Develop and manage the e-commerce budget, including marketing expenditures, website maintenance, and other operational costs. Competitor Analysis : Keep an eye on the e-commerce landscape, including competitor activities, pricing, and industry trends, and adjust strategies accordingly. Content Management : Create and manage product descriptions, images, and other content that can engage customers and drive sales. E-commerce Technology : Stay updated on e-commerce technologies and trends, and implement new tools and systems that can improve the efficiency and effectiveness of the e-commerce operation. Legal and Regulatory Compliance : Ensure compliance with e-commerce laws and regulations, including data protection, privacy, and consumer rights.

title: Outsource Supervisor
description: Duties and Responsibilities: - Lead team of Outsource Assistants and Seniors - Provide guidance to team members - Book-keeping and preparation of management accounts - Preparation of Statutory report (Financial Statements and Notes to the accounts) - Preparation of custom reporting packages based on client requirements - Where clients are audited, provide support with respect to services provided to the client - Cash management and payment processing - GST preparation and filing - Payroll processing · Preparation of payroll details (including calculation of OT, allowances etc.) · Payroll disbursement · Leave management · Preparation of IR8A and IR21 - Handle portfolio of clients, including assignment of jobs to team members - Responsible for billings and debtor management for portfolio - Review work by Assistants and Seniors - Managing team workload to meet client deadlines · Where possible or required, this would include negotiation with the client on deadlines and corresponding fee adjustments - Propose resolution steps for issues that may arise with clients - Report to director on the following · Issues that arise with clients · Anticipated issues that may arise (eg Deadline not able to be met) · Time spent by team on jobs Requirements: - A degree in accountancy and/or equivalent professional qualification - At least 4 years of relevant experience from a professional services firm in an Business Process Outsourcing (Client Accounting) environment - Proficient with accounting software - Meticulous with good accuracy and attention to detail - Good communication and interpersonal skills - Good knowledge and understanding of Singapore requirements on accounting, GST and payroll

title: Accountant
description: Key Responsibilities 1. Daily Accounting & Bookkeeping Manage the full set of accounts independently. Record and review all accounting entries, including revenue, expenses, and AR/AP. Perform monthly bank reconciliations . Manage petty cash and monitor daily cash flow. 2. Accounts Receivable (AR) & Accounts Payable (AP) Issue invoices and monitor collection status/aging reports. Process vendor payments and manage payment schedules. Maintain accurate and up-to-date ledgers for customers and suppliers. 3. Financial Reporting & Management Analysis Prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements (P&L, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow). Provide financial analysis and management reports as required by leadership. Assist in annual budgeting and perform variance analysis (budget vs. actual). 4. Tax & Regulatory Compliance Handle all tax filings (e.g., VAT/GST, withholding tax, and corporate income tax). Ensure all accounting practices comply with local accounting standards and tax laws. Coordinate with external auditors, accountants, and tax authorities. 5. Payroll & HR Administration Calculate monthly salaries, bonuses, and overtime pay. Manage statutory contributions (e.g., social insurance, health insurance, and pension funds). Prepare payroll-related reports and filing documents. 6. Internal Control & Administrative Support Establish and maintain basic internal control procedures. Organize and archive financial documents, contracts, and sensitive records. Provide general administrative support and handle ad-hoc tasks as assigned. Qualifications Education: Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field. Experience: Proven experience in managing full set of accounts is highly preferred. Technical Skills: Proficiency in accounting software (e.g., QuickBooks, Xero, SAP) and Advanced Microsoft Excel . Soft Skills: * Ability to work independently with strong time-management skills. High level of integrity, attention to detail, and a strong sense of confidentiality. Problem-solving mindset and the ability to meet tight deadlines.

title: Head of Accounts – Regional Bank – Singapore
description: A regional bank is hiring a Head of Accounts to lead its Finance team in Singapore. This role oversees the finance function and covers the full spectrum of financial reporting, regulatory reporting, and financial management, playing a key role in strengthening financial governance and internal controls. Your responsibilities will include: Lead and manage the finance team covering regulatory reporting, financial reporting, MAS reporting, GST, and FP&A. Oversee month-end, quarter-end, and year-end financial close processes, ensuring accuracy, timeliness, and compliance with accounting standards and regulatory requirements. Act as the key finance lead for MAS reporting, statutory filings, audits, and regulatory submissions, ensuring strong governance and regulatory compliance. Provide financial oversight, analysis, and insights to support senior management decision-making, budgeting, forecasting, and business planning. Drive process improvements, strengthen internal controls, and develop the finance team through coaching and leadership. Requirements: Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related discipline; professional qualifications (CA / CPA / ACCA) are preferred. Strong experience leading finance teams, ideally at VP level or above, within a banking or regulated financial services environment. Hands-on exposure across financial reporting, regulatory reporting, GST, and FP&A. Proven people management and leadership capabilities. Strong communication and stakeholder management skills. Candidates who meet the above requirements are welcome to submit their updated CV. Claire Ang (R22109899) EA License: 22S1193

title: Head, GM Client & Sales Operations
description: Job Summary The Head of Global Markets Client and Sales Operations is a senior leadership role responsible for the end-to-end operational support of Global Markets client coverage and sales activities. This role oversees Markets Client Implementation, Client Services, Channel Management, and Sales Support, ensuring a seamless, scalable, and compliant client experience across all regions and asset classes. The role partners closely with Global Markets Sales, COO, Coverage, Product, Compliance, Risk, Technology, and Operations to enable revenue growth while maintaining strong controls, regulatory compliance, and operational excellence. What Success Looks Like •    Seamless, compliant, and scalable client onboarding and servicing model. •    Improved client experience and Sales productivity. •    Strong regulatory outcomes and effective risk management. •    High-performing, engaged global teams supporting Global Markets growth. RESPONSIBILITIES •    Fostering and maintaining dialogue across the SCB global on-boarding network to develop and enhance global client relationships and responding to client needs. •    Develop and implement a forward-looking transformation strategy for Markets client and sales support, focusing on scalability, efficiency, and enhanced client experience. •    Identify key opportunities for process redesign, automation, and digitalization to improve operational outcomes. Markets Client Implementation •    Oversee end-to-end Markets client implementation, including product provisioning, documentation and regulatory requirements. •    Ensure timely, risk-appropriate onboarding aligned with revenue priorities and client experience expectations. •    Partner closely with Compliance, Legal, Risk, and Front Office to manage onboarding complexity and regulatory change. •    Continuously enhance onboarding processes through automation, digitization, and data quality improvements. Markets Operational Client Relationship Management •    Lead global Markets Client Services teams responsible for post-onboarding client support across products and regions. •    Ensure effective resolution of client inquiries related to account setup, trading enablement, static data, and service issues. •    Establish service standards, SLAs, and performance metrics aligned with client and business expectations. •    Drive a client-centric culture while maintaining strong controls and risk awareness. Markets Channel Management •    Own operational oversight of client channels, including electronic platforms, portals, and communication tools. •    Ensure effective enablement and support of digital and electronic trading channels in partnership with Technology and Product teams. •    Oversee client access entitlements, permissions, and controls across channels. •    Support the rollout of new channels and enhancements with appropriate operational readiness and governance. Markets Sales Support •    Provide operational support to Sales and Coverage teams, including data, reporting, client analytics, and enablement tools. •    Ensure effective coordination of client requests, product onboarding, and issue resolution across internal teams. •    Support Sales productivity initiatives through process simplification, automation, and improved information flow. Processes •    Ensure alignment between global standards and local execution, managing regulatory divergence where required •    Ensure consistent, efficient, and scalable operational processes across regions while allowing for local market requirements. •    Drive continuous improvement, automation, and digital transformation initiatives across trading operations People & Talent •    Lead, develop, and retain a global, multi-regional team. •    Build strong leadership capability and succession planning across the organization. •    Foster a culture of accountability, continuous improvement, and client focus. •    Champion diversity, equity, and inclusion. Risk Management •    Own the control environment for Client and Sales Operations, including operational risk, conduct risk, and regulatory compliance. •    Ensure adherence to global policies, local regulations, and internal governance standards. •    Lead responses to audits, regulatory exams, and control reviews related to client and sales operations. •    Establish robust governance forums, metrics, and management reporting. Regulatory & Business Conduct •    Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct. •    Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct. •    Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters. Key Stakeholders •    Act as a key partner to Global Markets COO and front office leadership, providing operational insight and enabling business growth. •    Collaborate closely with Technology on platform stability, change delivery, and strategic investment priorities. •    Work with Finance, Treasury, Risk, Compliance, and Legal to ensure integrated decision-making and execution. •    Represent Trading Operations at senior management committees and forums. Our Ideal Candidate •	Education - Bachelor’s degree in finance, business, economics, or a related discipline required. •	Advanced degree (MBA or equivalent) preferred. •	15+ years of relevant experience in leading Global Markets Client and sales operations. Role Specific Technical Competencies •    Strong financial acumen and analytical capability. •    Expertise in performance measurement, management information, and data analytics. •    Ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights. •    Experience supporting large-scale operational transformation •    Demonstrated ability to engage effectively with senior management and regulators. •    Strong judgment and ability to provide constructive, credible challenge. About Standard Chartered We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. title: Credit Risk Manager (Retail, Credit Cards)
description: Responsibilities: Own and continuously enhance retail credit risk frameworks for credit card underwriting and portfolio management, ensuring alignment with regulatory standards and internal risk appetite. Lead the drafting, review, and implementation of credit card–specific credit policies, underwriting criteria, and risk controls; assess policy effectiveness and recommend enhancements. Partner closely with risk modelling and analytics teams to drive the development, validation, implementation, and ongoing monitoring of credit risk models (e.g. scorecards, Basel, ECL), including review of model outputs and assumptions. Conduct in-depth credit card portfolio analytics, covering approval trends, risk grade migration, delinquency and loss performance, vintage analysis, early warning indicators, and concentration risks. Manage risk appetite and limit frameworks for credit card portfolios, including limit calibration, stress testing analysis, breach monitoring, and escalation. Provide risk input and challenge to business, underwriting, and product teams on credit card strategy reviews, policy changes, new product initiatives, and portfolio optimisation proposals. Produce and review recurring and ad-hoc risk reports for senior management, synthesising complex portfolio data into clear risk insights and actionable recommendations. Prepare and present materials for governance forums, committees, and internal stakeholders, highlighting portfolio performance, underwriting outcomes, key risk metrics, and emerging risk themes. Support junior team members through guidance and review of analytical work, contributing to team capability building and knowledge sharing. Drive process improvements and automation initiatives to enhance efficiency, data quality, and scalability of credit risk monitoring and reporting. Requirements: Bachelor’s degree or above in a quantitative discipline (e.g. Statistics, Mathematics, Financial Engineering, Quantitative Finance, Data Analytics) from a reputable institution. Minimum 3 years of relevant working full-time experience in retail credit products, such as credit cards and unsecured personal loans, or other financial risk domains. Proficiency in data and analytical tools such as SQL is a plus. Experience with additional analytics or BI tools is an advantage. Familiarity with credit card–relevant credit risk concepts, including scorecards, Basel, ECL, and stress testing. Demonstrated ability to independently conduct complex analyses and translate results into clear risk recommendations for decision-makers. Strong stakeholder management skills, with the ability to provide constructive risk challenge to business and product partners. Detail-oriented, well-organised, and able to operate effectively in a fast-paced, structured environment. Strong written and verbal communication skills. Note: Kindly note that you can only be considered for one role at a time with any of the companies within our group. If you have applied for other jobs with the group, you will be considered for roles in the order of your application

description: SME, P2P Transformation Procurement SME (contractor) Role purpose Provide director-level subject matter expertise to design and operationalise catalogue content, taxonomy, and guided buying capabilities within SAP Ariba, enabling scalable deployment across markets. The role is accountable for ensuring the buying experience is intuitive, compliant, and measurable - driving adoption of predefined content and increasing spend through preferred suppliers and contracts while improving end-to-end traceability. Scope and responsibilities 1) Content strategy, catalogue structure and governance Define and maintain the catalogue content strategy for the market rollout (starting Singapore), aligned to global design principles. Establish and enforce content standards (naming conventions, mandatory attributes, templates, quality thresholds, reject criteria). Define and run content governance: decision rights, change control, approvals, and escalation pathways (global vs local deltas). title: Audit Manager
description: The Audit Team Our audit team supports businesses in this important compliance measure that builds transparency and a complete picture of your financial position and regulatory obligations. Using a single audit approach and methodology across all Grant Thornton firms, our team brings consistent, high quality audit services to companies across the globe. The audit team’s flat hierarchy and culture of teamwork brings about an openness where team members of any level can raise their ideas and concerns to leaders and career coaches. As our clients are companies that span across a vast range of industries, our team members gain valuable exposure to industries that they are interested in. In addition to career coaching, the firm encourages our people to learn continually and pursue their professional qualifications. Through sponsored tuition fees, study leave, and the support of colleagues, our people are able to take the next step in a fulfilling career. Responsibilities Collaborating with audit team members and members from other departments for complex/specific accounting and auditing matters Facilitating cross-border engagements and group audits with group/component audit teams and/or Grant Thornton network teams, specifically focusing on the India corridor Managing and delivery of assurance engagements according to Grant Thornton audit methodology for multinational organisations of various sizes Identifying and developing new business growth opportunities with existing clients Training and coaching audit team members by conducting timely performance review and providing feedback to them Qualifications and skills At least 6 years of experience in statutory audits Experience with variety of audit related projects, such as internal audit, compliance, and/or corporate finance experience, is a plus Experience leading international/cross-border engagements in current role Progressive audit experience in Big 4 or mid-tier accounting firm in India would be an advantage Required degree in Accounting or Finance and completed qualification for one of the following: Singapore CA, ACA, ACCA, CA Australia, CPA Australia, ICAEW, US CPA, or equivalent Strong technical accounting skills with proficiency in SFRS and SSA or IFRS and ISA. US GAAP and/or SOX 404 knowledge will be advantageous

title: Periodic Review Compliance Officer (12 months)/Private banking Experience/$7,000/ KYC
description: Job responsibilities Build strong working relationships with key teams, including Front Office, Client Documentation Services (CDS), KYC/AML Onboarding, AML Investigations, and Risk Management. Support the Head of AML/KYC and Head of Periodic Review in managing compliance reviews for existing client relationships and addressing new or incremental KYC/CDD risks. Guide Relationship Managers across Singapore and overseas offices (Hong Kong, Thailand, Dubai) on periodic review submissions, including KYC updates, account structure changes, and adverse news handling. Assist with ad-hoc KYC/CDD matters and respond to queries from regulators, auditors, and Head Office. Review client profiles during periodic reviews to ensure compliance with internal standards and regulatory requirements for AML/CFT/CDD. Verify completeness and accuracy of KYC information, adequacy of source of wealth narratives, and plausibility assessments. Conduct client risk assessments and due diligence for high-risk profiles. Perform name screening (Google, World-Check, Factiva) and transaction reviews to ensure activities align with client risk profiles; investigate suspicious transactions. Participate in projects and process improvement initiatives related to KYC/CDD. Uphold values of integrity, diligence, fairness, and compliance with laws and regulations. Ensure proper identification, assessment, and documentation of KYC/CDD issues. Escalate unresolved KYC/CDD measures or suspicious activities promptly. Skills and Qualifications Minimal 5 years of experience in KYC onboarding, periodic review , or AML roles within private banking or wealth management. Fluent in English; proficiency in other Asian languages is an advantage. Solid understanding of private banking operations and MAS 626 guidelines. Experience handling AML/CFT regulatory inspections, audits, and strategic projects. Proficient in MS Word, Excel, and PowerPoint. title: Key Account Manager, Food and Nutrition
description: Position Overview We are seeking a proactive and dedicated Account Manager/ Key Account Manager with experience in the specialty food ingredients industry to join our dynamic team. This role combines managing and growing existing customer accounts with a strong focus on acquiring new business opportunities . You will play a crucial role in nurturing relationships, ensuring customer satisfaction, and driving revenue growth by identifying new customers and expanding the company’s market share. This position is ideal for a results-driven professional who thrives in both relationship management and business development. If you excel at retaining and growing existing accounts while actively seeking and converting new prospects, we’d love to hear from you. Key Responsibilities Account Management & Relationship Building: Cultivate strong relationships with existing principals, suppliers, key accounts, ensuring their ongoing satisfaction and identifying additional growth opportunities. Act as the primary contact for a portfolio of existing accounts, fostering long-term relationships to ensure customer satisfaction and retention. Regularly engage with clients to understand their business needs and proactively offer solutions. New Customer Acquisition: Actively identify, prospect, and pursue new customer opportunities within the target market. Develop and execute strategies to expand the customer base, driving growth and establishing a strong pipeline of potential clients. Incremental Growth & Cross-Selling: Identify opportunities for incremental growth within existing accounts by promoting relevant products and services. Collaborate with the sales team to maximize revenue through upselling and cross-selling initiatives. Customer Support & Problem Resolution: Provide ongoing support to clients, addressing inquiries, troubleshooting issues, and ensuring their needs are met. Coordinate with internal teams to deliver a seamless customer experience and resolve challenges efficiently. Market Awareness & Feedback Gathering: Stay informed about industry trends and competitive activities that impact customer needs or expectations. Collect feedback from clients and prospects to refine product offerings and service approaches, ensuring alignment with market demand. Reporting & Performance Tracking: Track and report on account performance metrics, including customer satisfaction, account growth, and new customer acquisition progress. Prepare regular updates for internal stakeholders on account status, sales pipelines, and emerging opportunities Position Requirements & Key Abilities Education: Bachelor’s degree in Food Technology/ Food Science or related field. Experience: Proven experience in account management and business development within the Specialty Food industry with at least 5-8 years in a customer-facing role. Business Development Skills: Strong ability to acquire new customers, including prospecting, leadgeneration, and closing sales. Relationship Management: Demonstrated ability to buildand maintain long-term client relationships. Sales Skills: Consultative selling approach with experience in both upselling to existingclients and developing new accounts. Communication: Exceptional interpersonal, verbal, and written communication skills. Analytical Ability: Ability to assess client needs and translate them into actionable strategies. Customer Focus: Commitment to customer success and satisfaction. Tools: Proficiency in MS Office and CRM software. Key Personal Attributes Customer-Focused: A commitment to meeting and exceeding clientexpectations and building long-term loyalty. Problem-Solver: Strong problem-solving skills with the ability toidentify and address customer challenges effectively. Collaborative: Works effectively within a team, collaborating acrossdepartments to meet customer needs. Detail-Oriented: High attention to detail in managing client accounts,ensuring accurate and timely delivery of solutions. Integrity & Trustworthiness: Demonstrates reliability and builds trust withclients through consistent actions and ethical decision-making. Please note that this is a general description of the role and its requirements. The duties andresponsibilities within this role may change over the course of employment at the employer’s discretion. About Caldic: At Caldic, we’re a global leader in distribution solutions, serving diverse markets across the world. Joining us means becoming part of an entrepreneurial, collaborative, and innovation-driven team. We offer: Global opportunities with rapid career growth and development An inclusive, diverse, and dynamic work environment The chance to shape the future of specialty ingredient solutions across industries like food, pharma, personal care, and more We operate in 43 countries around the world. Our innovative solutions are tailored to meet the needs of industries such as food, pharma, personal care, and industrial markets, ensuring sustainable success for all stakeholders. Engage in work that matters and drives true business impact for the this very important client. What You'll Do at Pega: • Develop a trusted advisor relationship with multiple stakeholders across the Client Organisation • Leverage industry and product knowledge to share insights about how a client can maximize license to drive business benefits • Penetrate new organizations/accounts and radiate new business in Malaysia focusing on Public Sector vertical • Provide insight to the customer as the basis of the commercial conversation, teaching customers something new/valuable about how to compete in their market • Continuously build and maintain a pipeline of sales activity through prospecting and pipelining using a variety of methods • Work with Pega’s partners to generate Pega value propositions Who You Are: A motivated enterprise software sales executive, driven to add value to your clients and their business process automation strategies. title: Senior / Revenue Analyst - Frasers House, a Luxury Collection Hotel, Singapore
description: JOB SUMMARY The Senior / Revenue Analyst role supports the development and execution of the hotel’s revenue strategy through strong system expertise, pricing analysis, forecasting, and performance reporting. Working closely with the Director of Revenue, this role ensures effective use of revenue systems, maintains pricing and inventory integrity, and provides data-driven insights to optimize total hotel revenue. CANDIDATE PROFILE Education and Experience A degree or diploma from an accredited university in Business Administration, Finance and Accounting, Economics, Hotel and Restaurant Management, or related subject OR 2 years experience in the revenue management, sales and marketing, or related professional area. CORE WORK ACTIVITIES Revenue Strategy Execution & Pricing Support Support the execution of revenue and pricing strategies aligned with hotel objectives. Monitor market conditions, competitor pricing, demand trends, and booking pace to recommend tactical pricing and sell strategy adjustments. Assist in evaluating business mix, displacement, and profitability by segment to support rate setting, negotiations, and longer-term planning. Support the inclusion of non-room revenues (meetings, events, and F&B) into total revenue considerations. Forecasting, Analysis & Performance Reporting Prepare and maintain accurate short-term, long-range, and budget forecasts for rooms and function space. Analyze revenue, demand, occupancy, and performance trends to identify risks and opportunities. Compile and validate data from internal and external sources to support revenue and profit analysis. Prepare reports and insights for revenue meetings and leadership reviews. Revenue Systems, Inventory & Distribution Manage the daily use and integrity of revenue management and reservation systems. Implement approved pricing, inventory controls, restrictions, and sell strategies across all distribution channels. Maintain accurate system data including forecasts, rates, room types, and group inputs. Monitor channel performance and ensure rate integrity and compliance with brand standards. Provide technical revenue management support to Front Office and Reservations teams. Revenue Meetings & Stakeholder Support Support sales strategy meetings through preparation of analysis and documentation. Communicate revenue updates, changes, and performance insights to relevant stakeholders as required. Compliance & Professional Standards Support compliance with brand standards, pricing rules, and revenue governance. At Marriott International, we are dedicated to being an equal opportunity employer, welcoming all and providing access to opportunity. We actively foster an environment where the unique backgrounds of our associates are valued and celebrated. Our greatest strength lies in the rich blend of culture, talent, and experiences of our associates. We are committed to non-discrimination on any protected basis, including disability, veteran status, or other basis protected by applicable law.

title: Assistant Project Cost Analyst
description:
Objective To support project teams by monitoring costs at the pre-commitment and post-commitment stages, ensuring expenditures are aligned with approved budgets, identifying cost variances early, and supporting effective cost control and reporting. Key Responsibilities Cost Monitoring & Budget Control Review Purchase Requisitions (PRs) and Purchase Orders (POs) against approved project budgets and cost codes prior to commitment. Check that proposed costs are within approved budget limits and
highlight potential overruns or abnormal pricing. Monitor committed costs versus budget, including tracking PRs, POs, and variations. Identify cost trends, repeated cost increases, or unusual pricing
compared to historical data or previous hull/projects. Support price comparison by reviewing quotations,
technical/commercial evaluations, and cost breakdowns submitted by Procurement team. Assist in verifying cost reasonableness for change orders, repeat orders, or scope variations. Work with procurement, project and commercial teams to obtain justifications for cost increases or budget deviations. Support reconciliation of committed cost versus actual cost where
required. Prepare cost summaries, variance reports, and simple dashboards and potential cost impacts. Ensure cost-related documentation (approved budgets, justifications, approvals) is properly filed and traceable. Required
Skills & Competencies Understand project cost control, budgeting, and procurement processes. Strong analytical skills with attention to detail in cost
comparison and variance analysis. Ability to work with numbers, spreadsheets, and cost data
accurately. Good communication skills to coordinate with project, procurement,
and finance teams. Familiarity with ERP systems (e.g. Workday, SAP, Navision) and
Excel is essential. Organised, systematic, and able to manage multiple cost items and
projects concurrently.

Coordinate with external vendors, performers, and service providers for events, entertainment programs, and outlet activities. 5. Prepare business proposals, concept briefs, and presentation materials for management review. 6. Monitor outlet operations from a business development perspective to identify areas for improvement or expansion. 7. Assist in planning outlet expansion, renovation concepts, or new entertainment offerings. 8. Support the Director in managing partnerships with alcohol suppliers, food partners, and entertainment providers. 9. Ensure business activities align with licensing requirements, entertainment regulations, and operational guidelines. 10. Compile reports on business initiatives, industry developments, and outcomes for internal discussion. 11. Participate in management meetings to provide input on operational improvements and customer engagement strategies. Technical Skills and Competencies: 1. Strong understanding of nightlife, entertainment, and Izakaya-style business operations. 2. Ability to analyze market trends and customer behavior within hospitality and entertainment environments. 3. Competency in preparing business proposals, concept notes, and operational reports. 4. Knowledge of basic licensing, compliance, and regulatory requirements for nightlife establishments. 5. Good coordination and communication skills for working with vendors and external partners. 6. Ability to plan and organize entertainment activities and promotional initiatives. 7. Practical problem-solving skills applied to real operational challenges. 8. Proficiency in basic office software for documentation, reporting, and presentations. 9. Ability to follow management direction closely and execute tasks according to business needs. Duration of Traineeship/Attachment Offered - 6 months Mature Mid Career Individuals - $3,300 This Position is open for mid career individuals (Mature, 40 years and above). Mid Career individuals from any qualifications level can apply.

title: Membership Specialist (Membership Acquisition), Member & Corporate Relations
description: The Singapore National Employers Federation (SNEF) is the national trade union of employers, representing more than 3,900 employers and the interests of all sectors of the economy. In the tripartite system, SNEF is the body representing employers and it aims to be the trusted partner of the National Trades Union Congress representing the labour movement, and the Ministry of Manpower representing the Government. SNEF’s vision is to help organisations achieve sustainable growth that is supported by responsible employment practices. Therefore, the federation aims to strengthen industrial and labour relations through advancing tripartism and responsible employment practices. Industrial peace will, in turn, help organisations achieve business sustainability. The Membership Specialist, Membership Acquisition will play a pivotal role in expanding SNEF’s corporate membership base. This function is responsible for prospecting and acquisition of new members, driving engagement with key decision-makers, and ensuring strong conversion results aligned with performance metrics. The ideal candidate thrives in a fast-paced B2B environment, maintains a high-quality prospect pipeline, and demonstrates strong ownership of revenue and growth KPIs. This is a 2-year contract role with provision of extension yearly based on performance. Responsibilities: ·        Develop and execute recruitment strategies for the full membership recruitment lifecycle: Recruitment, Conversion, and Retention of corporate members ·        Establish and maintain a strong pipeline of qualified leads through proactive prospecting, cold outreach, referrals, and participation in relevant business events and industry engagements. ·        Conduct consultative engagement with senior stakeholders (C-suite, HR Leaders) to articulate SNEF’s value proposition and drive membership interest and acquisition. ·        Maintain accurate lead tracking, conversion updates, and follow-through, ensuring high pipeline visibility and data integrity. ·        Analyse recruitment outcomes, market feedback, and business trends to refine outreach plans and enhance membership growth performance. ·        Prepare periodic reports and insights to demonstrate progress against KPIs and recommend strategic actions for continuous improvement. ·        Take on project and task as assigned by the reporting officer. Requirements: Relevant experience in sales, business development, or client engagement (typically 2 years or more), with the ability to demonstrate applied skills. Proven history of achieving or surpassing sales targets, ideally within membership-based, subscription, or professional services environments. Highly motivated, results-driven, and resilient with the ability to operate independently while collaborating effectively within a team structure. Strong relationship-building and communication capabilities, with the confidence to engage and influence external stakeholders at senior levels. Analytical mindset with the ability to interpret data to optimise outreach execution and performance strategies. Basic Knowledge in Customer Relationship Management (CRM)

Organized and results-driven with a proactive mindset. Team player with the ability to promote cross-functional collaboration. Effective in decision-making and responsive to a fast-changing environment.

title: Manager, Business & Program Development
description:
The Manager, Business & Program Development, will provide strategic leadership to enhance the
organization’s overall growth.  This role involves spearheading business development efforts, creating and executing impactful programs, and fostering collaboration to drive revenue and achieve organizational objectives. Additionally, the Manager will lead the business development and program team in diversity and SMART MedTech, and Sustainability Initiatives in Southeast Asia to raise awareness and attract talents for the industry. This is a full-time position and reports directly to the Director, Business & Program
Development at SEMI SEA Office. THE IDEAL CANDIDATE: The ideal candidate can communicate internally and externally to meet the company’s overall target goal. The candidate is self-driven and collaborates closely with other senior leaders to drive the organization’s overall success. CORE RESPONSIBILITIES: · Increase programs relevancy and value to members – Develop new content for programs. Work closely with program committees, as they set program objectives and develop content. Assist in preparing meetings and project plans to meet program needs. Ensure quality of speakers and presentation materials. Create and develop new digital programs –increase values to members. Provide strategies for SEMI Southeast Asia – Play a vital role in contributing to strategizing for the expansion of the SEA region market, encompassing budget management and performing ROI analysis.  Take the lead in Key Account Management to ensure sustained growth and success. Manage, develop and execute the business growth and Go-to-market strategies for new service. Conduct and execute business plans for new SEMI products - services and platforms to support SEMI’s larger span of the global electronics manufacturing supply chain in areas such as semiconductor equipment
and materials, semiconductor manufacturing, automotive electronics, medical electronics, flexible and hybrid electronics, MEMS, Advanced Packaging (+ other IoT markets). Develop strategic partnerships (industrial, Public, Associations)
for new segments (horizontally and vertically). Assess and prioritize SEMI’s targeted engagement in vertical and horizontal/adjacent supply chain segments. Strategic Planning – Contribute to the development of organizational strategies to drive sustainable growth. WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE: Successful implementation of impactful programs. Work closely with senior management to align business and program
development initiatives with overall company objectives. Identify new business opportunities and partnerships to expand market
presence. CRITICAL SKILLS: Working with regional offices and partners to identify target client markets and developing outreach strategies. Ability to create and communicate a long-term vision while balancing short term goals Provide effective leadership to the business and program development teams. Demonstrate sound judgement in evaluating creative ideas and suggestions and adeptly manage the processes with effectiveness and efficiency. PREFERRED KNOWLEDGE OR PRACTICAL EXPERIENCE: Education - B.S./B.A. degree in semiconductor or related field or an equivalence in working experience. Minimum 5 years of experience in business development or related roles. Knowledge of the semiconductor industry is mandatory with good contact points in E&E ecosystem. Strong collaboration and communication skills as demonstrated by ability to work cross-functionally with internal stakeholders. Ability to collaborate effectively and articulate clear organizational vision. ABOUT SEMI SEMI is the global industry association representing the electronics manufacturing supply chain, connecting over 3,000 member companies and 1.3 million professionals worldwide. SEMI members are responsible for the innovations in materials, design, equipment, software, devices, and services that enable smarter, faster, more powerful, and more affordable electronic products. Since 1970, SEMI has built connections that have helped its members prosper, create new markets, and address common industry challenges together.

title: Partner Risk Assessment Specialist - APAC
description: As a Partner Risk Assessment Specialist, you will play a critical role in ensuring that Wise adheres to regulatory and compliance requirements. You will be responsible for onboarding and risk assessing banks and online platforms partnering with Wise. This position offers the unique opportunity to influence Wise’s mission and establish partnerships that facilitate instant, borderless money for more people. Your mission Wise Platform helps financial institutions and large enterprises leverage Wise’s infrastructure to offer their customers world class payments products through their own platforms. Wise Platform is a start-up within a scale up.  We’re a cross functional team across sales, account management, product, engineering, compliance, analytics and many other functions. We’re based across the world – from London to Singapore, Austin and beyond. Your primary mission will be to onboard banks and online platforms, assessing their compliance policies, producing comprehensive compliance memos to determine partner risk and hand-holding partners through their KYC journey. Your responsibilities Policy and Procedure Review: Conduct thorough and detailed due diligence on our banks and online platforms to assess their compliance with regulatory requirements, internal policies, and industry standards. Review partners’ documentation, including Know Your Customer (KYC) information, legal agreements, and other relevant materials to ensure accuracy and completeness. Document your findings in compliance memos that summarize potential risks. Assist in the development and improvement of the compliance onboarding process to enhance efficiency and effectiveness. Onboarding New Partners: Manage the onboarding process for banks and online platforms, ensuring seamless integration with Wise Platform. Keep abreast of regulatory developments and industry best practices to ensure that the onboarding process aligns with the latest compliance standards. Develop and maintain strong relationships with partners to facilitate smooth onboarding and address any compliance-related queries or concerns. Risk Assessment and Control Recommendations: Evaluate and recommend appropriate monitoring controls based on the inherent risks identified during the onboarding process. Apply broad industry, operational risk, and regulatory knowledge to drive onboarding excellence Cross team collaboration: Collaborate with internal stakeholders, such as Legal, Risk Management, and Business Development, to obtain necessary information and approvals for client onboarding. Act as a SME to senior stakeholders and/or other team members related to Enterprise Due Diligence processes and compliance requirements Qualifications Excellent verbal and written English skills (additional language skills are a plus) with the ability to clearly articulate your thoughts. A minimum of 2+ years of financial services industry compliance experience, preferably at a bank, trading, payments, or fintech institution Skilled in investigation and business notation, specializing in evaluating risk assessments and compliance documentation, able to make decisions based on these Proven experience onboarding large enterprises to financial platforms, effectively managing complex organisational structures and processes Strong capabilities in documenting business processes and compliance measures with clarity and precision Understanding of risk-based approaches and assessments as well as control design and implementation in Risk and Compliance Values and behavioural expectations This isn’t just a job, it’s a revolution Proactively Seeks Opportunities: Identifies areas for improvement in processes and documentation, works on solutions to enhance customer experience, and suggests fixes for recurring errors. Embraces Change and Demonstrates Adaptability: Stays updated with recent changes, readily embraces new processes, and adapts quickly to shifting priorities while maintaining quality and focus. Team Participation: Actively contributes to team discussions, collaborates to solve challenges, is receptive to feedback and able to provide feedback to peers. We get it done Overcomes Blockers: Quickly identifies roadblocks, leverages critical thinking to find solutions, and takes decisive action to prevent delays or issues from escalating. Takes Ownership: Takes full responsibility for assigned tasks, maintains up-to-date product knowledge, and continuously drives performance improvement to meet or exceed KPIs. Enables team success: Works collaboratively to ensure team goals are met. Shares knowledge and resources, ensuring everyone has the tools and information needed to succeed. Data Driven: Demonstrates an understanding of data to measure and quantify the impact of their work. Analyses performance metrics to identify areas for improvement and supports decisions with relevant and reliable data. Customer > team > ego Customer Satisfaction: Prioritises customer satisfaction over personal preferences, actively listens to customer feedback, and advocates for their needs within the organisation. Team Collaboration: Focuses on team success, recognizes contributions from all team members, and maintains humility to foster a collaborative work environment. No drama, good karma Professionalism and Interpersonal Skills: Approaches interactions with a positive and constructive attitude, is ethical and fair, maintains respect and professionalism even in challenging situations. Transparent Communication: Demonstrates great listening skills, empathy, and patience, and communicates honestly and transparently, focusing on solutions. Gives and receives timely and specific feedback to support growth and impact. Fosters a Supportive Environment: Challenges arguments, not individuals, to promote healthy discussions. Recognizes personal limits and seeks help when needed, encouraging a culture where asking for assistance is welcomed.

title: Investment Associate/Senior Investment Analyst
description: Job Title Investment Associate / Senior Investment Analyst – Luxury Hospitality Private Equity Company Overview Client is a specialist private equity firm focused on direct investments and operating platforms in the luxury and upper-upscale hospitality sector globally. They target iconic trophy assets, branded luxury resorts, ultra-luxury boutique hotels, and high-end branded residences, and luxury hospitality-related operating companies across North America, Singapore, New Zealand and select Asian gateways. Role Overview The Investment Associate will play a lead role in the sourcing, evaluation, execution, and asset management of luxury hotel and branded residence investments. Working directly with the Partners and senior team members, the Associate will take ownership of financial underwriting, market research, stakeholder presentations, and ongoing portfolio oversight. This is a high-exposure position within a lean, entrepreneurial team that offers significant carry participation and career progression. Key Responsibilities 1. Transaction Execution & Underwriting Lead the construction and ongoing maintenance of sophisticated three-statement hotel financial models (P&L, cash flow waterfalls, promote structures, multi-scenario IRR/EM analysis, hold/sell/refi scenarios). Underwrite luxury hotel acquisitions, recapitalizations, and ground-up developments with debt and preferred equity layers. Develop detailed operating assumptions (ADR, RevPAR, GOPPAR, F&B margins, management & franchise fees, FF&E reserves, PIPs, ROI on renovations). 2. Luxury Market Intelligence & Research Perform deep-dive research on global luxury travel trends, ultra-high-net-worth spending patterns, branded residence demand, and competitive supply pipelines. Analyze sub-market dynamics in gateway cities and prime resort destinations (Maldives, Aspen, Côte d’Azur, Dubai, Mykonos, St. Barths, etc.). Maintain relationships with luxury operators (Four Seasons, Aman, Rosewood, Mandarin Oriental, One&Only, Ritz-Carlton, Peninsula, etc.) and track brand expansion pipelines. 3. Deal Execution & Stakeholder Management Prepare investment committee memos, board presentations, and LP updates with institutional-grade quality and storytelling. Coordinate due diligence (legal, technical, commercial, brand/franchise, reputation & ESG risk). Negotiate term sheets, LOIs, purchase agreements, management agreements, and franchise agreements alongside external counsel. 4. Portfolio & Asset Management Actively monitor portfolio assets: monthly/quarterly performance vs. underwriting, annual budgeting, major capex/renovation oversight, and re-financing/disposition planning. Work directly with third-party operators and internal asset management teams to drive revenue enhancement and cost efficiency. Prepare investor reporting and quarterly valuation updates. 5. Network & Market Coverage Cultivate and maintain relationships with luxury hotel brokers (JLL Hotels, CBRE Hotels, HVS, Eastdil, Christie & Co), lenders, high-end operators, family offices, and sovereign wealth investors. Attend key industry events (ILTM Cannes, IHIF, ALIS, etc.). Required Qualifications & Experience Minimum 5–9 years of relevant experience with a clear track record in luxury/upscale hospitality investments. Prior roles within: – Hotel-focused private equity or real estate private equity – Investment banking (real estate/hospitality M&A or lodging coverage) – Luxury hotel operator corporate development / strategy (e.g., Four Seasons, Aman, Rosewood, Dorchester Collection) – Hospitality investment sales brokerage or advisory (JLL, CBRE, Eastdil Secured) Demonstrable experience in project management of luxury hotel transactions (acquisitions, developments, or recapitalizations) Bachelor’s degree from a top-tier university; MBA/CFA/CAIA strongly preferred. Key Skills & Attributes Mastery of Excel; ability to build institutional-quality hotel models from scratch. Exceptional PowerPoint skills and ability to distill complex analyses into clear, persuasive presentations. Deep understanding of hotel profit & loss dynamics, franchise/management agreements, brand standards, and PIP economics. Commercial intuition for luxury consumer behavior and what drives value in trophy assets. Strong presence and communication skills; comfortable presenting to CEOs, family offices, and investors. Fluency in English required; additional European or Asian languages highly advantageous. If this sounds like you and you have a genuine passion for five-star hospitality and trophy real estate, we would love to hear from you.
