Support in client facing activities such as Quarterly Business Reviews with identified clients Proactively stay updated on accounts and market changes, demonstrating a keen sense of numbers and a strong attention to details What we look for: Bachelor’s degree or equivalent with 3-5 years working experience in business/sales analytics, sales operations, Strong analytical skills – able to conduct data analysis independently and summarize results to meaningful findings for stakeholder requirements Pursuing a relevant degree in business/engineering/computing, with keen interest in commercial activities Strong analytical skills and the ability to interpret data effectively Ability to work collaboratively within a team environment Creative problem-solving skills and a proactive approach to tasks Ability to integrate and work with cross-functional teams from different background, cultures and geographies Excellent verbal and written, strong business acumen and excellent interpersonal and communication skills What we offer: Competitive salary Paid vacation/holidays/sick time Comprehensive benefits package On the job/cross training opportunities Encouraging and collaborative team environment Dedication to safety through our Zero Harm policy

title: Financial Planning & Transformation Analyst
description: We are hiring Financial Planning & Transformation Analysts as part of our ongoing efforts to build a strong transformation and analytics capability within the Accounting & Finance and Tax Departments. This role will shape and implement process improvements, drive data-backed decision-making, and support the departments in achieving long-term operational efficiency and digital enablement. Responsibilities Review and map current workflows to identify inefficiencies and opportunities for improvement. Develop and maintain SOPs and future-state process designs.Gather, analyse, and translate business requirements for new systems, tools, and automation. Support the evaluation, testing, and rollout of workflow, digital, and automation solutions. Manage project timelines, deliverables, stakeholder communication, and prepare for progress reporting. Analyse operational and financial data (budgets, forecasts, margins) to identify efficiency gaps and provide insights that inform transformation priorities. Required Skills & Experience: Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business, Information Systems, or a related field. 4–7 years of experience in financial planning, business analysis, process optimisation, or operations improvement. Strong communication, stakeholder management, and project coordination skills, with an understanding of professional services workflows being an advantage. Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret financial and operational data. Experienced in documenting processes, gathering requirements, and supporting workflow or automation tool implementation. Proficient in Excel for data analysis and reporting while proficiency with BI tools or accounting/workflow systems (e.g., Power BI, Xero ecosystem, automation platforms) is a plus.

title: KYC Analyst (Corporate Banking)
description: about the company Our client is a versatile bank that provides a comprehensive range of financial solutions, from personal banking to global corporate services. about the job Execute comprehensive Know-Your-Customer (KYC) and Customer Due Diligence (CDD) workflows, managing the end-to-end process for both new customer onboarding and periodic reviews. Operates as a critical support function within the First Line of Defense, ensuring all activities align with the bank’s internal AML/KYC Policies and Procedures and meet Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) standards. Conduct thorough risk assessments and verify the completeness of all supporting documentation and CDD information for the client base Perform detailed name screening and searches for both prospective clients and the existing customer base (including ad-hoc requirements). Maintain and update customer lists, records, and static customer data in T24 (upon access availability for the Singapore branch). Oversee the maintenance of static information for batch screening, including the systematic clearing of alerts and completeness checks. Manage KYC/CDD trigger events and ensure all follow-up actions are executed in compliance with bank policy. skills and experience required A degree or diploma in a relevant field such as Business, Banking, Finance, or Accounting, or equivalent practical experience. Proven experience in performing KYC within a banking environment, demonstrating a strong understanding of the end-to-end process. Proficiency in banking operations and internal controls, with a specific focus on anti-money laundering (AML) frameworks. Background in internal or external auditing is highly regarded and will be considered an asset to the team. To apply online please use the 'apply' function. (EA: 94C3609/R24124496)

title: AI Delivery Lead
description: JOB SUMMARY Background and Strategy Programme “Aspire” is a multi-year investment programme to transform Finance and Treasury functions to a global target model. This involves investment to replace the infrastructure, and move to a more centralised, standardised and automated business model. This will enable faster processing, easier and more frequent access to information, at lower levels of cost. Through the roll out of the strategic Aspire toolset most of the monthly Finance and Treasury processes are being standardised and centralised. This will give functions and businesses access to key financial metrics and KPIs in a more controlled and robust environment. Leveraging on the data that the sub-ledger and general ledger consume as well as other golden source data providers, this new AI delivery role will own and be accountable for end-to-end delivery of AI-powered advanced AI capabilities to enable business insights, commercial decision making and risk management. GCFO AI Delivery Lead is responsible for the following: •    Define and execute the end-to-end AI delivery roadmap as per the GCFO vision and strategy. •    Drive the functional design and delivery of the AI use cases ensuring the Aspire/GCFO first principles and data strategy is adhered. •    Drive the translation of GCFO business problems into well-defined AI use cases with measurable outcomes •    Ensure AI solutions are scalable, reusable and alignment to enterprise platforms and standards •    Manage end-to-end financials and costs, delivery governance and controls and all aspects of delivery management for AI book-of-work ensuring all quality delivery objectives are met. •    Run regular delivery huddles, governance and prepare for necessary refinement forums. •    Articulating, prioritizing and delivering key use cases for analytics. RESPONSIBILITIES Strategy •    Own and be accountable for end-to-end delivery of strategic data and analytical capabilities to enable business insights, commercial decision making and risk management. Business •    Be a fearless leader in driving the adoption and associated operational benefits of the strategic technology. •    Proactive in seeking regular assurance that the teams are performing to an acceptable risk and control standard. •    Act quickly and decisively when any risk and control weakness become apparent and ensure they are addressed within an appropriate timeframe and escalated through the relevant committees. •    Include all necessary internal, legal and regulatory controls through the development of Global Finance requirements. •    Balance business performance delivery and cost management constraints with risk and control matters to ensure that they do not materially threaten the Group’s ability to remain within risk appetite. Processes •    Collaborate and partner with Global Process Leaders as part of the Build & Maintain model •    Deliver the infrastructure and capabilities to enable target operating model and control framework •    Optimizing current business model and enabling business benefit materialization through straight-through processing People & Talent •    Lead through example and build the appropriate culture and values. Set appropriate tone and expectations from their team and work in collaboration with risk and control partners. •    Ensure the provision of ongoing training and development of people and ensure that holders of all critical functions are suitably skilled and qualified for their roles ensuring that they have effective supervision in place to mitigate any risks. •    Employ, engage, and retain high quality people, with succession planning for critical roles. •    Responsibility to review team structure/capacity plans. •    Set and monitor job descriptions and objectives for direct reports and provide feedback and rewards in line with their performance against those responsibilities and objectives. Risk Management •    Reinforce an effective and exemplary risk and control culture and further strengthen the control environment, ensure that specialist knowledge and skills are assigned to relevant needs. •    Act to minimize operational loss and audit failures and take proactive measures to respond to matters arising and identify and manage forward looking risks. •    Work with relevant teams to identify and mitigate identified operational risks in their areas. •    Through assignment of direct and indirect reports, establish and maintain an appropriate framework and procedures for monitoring, identifying, measuring, assessing, reporting and managing compliance, regulatory, financial crime, operational and reputational risks. Governance •    Embed the Group’s values and code of conduct in the Global Finance Technology teams to ensure adherence with the highest standards of ethics and compliance with relevant policies, processes and regulations among employees form part of the culture. •    Ensure compliance with all salient SCB governance frameworks where there are direct touchpoints with them or any subsequent downstream influence from the Global Finance Technology Team. Regulatory & Business Conduct •    Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct. •    Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct. •    Lead the GCFO team to achieve the outcomes set out in the Bank’s Conduct Principles •    Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters. Key Stakeholders •    GCFO COO Management & Leadership Team •    GCFO COO Business Process Owners and Leads •    Business Finance Leads •    Regional and Country Finance Leads •    T&O Finance and Functions •    GCFO Programme Management Office (PMO) Our Ideal Candidate •    Bachelor's degree in finance or related discipline •    Strong track record of delivery in transformation projects (preferably implementation of AI) •    10+ years of experience combined in Agile and traditional Project delivery methodologies. •    Strong understanding of Finance data products (GL, sub-ledger, reference data etc) •    Exposure to core Finance domains – FP&A, forecasting & planning, Cost, Treasury •    Ability to collaborate/challenge vendors & internal teams to deliver optimal, value-adding AI solutions •    Ability to work proactively, independently when necessary and displays strong initiative. •    Agile framework or PMP certifications •    Excellent communication and presentation skills •    Consistently high performer and excellent role model of the Banks core values •    High levels of energy and drive, willingness to work hard •    Pragmatic team player with attention to detail •    Hands on experience in delivering Advanced Analytics changes in Tier 1 Bank •    Experience in working in multicultural teams •    Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills •    Exposure to SAP AI ecosystem – framework, models & self-service tools •    Exposure to generative AI, NLP, forecasting, anomaly detection •    Exposure to API, knowledge-graph & AI agent driven modernization to reduce cost and time of end-to-end delivery & ops •    Understanding of AI limitations, risks and failure models •    Exposure to Responsible AI principles (bias, fairness, accountability) Role Specific Technical Competencies •    Data Science •    Security, Privacy & Responsible AI •    AI use case frame and feasibility assessment •    Data Quality, reconciliation and lineage •    Model risk management and auditability •    Agile delivery for AI & Analytics products •    SAP BI & Analytics visualisation tooling About Standard Chartered We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can't wait to see the talents you can bring us. Our purpose, to drive commerce and prosperity through our unique diversity, together with our brand promise, to be here for good are achieved by how we each live our valued behaviours. When you work with us, you'll see how we value difference and advocate inclusion. Together we: • Do the right thing and are assertive, challenge one another, and live with integrity, while putting the client at the heart of what we do • Never settle , continuously striving to improve and innovate, keeping things simple and learning from doing well, and not so well • Are better together , we can be ourselves, be inclusive, see more good in others, and work collectively to build for the long term What we offer In line with our Fair Pay Charter , we offer a competitive salary and benefits to support your mental, physical, financial and social wellbeing. • Core bank funding for retirement savings, medical and life insurance , with flexible and voluntary benefits available in some locations. • Time-off including annual leave, parental/maternity (20 weeks), sabbatical (12 months maximum) and volunteering leave (3 days), along with minimum global standards for annual and public holiday, which is combined to 30 days minimum. • Flexible working options based around home and office locations, with flexible working patterns. • Proactive wellbeing support through Unmind, a market-leading digital wellbeing platform, development courses for resilience and other human skills, global Employee Assistance Programme, sick leave, mental health first-aiders and all sorts of self-help toolkits • A continuous learning culture to support your growth, with opportunities to reskill and upskill and access to physical, virtual and digital learning. • Being part of an inclusive and values driven organisation , one that embraces and celebrates our unique diversity, across our teams, business functions and geographies - everyone feels respected and can realise their full potential. Recruitment Assessments Some of our roles use assessments to help us understand how suitable you are for the role you've applied to. If you are invited to take an assessment, this is great news. It means your application has progressed to an important stage of our recruitment process. Visit our careers website www.sc.com/careers

title: Senior Finance Analyst
description: Key Responsibilities 1. Financial Modelling & Investment Analysis Build and maintain development and acquisition financial models (DCF, IRR, NPV, equity waterfall, sensitivity analysis). Analyse GDV, construction costs, land cost, financing costs, exit values and cash-flow timing. Evaluate different ownership and investment structures (direct holding, SPV, JV, fund structures). Stress-test assumptions under various market, interest-rate and sales-velocity scenarios. 2. Singapore Property Market Analysis Conduct micro-market research by district, sub-market, and asset class (residential, industrial, commercial). Track URA data, transaction trends, price indices, rental yields, take-up rates, and supply pipelines. Assess the impact of government policies (ABSD, BSD, SSD, LTV limits, cooling measures, GLS/IGLS supply). Monitor URA Master Plan and infrastructure developments affecting asset values. 3. Deal Evaluation & Due Diligence Support feasibility studies for new developments, redevelopment and value-add opportunities. Review key due-diligence inputs: valuation reports, cost plans, sales assumptions and consultants’ reports. Assess project risks (planning, construction, sales, financing, regulatory) and propose mitigation strategies. 4. Investment Committee & Stakeholder Support Prepare investment memoranda, IC papers, and executive summaries for senior management and investors. Translate complex financial outputs into clear insights and recommendations. 5. Portfolio & Performance Monitoring Track actual vs forecast performance for live projects and stabilised assets. Analyse deviations in costs, sales velocity, pricing and cash flows. Support periodic asset revaluation and portfolio reporting. Requirements Technical & Analytical Strong proficiency in Excel financial modelling (advanced formulas, scenario analysis). Solid understanding of real estate finance metrics: IRR, NPV, DSCR, LTV, ICR, GDV. Familiarity with Singapore property regulations and tax framework. Ability to interpret market data and translate it into investment insights. Professional Background Degree in Finance, Real Estate, Economics, Accounting, or related discipline. 2–5 years of experience in real estate investment, development, advisory, valuation, or fund management. Experience covering Singapore property is strongly preferred. Soft Skills High attention to detail with strong commercial judgement. Clear written and verbal communication skills (able to brief senior stakeholders). Ability to work across multiple projects and tight timelines. Strong ethics and professionalism in handling confidential information.

title: Vice President, Corporate Functions Human Resources Business Advisor
description: Step into an exciting opportunity in Singapore as a Corporate Functions Human Resources (HR) Business Advisor. This role allows you to shape impactful HR strategies and build strong relationships with key business stakeholders. You'll be part of a dynamic team that values innovation and professional growth, offering you the chance to advance your career in a global environment. Experience the benefits of joining a leading firm that supports your development and success. Be ready to make a significant impact and grow with us. As a Vice President, HR Business Advisor within our Human Resources team, you will work closely with clients in Corporate Functions. You will provide strategic HR advisory and support, thriving in a fast-paced, demanding environment. You will execute strategic priorities set by senior leaders and collaborate with managers at various levels to drive organizational success. You will engage with senior HR Business Advisors and managers to resolve HR-related matters. Your role involves implementing HR policies, programs, and services effectively. In this role, you will own relationships with key business stakeholders, offering expert advice across a full spectrum of HR activities. You will design collaborative solutions for emerging people matters, ensuring alignment with regulatory and corporate policies. You will operate effectively within a highly matrixed organization, leveraging resources from multiple areas to deliver results. Your contributions will make a meaningful impact on our firm and the wider community. Join us and be part of a dynamic and supportive environment that values your expertise. Job Responsibilities: • Consult with business stakeholders on HR activities, including organizational design, employee relations, compensation, staffing, and talent management. • Provide creative and pragmatic advice to manage human capital issues, ensuring compliance with regulatory and corporate policies. • Coordinate talent management programs, identifying future leaders, succession plans, and diverse talent. • Offer Employee Relations advice, supporting managers on flexible working, grievances, disciplinary issues, and redundancies. • Coordinate the compensation process, ensuring data integrity and educating managers on compensation philosophy. Required Qualifications, Capabilities, and Skills: • Minimum 8 years of overall HR experience • Minimum Bachelor’s degree or equivalent. • Proven experience in HR advisory roles within a financial services environment. • Ability to navigate a global matrix organization and partner on issues across HR and the business. Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to build relationships with stakeholders. • Knowledge of HR policies, procedures, and best practices. • Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities in a dynamic environment. • Exceptional judgment, demonstrated ability to make sound decisions and be creative in developing alternative solutions in a fast-paced environment. Ability to utilize critical thinking and analytical skills regularly to identify issues and trends, develop solutions and/or recommendations to address root cause • Strong analytical, consulting, quantitative and technical skills that enable individual to leverage data to create HR strategies that support and drive business results. Project management abilities including execution skills and end to end process improvement. Technically proficient in MS Office Suite – including Excel and PowerPoint • Ability to communicate professionally and effectively, at all levels of the business; Preferred Qualifications, Capabilities, and Skills: • Strong influencing capability to promote and sell ideas to drive change; • Resilience in a constantly changing environment; • Strong generalist knowledge of human resources processes, principles, concepts and methodology in a business setting, across goal setting, performance assessment and improvement, compensation practices, talent review and talent management, workforce planning, reductions in force, manager development • Able to manage multiple projects, understand the business and execute efficiently. To apply for this position, please use the following URL: https://ars2.equest.com/?response_id=56cd1b2eea4c9360ef4f5d3edd598b7c

title: Accountant
description: Overview We are seeking a detail-oriented and reliable Accountant to join our team. The ideal candidate will be responsible for maintaining accurate financial records, managing accounts payable and receivable, preparing financial statements, ensuring compliance with relevant regulations, and liaising with external auditors and tax agents. Job Description - Record, categorize, and reconcile all financial transactions in the general ledger to ensure accuracy and completeness of financial data - Manage accounts payable and receivable, including processing invoices, ensuring timely payments to vendors, and following up on outstanding receivables - Prepare and maintain balance sheets and profit/loss statements, providing management with clear and timely reports on the company’s financial performance - Ensure compliance with internal financial policies and external regulations, maintaining up-to-date knowledge of relevant accounting standards and practices - Liaise with external auditors and tax agents, providing necessary documentation and support during audits and tax filings - Assist in the preparation of tax returns and support other financial reporting and analysis as required Requirements - Relevant qualification in Accounting, Finance, or a related field. - Proven experience as an Accountant or in a similar role. - Strong knowledge of accounting principles, financial regulations, and reporting requirements - Proficiency with accounting software (SAP) and MS Excel. - Excellent attention to detail, organizational, and analytical skills. - Strong communication skills to effectively collaborate with internal teams and external partners.

title: Sales Manager/Senior Sales Manager
description: Main roles and responsibilities for Montigo Resorts, Nongsa The appointed professional serves as a representative of the organization, showcasing the Resort's villas, products, and services in a polished and professional manner. They are responsible for driving revenue and securing bookings through direct sales and effective relationship management, ensuring all client inquiries and reservations are handled proactively to surpass guest expectations. Additionally, they oversee corporate account management in Singapore by identifying potential clients, evaluating opportunities, and promoting the Resort's Villas and Meeting Spaces. Main Responsibilities · Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure seamless event execution and guest satisfaction. · Conduct market research to identify new business opportunities and stay abreast of industry trends. · Cultivate and maintain strong relationships with corporate clients, understanding their needs and providing tailored solutions. · Develop and implement effective sales strategies to achieve corporate sales targets · Ensure all client enquiries and bookings are managed in a proactive manner that exceeds guest expectations. · Implement sales action plans related to respective market(s) and account portfolio. · Participate in weekly revenue and sales meetings, as well as account reviews with the Director of Sales & Marketing, to discuss strategies for achieving established targets. · Prepare and organize marketing proposals and activities · Prepare and present compelling sales proposals to potential clients. · Prospects and develops new business continuously through customer interaction or research. · Regularly review account portfolios, analyze performance, and present findings to Director of Sales & Marketing for action planning. · Responsible for driving revenue and securing bookings for the Resort through direct sales and relationship management. · Responsible for implementing sales activities to maximize business opportunities and revenues in their specific area of responsibility to exceed the segment goals for the Resort. · Responsible for managing a portfolio of accounts and negotiating sales contracts. · Understand factors that affect the market, inform management accordingly and develop solutions · Weekly update of sales activities and other sales reports as needed

title: Finance Executive
description: We are seeking a motivated Finance Executive to join our team and manage a range of accounting and financial functions. This role provides an excellent opportunity for individuals with 1 to 3 years of experience, including juniors who are eager to grow and develop in a dynamic environment. Key Responsibilities: Daily Tasks: Ensure proper documentation and approvals are in place. Handle data entry, including scanning and inputting invoice details (Accounts Payable). Prepare supporting documents for inter-company billing. Update Accounts Receivable (AR) receipts. Manage Accounts Payable, including payment vouchers and filing. Process contractor payments and maintain payment schedules. Oversee office supplies, inventory, and maintenance tasks. Monthly Tasks: Prepare and review cash card reports, accruals, and prepayments. Sort and record transactions from debit and credit cards. Send monthly statements of accounts to customers. Handle petty cash claims and reimbursements. Conduct monthly bank reconciliations. Submit quarterly GST reports (if applicable). Maintain detailed balance sheet schedules. Ad Hoc Duties: Manage office supplies and inventory, including ordering and approvals. Oversee office maintenance and mail sorting. Qualifications: Minimum diploma in accountancy/finance or equivalent. 1 to 3 years of experience in finance and accounting roles (junior candidates are welcome to apply). Proficiency in accounting software such as QuickBooks and Million Accounting. Strong understanding of accounting principles and practices. Excellent attention to detail and organizational skills. Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines. Effective communication and interpersonal skills.

title: Finance Manager (Marine and Offshore)
description: Responsibilities & Duties To manage the finance team in Singapore. To ensure the accounting team is properly performing all expected daily AP, AR and GL tasks, and to give them proper guidelines to fulfill their mission. To ensure an accurate and timely monthly closing (accruals, accounts matching, intercompany matching) To ensure all accounting activities are performed timely in accordance with client rules and in compliance with the regulations. To monitor P&L evolution vs budget and regularly report to the top management. To analyze P&L and Balance Sheet (variances, gaps…). To ensure supplier invoices are recorded accurately and timely. To manage all third parties’ payments and approve bank payments within bank portals and TRAX as per bank signatory’s mandates. To ensure bank reconciliations are accurate and performed timely. To provide a monthly cash forecast report. To monitor tax declaration in accordance with specific legal requirements. To monitor the external audits. To establish or update internal procedures. To ensure constructive and fruitful relationship with other departments and company’s partners (Payroll and Crewing teams in Singapore, manning agencies) in order to drive the organization’s financial performance, process improvement, and value addition to the Group company. To review the employees’ payroll of the Singapore office. To set up the department’s monthly KPIs and to be a business partner for the managing director and Ships management. Minimum Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Accounting or Finance Proficiency in MS Office applications especially in Excel Proper command in formal English, both in written and oral Minimum 3 years of experience as a Finance Manager

title: Management Associate
description: About the Role The Management Associate Program is a 24-month rotational experience designed to develop future leaders. You’ll gain exposure to multiple teams, tackle high-impact challenges, and work directly with leaders across the company. This is not your average graduate program. We’re looking for individuals who thrive in ambiguity, move fast, and are energized by big goals — because you won’t just learn from us, you’ll help shape what’s next. Now, we're inviting fresh graduates and early-career talent to embark on a growth journey with us — and help reimagine the future of rewards. Your Adventure Ahead Accelerated growth across 2–3 business functions (e.g. Product, Marketing, Business Development, Operations), giving you a well-rounded hands-on execution experience of how the company drives impact at scale. Dedicated mentorship and leader engagement to guide your development, challenge your thinking, and unlock your potential. Impactful, hands-on initiatives where you’ll own workstreams, drive alignments with key stakeholders, and see your ideas drive real results. Cross-functional collaboration globally, building regional perspective and agility in a fast-paced, diverse environment. Critical learning in business and leadership, with a focus on growth mindset, data fluency, and customer-first thinking. A self-starter mentality — you take initiative, learn fast, and never wait to be told what to do Genuine curiosity about tech, startups, digital commerce, or growth businesses Demonstrated use of AI tools (e.g. ChatGPT, Cursor) to develop enhance productivity, and drive efficiency. Team player — thrive in collaborative environments, supporting and elevating others to achieve shared goals Leadership — demonstrate ownership, accountability, and the ability to rally others towards impact Degree in any discipline from a recognized university

title: Business Development & Relationship Manager
description:
Tangspac is a leading technology services, consulting, and talent advisory firm dedicated to helping
clients across Asia address complex technology, business, and resourcing challenges. Founded since 1994 and headquartered in Singapore, Tangspac serves a diverse customer base, including global financial institutions, fintechs, energy, consulting and public sector clients. With operations and affiliate networks spanning Singapore, Hong Kong, and Malaysia, Tangspac has deep industry expertise in providing talent solutions, staff augmentation, and managed services. Job responsibilities: Develop and execute business development strategies to identify and secure new clients in the financial services, professional services and technology sectors. Build and maintain strong, long-term relationships with existing clients to ensure client satisfaction and repeat business. Act as a trusted advisor to clients, understanding their business needs and providing tailored recruitment and
talent solutions. Collaborate with internal teams to deliver high-quality recruitment services and meet client expectations. Identify market trends, competitor activities, and opportunities to expand the company's market presence. Prepare and deliver compelling presentations, proposals, and pitches to prospective clients. Negotiate contracts and agreements with clients to ensure mutually beneficial outcomes. Track and report on business development activities, client feedback, and market insights to senior management. Represent the company at industry events, conferences, and networking opportunities to enhance brand visibility and build professional networks. Job requirements: Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Marketing, or a related field. Proven experience in business development, client relationship management, or sales, preferably within the recruitment or consulting industry. Strong understanding of the financial services and technology sectors, including market trends and challenges. Excellent communication, negotiation, and presentation skills. Ability to build and maintain strong professional relationships with clients and stakeholders. Results-driven mindset with a proven track record of meeting or exceeding business development targets. Strong organizational and time management skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities effectively. Self-motivated, proactive, and able to work independently as well as part of a team.

title: SALES EXECUTIVE
description:
About the Role: In this dynamic and client-facing position, you will play a key role in driving the commercial growth of Uniq Creation Singapore. You will be responsible for developing and managing business opportunities, strengthening relationships with existing partners, and expanding our customer base within the mobile accessories industry. We’re looking for a self-driven and persuasive communicator with strong business acumen and a passion for sales. effectively. ·       Provide professional advice, understand client needs, and recommend suitable product solutions. ·       Manage and achieve sales targets set by the company. ·       Collaborate closely with the product and marketing teams to align sales strategies with brand direction. title: Commercial Proposal Engineer (Central) Basic + AWS + VB! HSW
description: Job Summary: Responsible for creating competitive proposals with accurate costing and clearly defined scopes. Works closely with the Sales team, Customers, Vendors, Finance, and Legal/Contract teams to support opportunities such as parts supply, technical assistance, turnkey projects, and upgrade projects. Requires strong financial understanding to prepare cost calculations and pricing models, as well as the ability to review and negotiate contract terms. The Assistant Manager also participates in meetings to explain technical details, manage terms and conditions deviations, and negotiate commercial matters with customers. In addition to proposal development, the Assistant Manager supports project execution, business operations, and cross-functional collaboration. Strong problem-solving skills, time management, and regular reporting to stakeholders are essential to ensure smooth and timely delivery of projects and proposals. Key Responsibilities: 50% – Commercial Proposals, Costing & Contract Support Prepare detailed cost calculations, including complex turnkey project estimates in collaboration with Finance teams. Develop and submit competitive proposals and accurate invoices. Work closely with the Legal/Contract team to review, interpret, and propose deviations to Terms & Conditions. Negotiate pricing, scope, terms, and conditions with customers and vendors. Follow up with customers to ensure timely payments. Support the preparation and execution of annual sales forecasts. Prepare tender submissions, pricing strategies, and sales strategies. Participate in the development of long-term service agreements (LTSA). 20% – Customer Engagement, Technical & Contractual Communication Prepare and deliver service presentations to prospective customers. Serve as the key contact between the company and customers for all commercial and technical clarifications. Address technical queries with support from engineering teams. Explain contract terms, commercial implications, and technical scope during meetings. title: Senior Construction Operations Manager - Founding Member, New Strategic Business Unit
description: An Exceptional Opportunity to Shape the Future of a Growing Medium-Sized Enterprise's Greenfield Construction Venture in Singapore The Company: A successful and growing medium-sized enterprise with a strong track record in related industries is strategically establishing a new construction business unit in Singapore to capitalize on significant market opportunities. This is a unique and high-impact opportunity to be a foundational member of this venture, leveraging the established reputation and agility of a thriving company while building a dynamic and focused operation from the ground up. We are seeking an exceptional and entrepreneurial Senior Construction Operations Manager to spearhead site operations, drive business development, and establish robust regulatory and HR frameworks. The Role: As the Senior Construction Operations Manager, you will be a pivotal figure in the success of this new strategic business unit. This multifaceted role demands a seasoned construction professional with a proven track record in managing complex projects, a strong business acumen for client acquisition, and a deep understanding of Singapore's regulatory landscape, particularly within the construction sector. You will be instrumental in setting the operational blueprint, securing initial projects, and building a high-performing team. Key Responsibilities: Strategic Leadership & Operational Blueprint: Collaborate with executive leadership to define the operational strategy and establish core procedures, workflows, and performance standards for the new construction business unit. Lead the identification, evaluation, and acquisition of essential operational resources, including initial equipment, technology platforms, and strategic supplier partnerships, aligned with the company's growth       strategy. Define and champion the company's approach to project execution excellence, stringent quality assurance protocols, and industry-leading safety management systems. Strategic Business Development & Key Client Acquisition: Spearhead business development initiatives by leveraging your extensive network and proactively identifying and engaging with key potential clients, including developers, architects, and other businesses. Contribute strategically to the development of compelling business development materials, presentations, and proposals that align with the company's brand and value proposition. Cultivate and maintain strong, long-term relationships with strategic clients, industry influencers, and potential partners to secure initial and future projects. Represent the company at relevant industry events, conferences, and networking forums to build brand presence and generate leads. Provide critical operational and costing expertise in the preparation of complex bids and tenders, ensuring competitive and profitable proposals. Establish robust tracking and reporting mechanisms for business development activities and  outcomes. Project Leadership & Elite Execution (Flagship Projects): Directly lead and oversee the execution of the company's initial, high-profile construction projects, ensuring delivery on time, within stringent budgetary parameters, and to high-quality standards that reflect the company's commitment to excellence. Build, mentor, and empower a team of capable project managers, supervisors, and skilled workers, fostering a culture of accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement. Serve as the primary point of operational leadership, reporting directly to the designated General Manager or Business Unit Head on all strategic and tactical matters related to site and project performance. Chair comprehensive project briefings and strategic kick-off meetings, setting clear expectations and ensuring alignment across all stakeholders. Develop and present concise, data-driven periodic progress reports for internal executive review and external client communication. Strategic Construction Planning & Resource Optimization: Provide strategic direction in establishing advanced construction planning methodologies and resource allocation strategies for the new business unit. Establish sophisticated and efficient coordination protocols between internal project teams, pre-qualified subcontractors, key clients, and external consultants, ensuring seamless project delivery aligned with master schedules. Contribute to the development of standardized, company-level cost estimation and control processes, overseeing progress claim reviews, interim certifications, and payment authorizations. Strategically forecast and secure all necessary resources (highly skilled manpower, appropriate equipment fleets, suitable machinery, and quality materials) from project inception to completion, with a strong emphasis on optimized resource utilization and adherence to the company's financial guidelines. Enterprise-Level Risk Management & Regulatory Compliance: Proactively identify and mitigate potential risks across initial projects, translating lessons learned into robust company-wide risk management frameworks and best practices. Ensure unwavering compliance with all Singaporean regulatory requirements, including stringent quality, safety, and environmental standards, establishing a culture of compliance that aligns with the company's ethical and operational standards. Oversee the submission and clearance processes with relevant Singaporean authorities, establishing efficient and compliant protocols for all future projects. Implement and enforce adherence to approved specifications and relevant industry standards,       embedding a commitment to quality assurance throughout the organization. Establish, implement, and continuously monitor a comprehensive safety management system, conducting rigorous periodic safety audits on all initial sites to set the benchmark for a safety-first culture. Strategic HR & Regulatory Framework Development: Leverage a deep understanding of Singapore's Ministry of Manpower (MOM) regulations to strategically plan and manage foreign worker quotas, work permit applications, and other critical HR practices within the construction sector, aligning with the company's HR policies. Collaborate with the company's HR function to establish the business unit's HR policies, procedures, and talent acquisition strategies, ensuring compliance with local regulations and the company's overall standards. Utilize your comprehensive knowledge of Singapore's business certification and licensing requirements to ensure the new construction firm operates with full legal compliance from its inception, working with relevant internal teams or external advisors. Lead the application and acquisition of all essential business licenses and permits, ensuring a smooth and timely setup process. Strategically plan and manage initial project staffing to optimize workforce deployment while adhering strictly to MOM regulations and quota limitations. Strategic Ad-hoc & Foundational Initiatives: Undertake strategic ad-hoc assignments directed by executive leadership, the Construction Manager, and the Business Unit Head, particularly those critical to establishing the new company's operational, business development, and regulatory frameworks in alignment with the company's overall strategy. Job Requirements: Bachelor's Degree in Construction Management, Engineering (Civil, Structural, or equivalent), or a related field from a reputable institution. Advanced degree preferred. Equivalent to 15+ years of progressive and impactful experience within the construction sector in Singapore, with significant and demonstrable success as a Senior Construction Site Manager or equivalent management role on complex, large-scale projects. Exceptional and comprehensive knowledge of best-in-class project management methodologies, tools, and technologies across all project lifecycle stages, with a strategic understanding of the unique challenges and opportunities of a new business unit within a growing enterprise. Proven and demonstrable track record of successfully soliciting new business, building strong client relationships, and contributing to significant business development wins within the Singaporean construction market. Deep and current understanding of Singapore's Ministry of Manpower (MOM) regulations concerning foreign worker quotas, work permit processes, and strategic HR management within the construction industry. Comprehensive knowledge of the necessary business certifications, licenses, and regulatory requirements for operating a construction firm in Singapore, including interactions with relevant government agencies. Mastery of MS Project and the MS Office suite, with proficiency in other relevant project management and collaboration tools. A strategic and passionate leader with exceptional people management skills, demonstrated ability to build and motivate high-performing teams in a start-up or new business unit environment, and a commitment to fostering a culture of excellence and innovation. A results-oriented and decisive individual with exceptional analytical, problem-solving, and negotiation skills, crucial for navigating the complexities of a new business and the Singaporean regulatory landscape. World-class written, communication, and interpersonal skills, essential for engaging with executive leadership, diverse stakeholders, building strategic client relationships, securing new business, and effectively interacting with regulatory bodies. Unwavering willingness to work flexibly, including extended hours as required during critical project phases, business development initiatives, and the strategic establishment of the new business unit. A proven ability to thrive under pressure, manage complex priorities, and deliver exceptional results within demanding timelines. A strategic, proactive, and highly entrepreneurial mindset with a strong personal drive to build and shape a significant new construction business unit within a growing enterprise, with a direct and measurable impact on its growth and success across operations, business development, and regulatory compliance. Working Conditions: A dynamic and challenging leadership role based in Singapore, requiring a commitment of 5 working days per week, with the understanding that significant flexibility and extended hours will be necessary, particularly during the      initial strategic phases of the business unit's establishment, critical flagship project milestones, and strategic business development activities. This is a rare and compelling opportunity to leave a lasting legacy by building a significant new construction business unit within a thriving medium-sized enterprise. If you possess the strategic vision, deep industry expertise, entrepreneurial spirit, and proven leadership capabilities to excel in this foundational role, we encourage you to submit your confidential application.

title: Markets Non-Financial Risk Oversight Lead

The role will also be responsible for reviewing and leading pan Markets existing risk and control framework, remediation activities for alignment. Roles & Responsibilities As such this role will: •    Support the holistic Markets/FSS governance framework, through understanding Market’s strategy, actions under the overall strategic plan, the flow of reporting information within Markets and understanding the delegations of authority / stakeholder landscape for the businesses. •    Lead pan Markets deliverables and initiatives, to align with group and CIB strategy. Business •    Good understanding of the Markets and FSS business, how Markets operate within Group, how it aligns with CIB strategy and the external market environment in which we operate including Regulators. •    Good understanding of Risk standards and Policy relevant to the Markets/FSS business and scope of the role. •    Awareness and understanding of the wider Markets/FSS business, in which the Group Operates. This role leads a team that provides core support to the business by providing risk management support. Processes •    Responsible for implementing processes to ensure continued adherence to Policies and Standards relevant to the Markets business. •    Responsible for improving efficiencies and timeliness of relevant processes. •    Provide guidance and analysis for FSS related matters across the Markets Process Universe and process owner support as required. People & Talent •    Responsible for continuously developing a team of strong risk managers. •    Responsible for leading through example and building the appropriate culture and values. •    Sets appropriate tone and expectations for the team and work in collaboration with risk and control partners. •    Responsible for driving training and communications to promote awareness and continuous learning for risk, control, and governance aspects. •    Responsible for ensuring the provision of ongoing training and development of people and ensure that holders of all critical functions are suitably skilled and qualified for their roles ensuring that they have effective supervision in place to mitigate any risks. Risk Management •    Oversee the non-financial risks which can impact the business. Ability to connect the themes and issues across products, functions and locations. •    Monitor control performance, behaviour, trends and internal/external events to proactively identify non-financial risks. •    Perform reviews and assessments of risk metrics (losses, near miss incidents, control effectiveness) to identify top themes and trends. •    Responsible for driving cross-business risk initiatives in support of Target Operating Model. •    Contributes to defining annual priorities for periodic review process as a form of First Line Assurance (i.e. “peer or targeted reviews”). •    Identify opportunities for rationalisation of controls and streamlining of risk administration processes, e.g. Risk Self Assessments (RCSA), (i.e. “control simplification”). •    Responsible for risk tooling and associated processes working in partnership with Group OR and vendors (e.g. Incident Management, KRI Dashboards, etc.) •    Oversee risk oversight documentation (top risk profiles, risk committee materials) and monitoring day-to-day risk treatment progress (residual risk assessments, risk treatment plans, management action plans). Governance •    Promote the development of risk-oriented culture within the organization. •    Accountable for the effective running of all Markets governance committees including FSS Non-Financial Risk Committees •    Drive standards and consistency in 1-down regional/country/function forums relevant to role and providing consistent escalations to 1-up forums/committees. •    Representative at required other unspecified Markets Governance forums and Committees as required to provide input relevant to role. •    Ensure Bank policies and standards are fit for purpose for FSS seeking changes and deviation where required. •    Create, set and evaluate governance standards, frameworks and processes to ensure they are fit for purpose to effectively manage Markets business risk. Regulatory & Business Conduct •    Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct. •    Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct. •    Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters. Key Stakeholders •    Markets Business Heads •    Markets Group and Regional COO teams •    Markets Functional Heads •    Markets and Group CFCC •    Group OR •    Markets MT Other Responsibilities •    Live by the Here for Good promise and the brand values as stated for Group, Business and Country •    Perform other responsibilities assigned under Group, Country, Business or Functional policies and procedures. •    Responsible for developing a strong working relationship across all Markets risk and risk ops teams and locations. •    Responsible for leveraging the opportunity provided by Corporate Social Responsibility to enhance the Group’s internal and external reputation and indirectly influence the bottom line. •    Responsible for promoting the Group’s brand and Here for good with employees, clients and regulators. •    Responsible for performing other responsibilities assigned under Group, Country, Business or Functional policies and procedures. •    Responsible for maintaining effective communication with key stakeholders, and staff. Degree education in related discipline is preferred. •    Significant experience in managing Operational, Compliance, Cyber or FCC risks in first or second line of defence. •    Significant experience in presenting and writing papers to senior management, up to and include Board Level committees. •    Working experience in supporting the Markets and Funds and Securities Services businesses •    Ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment. •    Ability to articulate complex issues concisely and communicate effectively. •    Collaborative and creative problem solver, while being outcome focused. •    Strong interpersonal and stakeholder management skills •    Strong analytical skills with an ability to understand complex workflows and excellent attention to details. •    Comfortable working in a relatively unstructured and multi-cultural environment Role Specific Technical Competencies •    Markets Business and Products (FSS, Markets) •    Operational Risk Standards •    Governance and Supervision •    Coordination between business and functions •    Change Management •    Process Optimization •    People Management •    Data Analytics About Standard Chartered We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. title: R0415275 - Corporate Bank Tech – Business Analyst - AVP
description: Details of the Division and Team: The Core Banking Transformation Team is responsible for delivering innovative, scalable, and regulatory-compliant banking solutions within the core banking application. As a Business Analyst, you will operate at the intersection of business analysis, delivery leadership, and domain expertise, driving transformation and modernization initiatives within a complex, multi-stakeholder environment. You will be engaged in high-impact projects where clarity, structure, and disciplined execution are critical—especially in environments involving regulatory deadlines, cross-border dependencies, and legacy-to-target platform transitions. This role requires close collaboration with business stakeholders (from front to back, including any other backend or downstream platforms such as operations, finance, coverage, investments, compliance, RDM, EOS, etc) to ensure successful delivery and sustainable solutions. Apart from owning the end‑to‑end analysis lifecycle (from discovery and requirements through design, UAT, release readiness), you will be expected to bring a strong track record of leading large-scale transformation, payments modernization, and regulatory change programs, particularly within Avaloq-based environments, translating business strategy and regulatory requirements into clear, testable system and process changes on Avaloq across regions. What we will offer you: A healthy, engaged and well-supported workforce is better equipped to do their best work and, more importantly, enjoy their lives inside and outside the workplace. That’s why we are committed to providing an environment with your development and wellbeing at its center. You can expect: Flexible benefits plan including virtual doctor consultation services Comprehensive leave benefits Gender Neutral Parental Leave Hybrid working arrangement 25 days of annual paid leave, plus public holiday A culture of continuous learning to aid progression Your key responsibilities: Lead requirements discovery with business stakeholders, perform independent analysis, and requirements documentation for Avaloq core banking platform enhancements, including regulatory or strategic projects as and when required. Conduct structured gap analysis between business expectations, regulatory requirements, and system capabilities. Coordinate and facilitate solution-shaping workshops (including demo sessions) with impacted business stakeholders. Translate business and regulatory needs into clear, actionable functional specifications (BRD/FSD) with traceability and impact transparency. Partner with Avaloq parameterization/configuration/developer teams to shape fit‑for‑purpose designs across modules (e.g., Client Lifecycle, Payments, Securities, Lending/Deposits, Fees, Corporate Actions, Securities Trading). Identify opportunities to improve stability, usability, and efficiency of the core banking platform, and promote test automation and standardization of BA artefacts. Oversee end-to-end delivery, including solution design, execution, testing (SIT, UAT, regression), and go-live readiness (such as training and documentation). Define and drive test strategy and execution across SIT, UAT, regression, and business dress rehearsals. Lead defect triage and prioritization, ensuring focus on business-critical and regulatory-impacting issues. Collaborate with cross-functional teams (such as vendors, internal IT teams, business stakeholders) to deliver high-quality solutions on time, as well as within scope and budget. Manage interdependent workstreams, delivery risks, regulatory milestones, and stakeholder expectations across global teams. Provide pragmatic decision support to sponsors, balancing business priorities, regulatory constraints, and system limitations. Provide structured delivery reporting (in the form of presentation and reports) and decision-ready status updates to senior management / business stakeholders Actively manage go-live risk, stabilization planning, and post-implementation support readiness. Consistently deliver stable, production-ready solutions with minimal post-go-live disruption. Drive process improvements, automation opportunities, and operational excellence within delivery and BAU environments. Support BAU production environment, including defect resolution & analysis, within the controlled change implementation framework. Mentor and support junior team members in their professional development. Foster a collaborative and inclusive team environment. Your skills and experience: Avaloq Certified Professional (ACP) with a Bachelor’s degree in relevant disciplines (eg. Business, Information Technology, Finance, or Computer Science) and minimum 10 years’ experience as business analyst in project management on private banking, specializing in core banking transformation, payments modernization, and large-scale regulatory and platform change. Strong functional knowledge within Avaloq pertaining to private banking processes and controls (such as security & client static data, payments, securities trading, FX, structured products, lending/deposit, settlement, corporate action and fees) and cross border payments (such as SWIFT standards, ISO20022 compliance and MT/MX coexistence strategies) Proven track record in leading and coordinating large, multi-year transformation initiatives, including ISO20022, MT-to-MX migration, Avaloq cloud migration, and major platform upgrades (including SWIFT and Avaloq version release upgrades). Deep expertise in Avaloq (versions 4.1 through 5.7, including cloud deployments), with coding experience at param level on core banking modules, downstream & upstream interfaces, static data, and integration architecture. Experience in requirements gathering, process mapping & reengineering, solution delivery within complex, multi-stakeholder environments. Hybrid experience operating effectively between Business Analyst and Project Manager roles, ensuring both solution integrity and delivery discipline. Familiarity with automated regression testing practices Excellent stakeholder management, communication, and facilitation skills. Analytical mindset with strong problem-solving abilities. Minimum 10 years of working knowledge and experience across both CTB and RTB release cycles. Strong written and verbal communication skills in English. Ability to work independently and collaboratively as part of a team in a fast-paced environment with minimal training required. title: Talent & Learning Manager (Fixed Term Contract)
description: The Opportunity… At Moët Hennessy, We Don't Just Create Exceptional Experiences – We Live Them. For generations, our 27 Maisons, including Dom Pérignon, Veuve Clicquot, and Hennessy, have defined luxury. Now, it's your turn. Imagine a career fueled by passion and creativity, where you redefine what's possible. We seek individuals who thrive in collaborative environments, sharing our love for craftsmanship, elegance, and integrity. We celebrate diversity and the pursuit of excellence. Ready to drive learning and talent innovation at a global luxury brand? As Talent and Learning Manager for Moët Hennessy in Asia Pacific, your role will involve partnering with regional and global teams to design and implement strategic L&D initiatives. Your work will directly empower our people, build essential capabilities, and cultivate an engaging and inclusive culture that is critical to our ongoing success. If you're passionate about fostering continuous growth, enhancing critical skills, and nurturing an engaging work environment, this is your opportunity to contribute to our success. This is a 9-momth fixed-term contract. How you will contribute… Learning & Development Strategy and Execution Strategic L&D Leadership: Support the development and implementation of the regional Talent & Learning strategy, aligning with regional and global objectives and leverage existing tools and platforms to foster a culture of continuous learning and critical capability enhancement across Asia Pacific. Capability & Needs Assessment: Conduct thorough skills assessments and needs analyses to identify critical skill gaps and development opportunities, directly informing the design of targeted training solutions that support the regional SOAR strategy. Program Design & Delivery: Lead the end-to-end design, development, and facilitation of a diverse learning calendar, encompassing engaging workshops, digital learning, and experiential programs, ensuring high-quality content delivery. Stakeholder Collaboration & Curation: Act as a strong people partner, collaborating effectively with global and regional teams, and engaging with external vendors to curate and deliver high-quality, relevant learning content. Performance Measurement & Improvement: Establish and implement robust evaluation methods for all learning programs and initiatives, utilizing data and feedback to drive continuous improvement and demonstrate impact. Learning Culture & Engagement: Champion a dynamic, feedback-driven learning culture, actively promoting participative learning and growth initiatives throughout the organization. Talent Development & Management: Strategic Talent Advancement: Drive regional talent investment strategies and programs, focusing on upskilling, leadership advancement, and overall capability building to support the long-term talent agenda. Talent Pipeline & Mobility: Collaborate with HR teams to actively build a robust and diverse talent pipeline within the region, ensuring talent mobility and readiness for future organizational needs. High-Potential Development: Identify and nurture high-potential talent, providing targeted development opportunities to accelerate their growth and prepare them for future leadership roles. Other: Budget Management & Operational Excellence: Manage the L&D and Talent Development budget effectively, ensuring efficient allocation of resources, and overseeing administrative processes to support the smooth execution of all programs and initiatives. Cross-Functional Support & Ad-Hoc Initiatives: Provide responsive support for various HR and business initiatives, including contributing to ad-hoc projects, preparing reports, and handling other duties as assigned to advance organizational talent objectives We are looking for unique and amazing talent. Join us if you have… 8+ years of experience in Learning & Development, Talent Management, or a similar HR function, with a strong focus on training and program delivery. Proven experience in designing, implementing, and evaluating learning programs with experience using LMS platforms Strong understanding of adult learning principles and instructional design methodologies. Excellent communication, presentation, and facilitation skills. Ability to work effectively in a multicultural and fast-paced environment. Proactive, results-oriented, and a strong team player. Proficiency in English; additional regional languages are a plus When you join Moët Hennessy… You embark on a career journey with the leaders of luxury wines, champagnes, and spirits, within the prestigious LVMH Group. You enter a world of possibility where career development opportunities across our 5 global regions and within the entire LVMH Group are unmatched. You join a passionate workforce within our internationally diverse and inclusive teams, exploring the rich heritage of our Maisons and shaping the next chapter of our story You discover our essential relationship with our terroirs and the soils that define our products. Through our Living Soils Living Together program, we are committed to regenerating soils, reducing climate impact, and empowering communities for a sustainable future. You will share in our passion for hosting, the art of living, and creating products that celebrate life’s most memorable milestones, always experienced through responsible tasting. Ready for a world of possibilities? Discover Moët Hennessy today!

title: Head, GMO Business Risk Management
description: Job Summary The Head of Global Markets Operations Business Risk Management is a senior leadership role responsible for the design, execution, and oversight of the operational risk, control, and governance framework across Global Markets Operations. The role ensures that operational risks are identified, assessed, mitigated, and managed in line with the firm’s risk appetite, regulatory expectations, and business strategy. This role acts as a line-of-defense–style partner embedded within Operations, providing independent challenge, oversight, and assurance while enabling safe, scalable, and compliant operations across all Global Markets products and regions. What Success Looks Like •    Strong, transparent operational risk and control environment across Global Markets Operations. •    Reduced severity and recurrence of operational incidents. •    Positive regulatory and audit outcomes. •    Effective partnership with Operations leadership enabling safe growth. •    High-performing, engaged global risk teams. RESPONSIBILITIES Business •    Own the Global Markets Operations Business Risk Management framework, aligned to enterprise operational risk standards. •    Identify, assess, and monitor operational risks across trading, post-trade, clearing, collateral, client, and regulatory operations. •    Ensure consistent execution of risk and control self-assessments (RCSAs), scenario analysis, and key risk indicators (KRIs). •    Provide independent challenge to Operations leadership on risk exposures, control effectiveness, and remediation plans. •    Act as a key Operations risk partner for regulatory exams, internal audits, and external reviews. •    Coordinate responses to regulatory findings and audit issues, ensuring sustainable remediation. •    Monitor regulatory developments impacting Global Markets Operations and assess operational risk implications. Processes •    Lead operational incident management, root cause analysis, and lessons-learned exercises. •    Oversee issue tracking, remediation, and closure in partnership with Operations and Technology. •    Provide risk oversight and challenge for operational change initiatives, including new products, new markets, and system changes. People & Talent •    Lead, develop, and retain a global regulatory operations organization. •    Build a strong leadership bench and succession pipeline. •    Foster a culture of accountability, control ownership, and regulatory excellence. •    Champion diversity, equity, and inclusion. Risk Management •    Oversee the design and effectiveness of preventative and detective controls across Global Markets Operations. •    Ensure timely identification, escalation, and remediation of control gaps, incidents, and near misses. •    Maintain strong governance, issue management, and management information frameworks. •    Support attestation processes and senior management certifications. •    Develop and maintain risk metrics, dashboards, and management information to support effective decision-making. •    Leverage data analytics to identify emerging risks, trends, and control weaknesses. •    Enhance transparency and consistency of risk reporting across regions and functions. Regulatory & Business Conduct •    Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct. •    Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct. •    Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters. Key Stakeholders •    Lead and develop a global Operations Business Risk Management team. •    Build strong risk talent pipelines and succession plans. •    Foster a culture of risk awareness, accountability, and continuous improvement. •    Champion diversity, equity, and inclusion. Our Ideal Candidate •	Education - Bachelor’s degree in finance, business, economics, or a related discipline required. •	Advanced degree (MBA or equivalent) preferred. •	15+ years of relevant experience in Risk Management Role Specific Technical Competencies •    Strong financial acumen and analytical capability. •    Expertise in performance measurement, management information, and data analytics. •    Ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights. •    Experience supporting large-scale operational transformation •    Demonstrated ability to engage effectively with senior management and regulators. •    Strong judgment and ability to provide constructive, credible challenge. About Standard Chartered We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can't wait to see the talents you can bring us. title: AVP, Investment Specialist
description: Some careers have more impact than others. If you’re looking for a career where you can make a real impression, join HSBC and discover how valued you’ll be. Whether you want a career that could take you to the top, or simply take you in an exciting new direction, HSBC offers opportunities, support and rewards that will take you further. International Wealth and Premier Banking (IWPB) helps deliver on HSBC’s purpose of opening up a world of opportunity by providing our customers with borderless banking and world-class wealth management through best-in-class, mobile-first capabilities, and exceptional people. Our international network and breadth of expertise enable us to support individuals, families, business owners, investors, and entrepreneurs.  International Wealth and Premier Banking provides a leading premium proposition through Premier Banking and, together with our Private Bank, are present across the world’s most important markets, booking centres, and corridors most valued by our clients. Our wealth offering is further enhanced through our best-in-class manufacturing capabilities in Asset Management and Insurance. We are currently seeking a high calibre professional to join our team as an Assistant Vice President, Investment Specialist. In this role you will: Client Advisory & Relationship Management: Advise Premier clients on investment needs, goals, and risk tolerance; build trusted relationships, collaborating with Relationship Managers Strategic Portfolio Management: Design and optimize Premier client portfolios, focusing on strategic asset allocation using structured products, mutual funds, and bonds Market & Investment Research: Translating Chief Investment Officer (CIO) view into actionable investment insights for client portfolios Product Expertise & Due Diligence: Evaluate and recommend suitable investment solutions, specializing in structured products, mutual funds, and bonds, based on rigorous due diligence Wealth Lending Advisory: Advise clients on wealth lending solutions (e.g., Lombard lending) to complement their investment strategies. Relationship Manager Coaching & Knowledge Transfer: Coach Relationship Managers to enhance their investment acumen and ability to articulate solutions to clients Portfolio Monitoring & Reviews: Continuously monitor client portfolio performance, conduct proactive reviews, and recommend adjustments for clients Compliance & Risk Management: Ensure strict adherence to regulatory requirements (Monetary Authority of Singapore) and internal policies, managing risks and ensuring fair client dealings To be successful you will need: Bachelor's degree or higher in Finance, Economics, Business Administration, or a related quantitative field from a reputable university CMFAS (Capital Markets and Financial Advisory Services Examination) Modules: Essential for licensing in Singapore (e.g RES1B, RES5, CM-EIP, CM-SIP, M8, M8A) CFA (Chartered Financial Analyst) or A Master's degree is preferred. Other relevant certifications like CFP (Certified Financial Planner) or CAIA (Chartered Alternative Investment Analyst) may also be beneficial Advanced relevant experience in an investment advisory, portfolio management, or product specialist role within a private bank, wealth management firm, or asset management company Proven track record of advising and managing portfolios for High-Net-Worth (HNW) clients in Singapore or the Asia-Pacific region Strong background and demonstrable expertise in structured products, mutual funds (unit trusts), and fixed income (bonds). Experience with other asset classes and wealth lending solutions is a significant advantage The chosen candidate for this role will be required to undergo enhanced vetting. Subject to local laws, this will require the individual to satisfactorily pass a series of additional checks as part of the recruitment process and on an ongoing basis, if appointed to the role. HSBC Group reserves its position with regards to any steps which it may take in relation to any material adverse findings which arise either when the checks are originally completed, and/or if relevant, on an ongoing basis.
